Summary: Generated code fails to compile because generated code refers TID[0] which doesn't exisit in rtModel.
Description: If customer target specifies 'SuppressScheduler' via -a option or in target tlc file and use model reference block. Generated code fails to compile because generated code refers TID[0] which doesn't exisit in rtModel.
Workaround: Use the attached tlc file to replace the same file in R2007b

Summary: multivarichart can produce plot with missing data when grouping variables are not sorted
Description: Under some conditions, the multivarichart  function may produce a plot with some data missing. This happens if the grouping variables do not appear in sorted order. For example, the plot does not have a problem when the following matrix is the grouping variable:      1     1     1     1     2     1     1     1     1     2     1     1     2     2     1     1     1     1     2     1     2     1     2     1     1     2     2     1     2     2     2     1     1     1     1     2     2     1     1     2     1     2     1     2     2     2     1     2     1     1     2     2     2     1     2     2     1     2     2     2     2     2     2     2 However, random permutations of the rows of this matrix can lead to an error or a plot with missing content.
Workaround: Sort the data before making the plot. For example, suppose G is a grouping variable with 4 columns and Y is the response variable. The following commands will convert G and Y into the form shown above:[G,idx] = sortrows(G, 4:-1:1);Y = Y(idx);

Summary: Ylabels and other rotated text draw backwards on MAC Snow Leopard
Description: Creating a ylabel  on MAC OS 10.6, Snow Leopard will draw the text backwards: ylabel('ylabel');

Summary: MATLAB program error causes process abort in R2008a (ONLY) on Snow Leopard
Description: Executing any code that causes a MATLAB error to be thrown can cause R2008a to abort, e.g. >> A = [1 2 3]; >> A(4) This bug only affects Macs with Snow Leopard installed.
Workaround: In R2008a on OS X 10.6, the native JIT must be disabled for MATLAB to work. This is accomplished by starting up MATLAB with the -nojit option or using "feature jit off" at the command prompt.

Summary: After using Simulink.BlockDiagram.buildRapidAcceleratorTarget(model), parameter updates are not used during subsequent Rapid Accelerator simulations
Description: After using Simulink.BlockDiagram.buildRapidAcceleratorTarget(model), the updated run-time parameters of the model are not used during subsequent calls to the Rapid Accelerator simulations.

Summary: A zoom box initially displays in an incorrect location for some axes inside a GUI.
Description: If you create a GUI that includes the figure toolbar and an axes, and then use the zoom tool to drag out a zoom box, the dashed zoom box can appear in the wrong place. The displacement only occurs on the first zoom operation. Subsequent zoom operations behave as expected.
Workaround: You can work around this issue by turning off the Brushing and Linking Message Bar, located beneath the menu bar. Remove it by unchecking the "Show Brushing and Linking Message Bar" in the General -> Confirmation Dialogues preferences panel. Alternatively, click on the X in the Brushing and Linking Message Bar at the top of any figure. Removing the message bar unsets the preference as well.

Summary: Calling uisetcolor in a uicontrol (uitable, text box, buttons etc.) causes a standalone application to hang.
Description: Create a GUI application with one or more uicontrol s. Call uisetcolor  in the callback for any one of the uicontrol s. Compile the code into a standalone application. Execute the standalone application. Activate the uicontrol  with the uisetcolor  callback to activate the callback. Click OK  or Cancel .  The application hangs.
Workaround: There is no known workaround for this issue.

Summary: Compilation failures might occur when building a target application from a model that contains a referenced model and has inlined parameters enabled
Description: Compilation failures might occur when building a target application from a model that contains a referenced model and has inlined parameters enabled. A message like the following indicates this issue: matlabroot \toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\src\xpctarget.c(120) : error C2065: '<Parameters_ELGEAR>' : undeclared identifier NMAKE : fatal error U1077: '"c:\program files\microsoft visual studio 8\VC\BIN\cl.EXE"' : return code '0x2' Stop. ?
Workaround: As a workaround:Stop MATLAB.Download the attached file, xpcoptions.tlc, and save it to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\tlc\xpcoptions.tlcRestart MATLAB.Rebuild your target application.

Summary: After installing a mix of R2008a and R2008a+ products on a Unix platform MATLAB can SegV when starting Simulink.
Description: Installing the R2008a products Signal Processing Toolbox 6.9 or Image Processing Toolbox 6.1 after installing the R2008a+ product Simulink 7.1.1, disables the Simulink Library Browser. This could occur if the R2008a version of these products is downloaded an installed at the same time as the R2008a+ version of Simulink.Clicking the Simulink icon or typing simulink at the MATLAB prompt causes MATLAB to throw a segmentation violation.The problem can occur when you download and install the current versions of all licensed products from the MathWorks Web site.
Workaround: Avoid the problem by installing all licensed R2008a products before installing R2008a+ products.

Summary: Multiple-step test cases with parameter values produce errors during simulation.
Description: If Simulink Design Verifier is configured to generate values for parameters and a generated test case has more than one time step, the parameter value for that test case will be invalid and the harness model might generate an error during simulation.
Workaround: Please perform the following steps to fix this bug:Move matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\private\parameters.m to a different folder, one that is outside the MATLAB path.Copy parameters.m to matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv\private\Restart MATLAB.?

Summary: After installing a mix of R2008a and R2008a+ products on a Windows platform, Simulink library browser doesn't open.
Description: Installing the R2008a products Signal Processing Toolbox 6.9, Image Processing Toolbox 6.1, or Embedded IDE Link MU 1.0.1 after installing the R2008a+ product Simulink 7.1.1, disables the Simulink Library Browser. This could occur if the R2008a version of these products is downloaded an installed at the same time as the R2008a+ version of Simulink.Clicking the Simulink icon or typing simulink at the MATLAB prompt displays the following error, Error: Simulink library browser failed to initialize  in the MATLAB Command Window.The problem can occur when you download and install the current versions of all licensed R2008a products from the MathWorks Web site.
Workaround: Avoid the problem by installing all licensed R2008a products before installing R2008a+ products.

Summary: Accelerator and Rapid Accelerator modes throw an error when Real-Time Workshop is not installed.
Description: Some Video and Image Processing Blockset blocks throw the following error message in Accelerator and Rapid Accelerator modes when Real-Time Workshop is not installed. "Problem creating Accelerator MEX file for model 'modelName'. Error returned is: Error using ==> accelbuild_private at 26Error using ==> getPathNameThe path '(matlabroot)\toolbox\rtw\dspblks\c' does not exist ..."
Workaround: Follow these steps to replace the rtwmakecfg.m file:1. Quit MATLAB?.2. The attached file attachment_501343_20081029.zip includes rtwmakecfg.m. Unzip the attached file and replace the rtwmakecfg.m file in the following location with the new one:MATLABROOT/toolbox/vipblks/vipblks/vipmex, where MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine.3. Restart MATLAB.4. Issue the following command:rehash toolbox

Summary: Setting uitable Position width and height to match its extent creates scroll bars when it should not
Description: When displaying a uitable on a Windows system that uses the Windows "Classic" theme, setting the width and height of its Position  property equal to the width and height of its Extent  property causes a scroll bar to appear on the uitable. Using Extent  to set Position  normally makes the table just fit into its position rectangle, but the Classic theme causes this computation to be done incorrectly. Under Windows "Classic" theme, you will see scroll bar in the following code: t = uitable('data', rand(4));ext = get(t, 'extent'); set(t, 'position', [20 20 ext(3) ext(4)]);
Workaround: To eliminate the unnecessary scrollbar, add 2 pixels to the width and height of the uitable's Position property when you set them to equal the uitable's Extent. For example:t = uitable('data', rand(4));ext = get(t, 'extent'); set(t, 'position', [20 20 ext(3)+2 ext(4)+2]);

Summary: Handle to a uitable is returned before its 'Extent' property is updated
Description: Give a uitable created programmatically a 2-by-2 array of data (the Data  property). If its Extent  property is retrieved, it is correct. However, if the data is immediately changed, for example to a 100-by-100 array, the Extent  Property is not immediately updated to reflect the new data. Then, if the next line of code attempts to retrieve the Extent , the previous value is obtained, incorrectly. For example: u=uitable('Data',ones(2,2)) ;get(u,'Extent')ans =     0     0   152    34set(u,'Data',ones(100,100)) ;get(u,'Extent')ans =     0     0   152    34
Workaround: Add a call to drawnow before getting the extent:u=uitable('Data',ones(2,2)) ;get(u,'Extent')ans =0 0 182 52set(u,'Data',ones(100,100)) ; drawnow();get(u,'Extent')ans =0 0 7542 1620?

Summary: Wrong property event names listed in documentation
Description: The documentation for property events lists the event names as PropertyPreSet  and PropertyPostSet . The correct event names are PretSet  and PoseSet .
Workaround: Use the following property event names:PreSet, PostSet, PreGet, and PostGet

Summary: Using the Open Watcom 1.3 compiler to build an xPC Target model that contains a Measurement Computing PCI-DAC6703 DA block crashes the target computer
Description: Using the Open Watcom 1.3 compiler to build an xPC Target model that contains a Measurement Computing PCI-DAC6703 DA block crashes the target computer. Running this target application will cause an incorrect hardware access that halts the target computer.
Workaround: As a workaround, perform one of the following:Upgrade to R2008b (xPC Target Version 4.0) and use the Open Watcom 1.7 compiler instead of Open Watcom 1.3. Note, previous versions of xPC Target do not support the Open Watcom 1.7 compiler.If you want to continue to use Open Watcom 1.3 and a version of xPC Target from R2008a or earlier: 1. Stop MATLAB.2. Rename matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\dacbpcidac670x.c tomatlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\dacbpcidac670x_bak.c.3. Download the file dacbpcidac670x.c and save it to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks.4. Restart MATLAB.5. Rebuild your model.

Summary: Struct assignment fails when using dynamic field names and END
Description: This bug appears only with more complex uses of dynamic field names and the END keyword, such as in the example shown below:    a.name = 'value:4';   typemeth= struct();   typemeth.(a(1).name(1:end - 2)) = a(1).name(end) This results in:    typemeth =       value: {} This is clearly not correct, as typemeth  is expected to be a struct.
Workaround: One way to avoid this problem is to calculate the value of END in a separate statement. You can then use this value in all cases where your code uses that same value of END.Here is the example shown above, but with the workaround added:   e = length(a(1).name);   typemeth.(a(1).name(1:e - 2)) = a(1).name(e);When run, it returns the expected answer:   typemeth =        value: '4'

Summary: Given a MATLAB class derived from dynamicprops, using a saveobj method to save a meta.DynamicProperty instance to a MAT-file without saving the object to which the dynamic property has been attached can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you use the addprop  method to add dynamic properties to a MATLAB class derived from dynamicprops , those dynamic properties are saved along with the object to which they are attached when you save the object to a MAT-file. However, if the class implements a saveobj  method that converts the object into another type of MATLAB variable, such as a struct , you should not save the meta-classes for the dynamic properties (i.e., the meta.DynamicProperty  instances) in the struct . If you save the meta.DynamicProperty  instances without the class instance to which they are attached, MATLAB can crash when you load the MAT-file if the corresponding loadobj  method references the meta.DynamicProperty  instances.
Workaround: When converting an instance of a MATLAB class to another type of variable (e.g., a struct) in a saveobj method, save the names and values of dynamic properties as fields of the struct. In addition, rather than saving the meta.DynamicProperty instance itself, if any attributes of the dynamic property must be preserved, save them in the struct by obtaining the values from the property's meta.DynamicProperty. Then, in the corresponding loadobj method, call addprop to add a new dynamic property, and set the properties of the new meta.DynamicProperty instance using the values that you saved in the struct.

Summary: Using upper case builtin name may cause MATLAB to crash.
Description: Using upper case builtin name may cause MATLAB to crash, e.g.x = SQRT(y);
Workaround: Use lower case function names for builtins.

Summary: Simulink crashes when simulating or generating code for a model containing a Stateflow chart that accesses a data store
Description: When a Simulink model contains a Stateflow chart that accesses a data store, Simulink may crash when simulating or generating code for that model. The stack trace refers to the function sluGetSignalSrc.
Workaround: Rename all Merge blocks in the model so that their names are later in alphabetical order than the names of other blocks in the model. For example, prefix all Merge block names with "zzz_".

Summary: ToVCD block causes error during model build process.
Description: The ToVCD block causes the following error during a model build process: Cannot modify parameter 4 of S-function 'model/To VCD File' while simulation is running.
Workaround: The attached files provide patches for the R2007b release. Patches for other releases will be made available upon request.To install the patch, copy the attachments to your MATLAB installation.move the existing files shdltovcd.mex_ext and shdltovcd.mex_ext.csf to .oldsave the attachment to matlabroot/toolbox/shared/hdllinkrename the file to remove the release number from the file nameexit and restart MATLABThe valid mex file extensions, mex_ext, include:mexw32 for 32bit Windowsmexglx for 32bit Linuxmexa64 for 64bit Linuxmexs64 for 64bit SolarisFor example, to patch the R2007b release for Windows:cd matlabroot/toolbox/shared/hdllinkmv shdltovcd.mexw32 shdltovcd.mexw32.oldmv shdltovcd.mexw32.csf shdltovcd.mexw32.csf.oldsave shdltovcd.mexw32.R2007b and shdltvocd.mexw32.csf.R2007b attachments to matlabroot/toolbox/shared/hdllinkmv shdltovcd.mexw32.R2007b shdltovcd.mexw32mv shdltovcd.mexw32.csf.R2007b shdltovcd.mexw32.csfexit and restart MATLABOnce the patch is installed, no errors should be generated by the ToVCD block during code generation or compilation.

Summary: ToVCD block causes error during model build process.
Description: The ToVCD block causes the following error during a model build process: Cannot modify parameter 4 of S-function 'model/To VCD File' while simulation is running.
Workaround: The attached files provide patches for the R2007b release. Patches for other releases will be made available upon request.To install the patch, copy the attachments to your MATLAB installation.move the existing files shdltovcd.mex_ext and shdltovcd.mex_ext.csf to .oldsave the attachment to matlabroot/toolbox/shared/hdllinkrename the file to remove the release number from the file nameexit and restart MATLABThe valid mex file extensions, mex_ext, include:mexw32 for 32bit Windowsmexglx for 32bit Linuxmexa64 for 64bit Linuxmexs64 for 64bit SolarisFor example, to patch the R2007b release for Windows:cd matlabroot/toolbox/shared/hdllinkmv shdltovcd.mexw32 shdltovcd.mexw32.oldmv shdltovcd.mexw32.csf shdltovcd.mexw32.csf.oldsave shdltovcd.mexw32.R2007b and shdltvocd.mexw32.csf.R2007b attachments to matlabroot/toolbox/shared/hdllinkmv shdltovcd.mexw32.R2007b shdltovcd.mexw32mv shdltovcd.mexw32.csf.R2007b shdltovcd.mexw32.csfexit and restart MATLABOnce the patch is installed, no errors should be generated by the ToVCD block during code generation or compilation.

Summary: launchDiscovery.m assumes use of a 'csh'-compatible shell.
Description: When running the EDA Simulator Link DS product demos, the following error will occur if a 'csh'-compatible shell was not used to start MATLAB: Error executing callback 'ClickFcn' Error using ==> lutils>GetAvailablePort at 74 /bin/sh: setenv: command not found  This error can also happen if you are directly invoking launchDiscovery  with the 'HostComm' parameter set to 'AutoGenSocketPort'. If you do not specify a value, the default is 'AutoGenSocketPort'.
Workaround: To avoid this error, you can start MATLAB in a 'csh'-compatible shell. For example, from a /bin/sh shell:sh> csh -c "setenv MATLAB_SHELL /bin/csh ; matlab"

Summary: Scripts used to support the product demos assume use of a 'csh'-compatible shell.
Description: When running the EDA Simulator Link IN product demos, the following error will occur if a 'csh'-compatible shell was not used to start MATLAB: Error evaluating 'OpenFcn' callback of {block name}.Error using ==> HdlServer>getAvailablePort at 1091/bin/sh: setenv: command not found    This error also can happen if you are directly utilizing the beta feature front-end script 'HdlServer.m' and setting the parameter 'socketNumber' to 'AUTO_GEN'.
Workaround: To avoid this error, you can start MATLAB in a 'csh'-compatible shell. For example from a /bin/sh shell:sh> csh -c "setenv MATLAB_SHELL /bin/csh ; matlab"

Summary: Simulink signals disconnect from EDA Simulator Link HDL cosimulation block on cosimulation block error.
Description: If an HDL cosimulation block is not at the top level of the Simulink model and there is an error thrown by the cosimulation block during an update diagram or Simulink simulation, the block's signals will be disconnected.
Workaround: You can avoid signal disconnects by doing any one of the following before executing an update diagram or simulation:save the .mdl with the containing subsystem open.execute "Edit / Refresh Model Blocks" (or Ctrl-K)ensure that the HDL simulator is up and connected and that there are no cosimulation block errors

Summary: Simulink signals disconnect from EDA Simulator Link HDL cosimulation block on cosimulation block error.
Description: If an HDL cosimulation block is not at the top level of the Simulink model and there is an error thrown by the cosimulation block during an update diagram or Simulink simulation, the block's signals will be disconnected.
Workaround: You can avoid signal disconnects by doing any one of the following before executing an update diagram or simulation:save the .mdl with the containing subsystem open.execute "Edit / Refresh Model Blocks" (or Ctrl-K)ensure that the HDL simulator is up and connected and that there are no cosimulation block errors

Description: If an HDL cosimulation block is not at the top level of the Simulink model and there is an error thrown by the cosimulation block during an update diagram or Simulink simulation, the block's signals will be disconnected.
Workaround: You can avoid signal disconnects by doing any one of the following before executing an update diagram or simulation:save the .mdl with the containing subsystem open.execute "Edit / Refresh Model Blocks" (or Ctrl-K)ensure that the HDL simulator is up and connected and that there are no cosimulation block errors

Summary: MATLAB crashes when a function within a package attempts to change the current working directory.
Description: A bug in MATLAB R2008a can cause MATLAB to crash if a package function attempts to cd  to a different location. For example if a package function /some/where/+pkg/fun.m  contains code like the following: function fun(dir)      cd(dir) Executing the following call to that function would crash MATLAB: >> pkg.fun(pwd)
Workaround: Avoid using cd in package functions.

Summary: MATLAB might crash during code generation for models with sum or product blocks in inlined atomic subsystems
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might crash MATLAB during code generation if any of the following blocks are used in an inlined atomic subsystem: Sum blocks with all operations specified as minuses Product blocks with all operations specified as divisions Sum blocks with more than two inputs using fixed-point data types and unequal biases Product blocks with more than two inputs using fixed-point data types and unequal biases

Summary: Error executing MEX functions created with emlmex after running clear all or clear mex
Description: When you try to execute a MEX function created with the emlmex  command after typing clear mex  or clear all  at the MATLAB? command line, MATLAB errors out. To reproduce this behavior, perform the following steps: Compile the MATLAB file into a MEX function using emlmex:  emlmex myfunction ¨Co myfunction_mex ¨Ceg {0}   Verify that the MEX function executes without error:  myfunction_mex(0)   Run a clear all  or clear mex  command:  clear all;   Execute the MEX function again:  myfunction_mex(0)   This time, MATLAB throws the following error: ??? Invalid MEX-file ˇ®C:\work\myfunction_mex.mexw32ˇŻ:
Workaround: To workaround this issue in R2008a, execute the following command before running a clear all or a clear mex command:embedded.numerictype;

Summary: Incorrect results for Abs block
Description: You might get incorrect results for test generation objectives related to an Abs block when you generate tests. In some cases, Design Verifier incorrectly reports that the input to Abs blocks can never be less than zero.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Change log:July 26, 2011: attachment_487719_08a_2011-07-26.zip: initial version, works on R2008a only

Summary: MATLAB returns an error when initializing a class property to an anonymous function with zero or multiple inputs.
Description: In the properties  section of a class definition, if an initializer of a property is an anonymous function with either zero inputs or two or more inputs, MATLAB will throw a syntax error whenever you attempt to create an object of the class. The text of the error message, which refers to a stored string that is invisible to the user, is inappropriate and confusing .
Workaround: As a workaround, initialize the anonymous function in the constructor of the class. For example,   classdef sampleclass      properties         f      end      methods         function obj = sampleclass          obj.f = @(x,y)x+y;         end      end   end

Summary: Referenced models containing blocks that detect zero crossings and are driven by discrete signals can cause errors
Description: Referenced models containing blocks that detect zero crossings and are driven by discrete signals can cause simulation and code generation errors. A block that can detect zero crossings, such as an Abs block, can contribute to these errors if the block's Enable zero-crossing detection  parameter is selected (ZeroCross  is set to on ). Simulation Errors:  When a referenced model simulates in any accelerated mode, incorrect simulation results or a segmentation violation can occur if the referenced model contains either of the following: A block that detects zero crossings and is driven by a discrete signal and a continuous signal. Two or more blocks that detect zero crossings, with one block driven by a discrete signal and another by a continuous signal.  Code Generation Errors:  When a referenced model that simulates in Normal mode contains one or more blocks that detect zero crossings, and every such block is driven only by discrete signals, code generated by Real-Time Workshop for the parent model might not compile.

Summary: Logging the output of a mixed-rate Model block or Subsystem block can give wrong time values
Description: When Simulink logs the output of a Model or Subsystem block with a hybrid sample time, and you enabled logging by right-clicking the signal and selecting Signal Properties > Log signal data , the values saved in the Time  field of the Simulink.Timeseries  object can be wrong, as follows: If a Model block references a model with multiple sample times, and one of the sample times is not represented by an output signal, the logged Time  values will be incorrect. If a Subsystem block uses multiple sample times, the block output is a non-virtual bus, and the output port sample time differs from the subsystem block sample time, the logged Time  values will be incorrect.

Summary: Calling clear all when the workspace contains MATLAB objects can slow down subsequent calls to class constructors.
Description: Use clear classes  to clear the workspace when the workspace contains MATLAB objects. For example, in a call sequence like the following: >> obj = MyClass >> clear all>> obj = MyClass the call to clear all  can cause a slow down in the second call to MyClass , where MyClass  is a MATLAB class.
Workaround: To avoid this MATLAB bug, use clear classes instead of clear all when the workspace contains MATLAB objects.

Summary: Why does MATLAB crash when trying to connect to a model to the target PC via Simulink external mode?
Description: The Simulink external mode interface has a problem preventing it from connecting to a target PC if a model contains more than 10 blocks that support signal uploading. Such a connection might cause a segmentation violation. ?
Workaround: As a workaround, you can limit the number of blocks that upload signals to 10.

Summary: BERTool throws an error message in Monte-Carlo mode if curve fitting to the simulation BER points fails
Description: BERTool can run Monte-Carlo simulations. In this mode, BERTool collects the simulation BER data at given SNR points. After all the SNR points are simulated, BERTool tries to fit a curve to these points. If a fit cannot be found, BERTool is supposed to display a warning. Instead of displaying a warning, BERTool throws the following error: ??? Error using ==> warningThe input to WARNING must have fields named 'state' and 'identifier'. Error in ==> bertool at 586 warning(warn);
Workaround: Follow these steps to replace the MATLAB file bertool.m.1. Quit MATLAB?.2. Rename the bertool.m file in your current MATLAB installation to another name, such as bertool.m.old. This file is in MATLABROOT\toolbox\comm\comm, where MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by using the matlabroot function at the MATLAB command prompt.3. Download the attached zip file, containing bertool.m, to the directory in step 2.4. Restart MATLAB.5. Issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Bus Selector block with output signal to certain blocks
Description: The Real Time-Workshop and Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder build process generate incorrect code when the output signal of a Bus Selector block connects to any of the following blocks: Vector Concatenation block Matrix Concatenation block, which is concatenating the last dimension of its input signals Merge block  ?
Workaround: Designate the output signal of the Bus Selector block as a test point.

Summary: Estimating low-order transfer functions (process models of IDPROC class) where some parameter values are fixed returns an error. This error occurs only in R2008a (Version 7.2).
Description: If you estimate a low-order transfer function (process model) using the System Identification Tool GUI or the PEM command with some of the parameters fixed at specific values, you will receive the following error: "Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values." This is a bug that has been fixed in release R2008b. ?
Workaround: To download and apply the patch:1. Exit MATLAB software.2. Download the attached files pem.m and obj2var.m. Replace these two files at the following locations:<matlabroot>/toolbox/ident/ident/@idpoly/pem.m<matlabroot>/toolbox/ident/ident/@idgrey/obj2var.mwhere <matlabroot> is your MATLAB root directory. If you do not know your MATLAB root directly, type matlabroot at the command prompt.3. Start MATLAB.4. Type the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcache?

Summary: Crash or incorrect answer accessing property from subclass instance
Description: MATLAB can crash or return an incorrect answer when accessing a property defined in a parent class from an instance of a subclass. This occurs only if there is a parallel hierarchy of classes that contain the same property, and only if at least one of the classes is a handle. For example,classdef container < handle  properties    obj;  endendclassdef base1  properties    prop = 99;  endendclassdef base2 < handle  properties    prop = 42;  endendclassdef sub2 < base2endfunction bug  c = container;  c.obj = base1;  objs{1} = c;  c = container;  c.obj = sub2;  objs{2} = c;  for i=1:length(objs)    o=objs{i};    disp(o)    v(i) = o.obj.prop;   % this can sometimes crash or give a wrong answer  end
Workaround: Enter the following command at the MATLAB command prompt to disable use of the JIT on MCOS code:   feature jitallow mcos off.

Summary: Build process fails when generating code for ERT-based S-function
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process fails when generating code to implement an ERT S-function interface to a model or subsystem when the model or subsystem references nonscalar parameters with a ImportedExternPointer  storage class. The compilation of the source file implementing the model's S-function wrapper fails, and an error similar to the following is reported in the MATLAB Command Window: matlabroot \BIN\MEX.PL: Error: Compile of 'subsystem_name _sf.c' failed. NMAKE : fatal error U1077: 'matlabroot \bin\mex' : return code '0x9' Stop. working_directory \ subsystem_name _ert_rtw>echo The make command returned an error of 2 The make command returned an error of 2  working_directory \ subsystem_name _ert_rtw>An_error_occurred_during_the_call_to_make 'An_error_occurred_during_the_call_to_make' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file. ### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: ' subsystem_name ' aborted due to an error.??? Subsystem build failed

Summary: MAKEMID does not generate code to represent interfaces in collection items
Description: If your IVI drivers have interfaces in collection items, MAKEMID will ignore them and will not generate the groups, functions or properties required to access the instrument functionality. This results in an incomplete MATLAB instrument driver (MDD) for some IVI-COM drivers. This issue was noted in the AgilentU254x IVI-COM driver. There may be other drivers with this problem. The interfaces "Measurement" and "Totalizer" under the "Counter" collection item are missing in the MATLAB Intrument Driver (MDD file) for the AgilentU254x device.

Summary: Calling deleted method crashes MATLAB
Description: MATLAB crashes when calling a method of a class after removing this method from the class definition. This can occur if the classdef file is edited and methods are removed. The following stack trace is indicative of this bug:   [0] libmwm_dispatcher.so:mdFcnName(0x17f00000000, 0x200000000, 0x4084b040, 2) + 33 bytes  [1] libmwm_interpreter.so:inBadFunctionArgumentTypesMsg(int, int, mpsType const*)(0, 0, 0x2ffffffff, 0x2b3900000002) + 34 bytes  [2] libmwm_interpreter.so:tfAnalyzer::MathOp(int*, int, int, int, int, mpsType*, tfFlowInfo*, int, mpsType const*, tfFlowInfo const*, int const*, int const*, bool)(0x4084b208, 0x4084b320, 0x100000002, 0x008da820) + 1231 bytes
Workaround: You can work around this problem by disabling the JIT using the following command:    feature accel off

Summary: In SISOTOOL, optimization terminates incorrectly for single-input/multiple-output systems that have time-domain requirements on multiple outputs
Description: When optimizing a system in SISOTOOL, the optimization terminates incorrectly with no solution under the following conditions: The system is single-input/multiple-output, and Design requirements on multiple outputs are time-domain requirements
Workaround: Replace the file <matlabroot>\toolbox\sloptim\sloptim\@sroengine\@TimeSpec\getCurrentResponseGradient.m with the attached file.

Summary: M-Lint autofix can break code involving max and find
Description: The following conditions can cause an M-Lint autofix to be incorrect: * A statement assigns to a variable the results of a find  call with one argument. * That variable is used as the only argument to max . * There are no other uses of that variable. In this situation, M-Lint produces a message that suggests that two more arguments, 1  and last , be added to the find  call for improved efficiency and offers an autofix in the Editor. However, it the you accept the autofix, the resulting code is incorrect.
Workaround: The incorrectly updated code typically causes M-Lint to produce a red warning message. In this case, undo the fix, and then update the code manually.

Summary: MATLAB can crash when program execution encounters a breakpoint in the constructor method of a class that inherits from a built-in class.
Description: If you set a breakpoint in the constructor method of a class that inherits from a MATLAB built-in class, MATLAB can crash when program excecution reaches the breakpoint. This crash occurs when you set the breakpoint at or before the line where the superclass constructor is called, like in the following example:  classdef myDouble < double  methods   function obj = myDouble(data)    obj = obj@double(data); % setting the breakpoint at or before this line causes crash.   end  end end  Given the class definition above, the following steps can cause MATLAB to crash: >> dbstop 4 myDouble>> myDouble(1)>> obj  ?
Workaround: To workaround this problem, you can set the breakpoint at a location after the call to the superclass constructor.

Summary: ToVCD block causes error during model build process.
Description: The ToVCD block causes the following error during a model build process: Cannot modify parameter 4 of S-function 'model/To VCD File' while simulation is running.
Workaround: The attached files provide patches for the R2007b release. Patches for other releases will be made available upon request.To install the patch, copy the attachments to your MATLAB installation.move the existing files shdltovcd.mex_ext and shdltovcd.mex_ext.csf to .oldsave the attachment to matlabroot/toolbox/shared/hdllinkrename the file to remove the release number from the file nameexit and restart MATLABThe valid mex file extensions, mex_ext, include:mexw32 for 32bit Windowsmexglx for 32bit Linuxmexa64 for 64bit Linuxmexs64 for 64bit SolarisFor example, to patch the R2007b release for Windows:cd matlabroot/toolbox/shared/hdllinkmv shdltovcd.mexw32 shdltovcd.mexw32.oldmv shdltovcd.mexw32.csf shdltovcd.mexw32.csf.oldsave shdltovcd.mexw32.R2007b and shdltvocd.mexw32.csf.R2007b attachments to matlabroot/toolbox/shared/hdllinkmv shdltovcd.mexw32.R2007b shdltovcd.mexw32mv shdltovcd.mexw32.csf.R2007b shdltovcd.mexw32.csfexit and restart MATLABOnce the patch is installed, no errors should be generated by the ToVCD block during code generation or compilation.

Summary: num2str displays incomplete data when you specify a format string that contains an escaped %% sequence.
Description: num2str may not print all of the data if a format is specified.  This only occurs if the specified format contains a literal % character, which is written '%%' in order for sprintf to parse it.
Workaround: Replace the %% in the format string with another placeholder character that is unused in the conversion. Then postprocess the answer by replacing the placeholder with %.For example, this num2str, which shows the erroneous behavior:   >> b = num2str(magic(5),'%%%df & ')      b =      %17f & %24f &  %1f &   %23f &  %5f &  %7f &    %4f &  %6f & %13f &   %10f & %12f & %19f &   %11f & %18f & %25f &can be replaced by this sequence:   >> b = num2str(magic(5),'#%df & ')   b =   #17f & #24f &  #1f &  #8f & #15f &   #23f &  #5f &  #7f & #14f & #16f &    #4f &  #6f & #13f & #20f & #22f &   #10f & #12f & #19f & #21f &  #3f &   #11f & #18f & #25f &  #2f &  #9f &      >> b(b=='#')='%'      b =      %17f & %24f &  %1f &  %8f & %15f &   %23f &  %5f &  %7f & %14f & %16f &    %4f &  %6f & %13f & %20f & %22f &   %10f & %12f & %19f & %21f &  %3f &   %11f & %18f & %25f &  %2f &  %9f &

Summary: pearsrnd throws an error for the Type IV distribution.
Description: The pearsrnd  function may throw a spurious error for certain combinations of inputs corresponding to distributions in the Pearson Type IV family. For example >> [r,type] = pearsrnd(1.645e-016,1.002,-0.007758,3.031,0,0)??? Attempted to access xstep(0); index must be a positive integer or logical.
Workaround: Change line 254 (in the current version, 1.1.4.4) fromxstep = x:1:99;toxstep = x:1:100;

Summary: Code generation fails when your model includes blocks that use absolute time
Description: If you have a model that contains a block that requires absolute time inside a function-call subsystem, such as Clock, Digital Clock, From Workspace, or Variable Time Delay, code generation fails with the following error: Reference to non-existent field 'AsyncTaskNeedsAbsoluteTime'
Workaround: Follow these steps to update your installation to stop the error from occurring:Download one of the attached files based on your MATLAB release--either 2007b_getIRInfo.m or R2008a_getIRInfo.m.Rename the file from xxxxx_getIRInfo.m to getIRInfo.m.Put the file getIRInfo.m in your matlab\toolbox\shared\idelinks\pjtgenerator\mdlinfo directory.At the MATLAB? command prompt, enter the following commands before you start to generate code:clear all classesrehash toolboxresetNow you can generate code properly.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when calling numel, length, or ndims on a property in an MCOS handle object.
Description: MATLAB can sometimes crash when calling numel , length , or ndims  on a property in an MCOS handle object. For example,classdef myclass < handle  properties    Data = ones(2,4,3);  end  methods    function b = GetNdims(o)      b = ndims(o.Data);      % MATLAB crashes here    end  endendfunction bug  obj = myclass;  x = obj.GetNdims();
Workaround: Store the property in a temporary variable before calling numel, length, or ndims. For example,    function b = GetNdims(o)      tmp = o.Data;      b = ndims(tmp);    end

Summary: Defining persistent variables in an Embedded MATLAB function that returns multiple outputs may produce incorrect results
Description: When you compile an Embedded MATLAB function in a Simulink model, or by using emlc  or emlmex , an attempt is made to eliminate redundant computation of expressions. This optimization can produce wrong answers under the following circumstances: You define a persistent variable in a function that returns multiple outputs, and The function is inlined in one call site, but not inlined in a different call site.
Workaround: Do not inline functions that return multiple outputs and define persistent variables. Disable inlining by using the eml.inline('never') construct in the function.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Embedded MATLAB Functions in a Simulink model or compiled with emlc or emlmex may produce wrong answers when performing bitwise or and xor mathematical operations.
Description: When compiling an Embedded MATLAB Function, an optimization pass attempts to use the most efficient variable type by analyzing the possible range of values of variables and expressions. This optimization can produce incorrect results if the function performs bitwise or  or bitwise xor  mathematical operations. An obvious example is calling the MATLAB functions bitor  and bitxor . A more subtle example is calling the MATLAB sqrt  function for fixed-point data; in this case, sqrt  performs the bitwise or  operation under the hood. This bug is rare. In most cases, the incorrect analysis results will not lead to any change in the generated code. This bug does not affect simulations where debugging is enabled (debug simulations). One way to determine whether this bug occurred is to check whether a non-debug simulation gives different answers from a debug simulation. This bug also affects code generated using Real-Time Workshop.

Summary: On UNIX, the 'ls' command does not expand the tilde character ('~') at the beginning of a path.
Description: On UNIX, for example, entering the command    ls ~ does not list the user's home directory.
Workaround: Use the unix or ! command to get the correct results. For example, unix('ls ~')

Summary: Attribute name maxLines misspelled in KML files written with function kmlwrite
Description: When function kmlwrite  exports a KML file, the Snippet  element includes an attribute named maxLine  in each Placemark  element. It should be maxLines .
Workaround: Because the KML file contains plain text, you can open it with a text editor and replaceSnippet maxLine=withSnippet maxLines=

Summary: Models that contain Analog Input and Counter Input blocks, both using the Advantech PCI-1710 board, might hang
Description: Models that contain Analog Input and Counter Input blocks, both using the Advantech PCI-1710 board, might hang. This is due to initialization problems in the driver.
Workaround: As a workaround:Stop MATLAB.Unzip, extract, and save the attached file to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\rtwin\drv\AdvantechRestart MATLAB.

Summary: File size increases every time you save a model that contains blocks from the Filter Design Toolbox library if the Filter Design Toolbox software is not installed.
Description: If you have Signal Processing Blockset software installed, but do not have Filter Design Toolbox software installed, and you work on a model that contains blocks from the Filter Design Toolbox library, the file size of the model increases every time you save the model. This happens when you create the model using Filter Design Toolbox software, and then move the model to a system that does not have Filter Design Toolbox software.
Workaround: Open and save the model on a system that has Filter Design Toolbox software installed. The file size of the model will decrease to its original size.

Summary: Incorrect coverage results for Saturation block when using fixed-point data
Description: If you use fixed-point data with a Saturation block, the coverage that is reported after the simulation might not be accurate.

Summary: Code generated by Repeat and Upsample blocks can assert for nonscalar initial conditions in a multitasking environment
Description: The code generated by the Repeat and Upsample blocks can assert when the blocks are used in a multitasking environment and you enter nonscalar initial conditions on the block mask.
Workaround: To work around this issue, either use scalar initial conditions or place your model in a single-tasking environment.

Summary: ABS block may cause dlinmod to fail
Description: If: the zero-crossing detection of the ABS block is turned on; the sample time of the ABS block is set to 0 (continuous sample time); and the ABS block is driven by a noncontinuous sample-time signal;  then dlinmod will fail with the error message "'model_name' implements a Jacobian method, but failed to configure its Jacobian dimensions in the DoPostPropagationTasks function." ?
Workaround: Turn off the zero-crossing detection of the ABS block that caused the problem. This workaround will not affect the model because if the ABS block is driven by a noncontinuous sample-time signal, the zero-crossing detection of this ABS block will already be disabled internally.

Summary: Difficulty positioning a datatip on a Magnitude Response graph in FDAtool and FVTool.
Description: In FDATool and FVTool, when you try to place a datatip on a Magnitude Response graph of a filter (Analysis > Magnitude Response), the datatip goes to the incorrect position. This only happens when Frequency Scale  in Analysis Parameters  is set to Log  (logarithmic).

Summary: Constant block with Boolean vectors/matrices produces errors.
Description: This problem affects models containing a Constant block that produces a Boolean signal with more than one element. It does not affect other signal types or scalar Boolean constants. Running Simulink Design Verifier analysis or compatibility check reports a compatibility error with the message "Engine Translation Failed".
Workaround: A workaround is to have the Constant block produce the same constant as an int8 and to insert a data-conversion block on the output of the Constant block to convert the int8 signal to Boolean.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder generates code for models containing reusable subsystems
Description: Generating code for a model causes MATLAB to crash under all of the following condition: The model contains a reusable subsystem. The reusable subsystem contains either: A Constant block with a custom parameter object A signal that references a custom signal object.   A Mux block connects to either: The output of the Constant block. The signal that references the custom signal object.   At least one input signal of the Mux block is an input to the reusable subsystem.
Workaround: On the input signal of the Mux block that is also an input to the reusable subsystem:Insert a Signal Conversion block.In the Signal Conversion block parameters dialog box, select the Override optimizations and always copy signal check box.

Summary: Documentation error for event.proplistener. The Properties argument must be meta.property objects.
Description: The help for event.proplistener  states that you should provide the property name as the second argument. This is not correct. The constructor errors if you do this; you must provide meta.property  objects to construct property listeners.
Workaround: Pass an object array or a cell array of meta.property object handles instead of the property names.

Summary: For some cases involving addition or subtraction of one-bit values, Simulink HDL Coder generates HDL code that will not compile, or which produces incorrect results.
Description: For some cases involving addition or subtraction of one-bit values (e.g. boolean, ufix1, ufix1_En2, ufix1_En3, etc.), Simulink HDL Coder can produce HDL code that will not compile, or which compiles but produces incorrect results . This situation can arise when the Add, Sum, or Subtract block is used with a data type which is one bit wide, either at a block input or output, or as one of the block's internal data types.
Workaround: To generate correct HDL, use data types that have a width of 2 bits or more with the Add, Sum, and Subtract blocks. Use Data Type Convert blocks to pad any one-bit values appropriately. Synthesis should remove the extra padded bit(s). Alternatively, you can use the Logical Operator block to build a logic gate and achieve the same functionality.

Summary: RCOSDEMO results are incorrect when sampling frequency, Fd, is not 1
Description: When the value of sampling frequency of input signal, Fd, is set to a value other than 1, the input signal delay no longer matches the filter output. The formula to calculate the filter delay is wrong. Instead of "PropD = Delay * Fd;" it should be "PropD = Delay / Fd;".
Workaround: Follow these steps to replace the MATLAB file rcosdemo.m.Quit MATLAB?.Rename the rcosdemo.m file in your current MATLAB installation to another name, such as rcosdemo.m.old. This file is in MATLABROOT\toolbox\comm\commdemos\, where MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by using the matlabroot function at the MATLAB command prompt.Download the attached zip file, containnig rcosdemo.m, to the directory in step 2.Restart MATLAB.Issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Adding a scalar double to an array of integers may result in the wrong answer or a segmentation violation.
Description: There is a bug in MATLAB R2008a. When adding a scalar double to a vector of integers, it is possible to get the wrong answer. A = ones(5,5,'int8');X = A(1,:) + 0 The bug will only arise in the case where a scalar double is the right-most operand added to an integer array.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds for this bug:1) Switch the order of the operands so that the scalar double is first:A = ones(5,5,'int8');X = 0 + A(1,:)2) Cast the scalar double to the same integer type as the other operandA = ones(5,5,'int8')X = A(1,:) + int8(0);

Summary: Wrongly formatted warning or error message for Data Store Memory block during simulation
Description: Simulink generates a wrongly formatted warning or error message during simulation when: Configuration Parameters > Data Validity > Simulation range checking  is set to warning  or error . A Data Store Memory block inputs a complex value that is outside the block's Minimum  or Maximum  value.  This problem affects only the formatting of the warning or error message, not the behavior of the Data Store Memory block itself.
Workaround: Either set the Data Store Memory block's Minimum and Maximum to [], which disables range checking for the block, or set Configuration Parameters > Data Validity > Simulation range checking to none, which disables range checking for the model.

Summary: multibandread creates return variable of the wrong size when using band subsetting on BIP files.
Description: When subsetting a band-interleaved-by-pixel (BIP) file by band, multibandread  returns data the wrong size. The extent in the band dimension is too large and the extra elements are filled with zeros.

Summary: Error message when using a Unit Delay block to build a discrete-time accumulator and connecting the resulting output signal to multiple Concatenate blocks
Description: This bug is due to insufficient information sharing between two optimization features in Simulink: Optimization 1  Unit Delay and Sum blocks are often used to build discrete-time accumulator. Simulink recognizes this common modeling pattern and optimizes simulation and code generation processes by treating the Unit Delay and the Sum blocks as an atomic unit. Optimization 2  Concatenate blocks are often optimized to eliminate extra copies of the input signals.  The interactions between the above optimizations can lead to an error message such as: Invalid memory layout connection setting found in 'accumulator/Sum' output port 1. Please contact The MathWorks for assistance with this error message. See the attached file accumulator.mdl  for an example model that exhibits this problem.
Workaround: To work around this issue, do one of the following:Disable block reduction optimization by unchecking Block reduction option in the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog.Insert a Signal Conversion block between the output of Unit Delay block and the feedback input of the Sum block to break the accumulator pattern. Be sure to check Override optimizations and always copy signal.Insert a Signal Conversion block between the output of the Sum block and the input of every Concatenate block connected to it. This prevents the interaction between the two optimizations described above. Be sure to check Override optimizations and always copy signal.

Summary: wavedec and wavedec2 stop MATLAB when the x input argument is an empty matrix
Description: If the x  input argument to wavedec  or wavedec2  is an empty matrix, MATLAB stops.
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the current wavedec.m and wavedec2.m files in your $ROOT\toolbox\wavelet\wavelet directory with the files in the Attachments section below.

Summary: External mode: parameters that should be tunable during simulation are not tunable
Description: During external mode simulation of a model, you might be prevented from tuning a parameter that should be tunable. This is caused by an indexing error during parameter processing.

Summary: Build process failure when generating code for model with triggered subsystem
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail to generate code under all of the following conditions: The Inline Parameters  check box is selected in the Configuration Parameters dialog box. The model contains a Triggered Subsystem block that has the trigger port either grounded or connected to a Constant block with non-tunable parameter. The triggered subsystem contains a Subsystem block with the Real-Time Workshop system code  option set to Function  or Reusable function .  The build process reports the failure message in the MATLAB Command Window similar to the following: Stack Trace:  [0] libmwcg_ir.so:cg_const_type(CG_Const const*)  [1] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::getFcnConstRTWCGTag(CG_Const*)
Workaround: Enter the following at the MATLAB command line:slfeature('RTWCGIncrementalCodeGeneration',0)Note: Entering this command causes increased memory consumption during Real-Time Workshop code generation.

Summary: External mode: uploading complex parameter values from targets other than xPC, Freescale MPC5xx, Infineon C166, and Texas Instruments C2000 can crash MATLAB session
Description: In external mode, each time the Simulink engine connects to a target program that was generated with Inline parameters  on, the target program uploads the current value of its parameters (if any) to the host. For targets other than xPC, Freescale MPC5xx, Infineon C166, and Texas Instruments C2000, if any of the uploaded parameter values are complex, MATLAB software might crash due to a communication buffer overflow.
Workaround: To work around this bug, either turn off Inline parameters for the target program or avoid using complex parameters.

Summary: Applications accesing a component generated by MATLAB Builder NE may unexpectedly throw a System.AccessViolationException
Description: An application accesing a component generated by MATLAB Builder NE may unexpectedly throw a System.AccessViolationException  usually after either explicitly or implicitly creating a MATLAB array. This is due to the fact that the unmanaged libmx  functions used to create the MATLAB arrays wrapped by managed MWArray  types do not throw an exception if they are not able to create the requested array. This may be due to a lack of heap space or a badly fragmented heap, in which case the function simply returns a null  pointer. When the pointer is later referenced in the managed function MWArray.SetMXArray , it causes the access violation.
Workaround: For now the only work around is to make sure that all code referencing a method belonging to a MATLAB Builder NE-generated component does so within a try-catch block that checks for System.AccessViolationExceptions.

Summary: Generated code for Stateflow chart fails to compile due to conversion from constant type to non-constant type
Description: When you set C++ as the Real-Time Workshop target language, generated code for Stateflow charts might not compile due to passing of constant type data to a function that has a non-constant type parameter. The following is an example of a compiler error: error C2664: 'function_foo' : cannot convert parameter 1 from 'const int8_T [3]' to 'int8_T []'Conversion loses qualifiers

Summary: Model Advisor fails to warn about disabled library links to S-function blocks
Description: When using the Model Advisor to search for disabled library links in a model, disabled links to S-function blocks are not shown in the report.
Workaround: Instead of using the Model Advisor, you can search for disabled library links from the MATLAB command line as follows:handles = find_system(system,'RegExp','on','FollowLinks','on','LookUnderMasks','on','AncestorBlock','.');where "system" is the name of the block diagram or subsystem in which to search.?

Summary: Reordering output signals in a Bus Selector block can cause errors in downstream Bus Selectors
Description: When you change the order of output signals in a Bus Selector block, Simulink automatically propagates the output signal names to downstream blocks. When one of the reordered signals is a bus signal, downstream Bus Selector blocks that output this bus signal may be updated incorrectly.
Workaround: Open the Block Parameters dialog of any downstream Bus Selector block that does not output the intended signals and specify the correct signals to output.

Summary: Using function handles in combination with indexed assignment may violate MATLAB value semantics.
Description: There are cases in which the use of function handles can break MATLAB value semantics. This may occur when using indexed assignment on a variable that has beenpassed into a function by means of a function handle, or referenced in the body of a function that has been called through a function handle. This can result in an overwrite of the contents of a closure. For example,  function x=hshared_bug(exp)  wrapper = @wrap;  val = exp;  wrapped_val = wrapper(val);  % ambiguous use of wrapper  val(1,1) = -10;  x = wrapped_val();  end  function wrapped_val = wrap(data)     function val = closure()        val = data;     end  wrapped_val = @closure;  end  hshared_bug(1)  ans =       1  hshared_bug(1)  ans =     -10
Workaround: To work around this problem, enter the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:  feature jitallow force_share_checks on

Summary: Simulink may log incorrect data for a virtual bus signal
Description: When Simulink logs a virtual bus signal, the log contains incorrect data if: The bus originated as a nonvirtual bus upstream of the logging point. The nonvirtual bus was converted to a virtual bus within a conditionally executed subsystem. The logging point is downstream of the conditionally executed subsystem. The bus source, the conditionally executed subsystem, and the logging point all have different sample times.  In this case, Simulink will log incorrect data for the bus signal.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block at the location where the virtual bus is being logged.Set the Signal Conversion block's Source parameter to Bus Copy mode.Log the virtual bus that the Signal Conversion block outputs, rather than the input bus.

Summary: Simulating models that contain two or more Multiport Switch blocks could create a segV.?
Description: Simulating the models that contain two or more Multiport Switch blocks could create a segV. The segV occurs when the following conditions are met: Simulink model coverage tool is used. The model has more than one Multiport Switch block. The Multiport Switch blocks have different input types, i.e., some Multiport Switch blocks have a fixed-point input type while others only have floating types.
Workaround: 1. Use only the same input type for all Multiport Switch blocks in the modelor 2. Disable coverage for some/all Multiport Switch blocks with different input type with the command set_param(blockh, 'DisableCoverage').

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The code generated by the Abs block returns the wrong result.
Description: The code generated by the Abs block gives an incorrect result when all four of the following conditions are true: 1. the input and output types are heterogeneous; 2. the input type is signed integer; 3. the output overflows; 4. and the user unchecks the checkbox 'Saturate on integer overflow'.

Summary: Functions with ambiguous return types may cause a performance problem.
Description: Under certain circumstances, the JIT may be unable to determine a consistent return type for a function.  The worst case is when a function may return either a scalar or non-scalar for a particular set of inputs. In the following example, even though the sum  function returns a scalar, the JIT is unable to optimize for the scalar case.  This is because the shape of the return value of the two input version of sum  is not statically determinable.  So, the JIT must assume the worst case output type for nansum  (a row of type double) instead of the actual output type (a scalar double). function y = nansum(x,dim)  x(isnan(x)) = 0;  if nargin == 1    y = sum(x);  else    y = sum(x,dim);  endfunction bug  x = nansum([1 2 3]);
Workaround: To work around this problem, you can do either of the following:In the body of any caller to the badly behaved function, use the commandfeature('scopedAccelEnablement','off')Use custom versions of the badly behaved function that don't have ambiguous output types.For example,function y = nansum(x)  x(isnan(x)) = 0;  y = sum(x);function y = nansumd(x,dim)  x(isnan(x)) = 0;  y = sum(x,dim);

Summary: Selecting an object with an empty parameter dialog can crash the Model Explorer
Description: Some objects, for example, EML blocks, use an invalid method for indicating that they do not have a parameter dialog box. Selecting such objects in the Model Explorer's tree view can cause the Model Explorer to crash.

Summary: Transfer Fcn First Order and Unit Delay blocks: generated code gives wrong result when block parameter 'Initial condition' is complex
Description: Generated code gives the wrong numerical result for the Transfer Fcn First Order block and the Unit Delay block. This can happen when you set the block parameter 'Initial condition' on the dialog box as a complex number, and when the real part of the complex number is zero or negative.

Summary: For R2007b and R2008a, the SaveAs feature handles custom targets in an incorrect and unpredictable manner.
Description: The selector block performs incorrectly during a model SaveAs if all of the following conditions are met: 1) A SaveAs of a model from either version R2007b or R2008a to version R2006b or earlier is performed. 2) The Selector block has an external source of index 3) A custom target for RTW builds is being used. This problem is fixed in R2008b.
Workaround: As a workaround, open your current (R2007b or R2008a) model and set your target to the GRTTarget. Perform a SaveAs to R2006b or earlier. Then open the R2006b or earlier version that you just created and set your model to your custom target.

Summary: MATLAB crashes or creates an illegal cell array when assigning a cell array into itself
Description: MATLAB may crash or create an illegal cell array if an attempt is made to assign a cell array into itself. For example,    c={};  c{end+1} = c;  whosor  c={{1}};  c{end+1} = c;  whos
Workaround: Use a temporary variable for the right hand side of the assignment expression. For example,  (1) temp=c;      c{end+1} = temp;      whos;  (2) c={{1}};      temp=c;      c{end+1} = temp;      whos;

Summary: When signal logging is enabled in a triggered subsystem, signals are logged incorrectly in Accelerator mode
Description: If a model has signal logging enabled inside a triggered subsystem, signals should be logged only at the times of trigger events. However, when the model simulates in Accelerator mode, signals are logged multiple times between trigger events.
Workaround: Replace commonbodlib.tlc by attached file. I am running sbruntests to qualify the workaround

Summary: External mode simulation of model with fixed-point data types on target PC might produce wrong answers under certain conditions
Description: External mode simulation of a model on an xPC Target target PC might produce wrong answers if either or both of the following conditions are true: Parameters or signals that are being downloaded or uploaded use fixed-point data types aliased through some other data type, such as a custom data type derived from the Simulink.NumericType  data object class. The model is configured with the Inline parameters  option off and contains Stateflow parameters with fixed-point data types.  External mode simulation for a target PC does not properly handle the fixed-point data types under these conditions. The Simulink software expects the fixed-point parameter and signal values to be in Stored Integer form, while the xPC Target interface requires these values to be in Real World Value form. The external mode simulation will not correctly convert the parameter and signal values between the Simulink host and target PC, resulting in a possible wrong answer.
Workaround: To work around this bug, apply either or both of the following actions, as appropriate for your model:Do not use custom data types to alias the fixed-point data types for your parameters or signals. Use the fixed-point data types directly.If your model contains Stateflow parameters with fixed-point data types, select the Inline parameters option for your model (for example, using the Optimization pane in the Configuration Parameters dialog box).

Summary: Changing the name of a bus and then selecting one of its component signals crashes  the Bus Editor
Description: The bus editor crashes if you change the name of a bus in the editor's dialog view and then select one of the bus's component signals in the editor's tree view.

Summary: Reading a static property of a handle class object may not return correct information when the Accelerator is enabled.
Description: Reading a static property of a handle class may return inaccurate information if the following conditions are met: The handle is being accessed as a property of another handle class. The MATLAB Accelerator is enabled.  In the following example, the value of x is not reliable if you have the Accelerator enabled:    x = handleClass1.handleClass2.static_property
Workaround: You can work around this problem by replacing the statement   x = handleClass1.handleClass2.static_propertywith two statements that use a temporary variable:   temp = handleClass1.handleClass2;    x = temp.static_property;

Summary: SPTool FFT spectrum incorrectly scaled by 1/NFFT
Description: The amplitude of the FFT-based spectrum in the SPTool Spectrum Viewer incorrectly varies with the number of points (NFFT ) at which the DFT is evaluated.

Summary: NaNs ignored in element-wise logical AND (&) and OR (|) operators with scalar inputs.
Description: The presence of NaNs in input arrays normally causes the & and | operators to return an error. When the & or | operator is used with a scalar operand that is sufficient to determine the result, the operator uses short-circuiting to return a result, even when NaNs are present in the other operand. This happens only in situations where JIT is disabled.      feature accel off  1|[NaN 2 3]     ans =         1 1 1

Summary: Not all samples are output with some combinations of RepeatOutput and BufferingConfig
Description:
Not all samples are output when you generate output with an analog output object with a combination of RepeatOutput  and BufferingConfig  properties, and where the result of the following equation equals zero:bufferConfigValue = ao.BufferingConfig; % ao is the analog output objectmod((ao.RepeatOutput + 1), floor(bufferConfigValue(1) / length(data))ans = 0 In the following code you should see 750000 samples output since you have queued 25000 samples and set RepeatOutput  to 29 (getting 29 + 1 = 30 copies of the output). But only 500000 samples are output.% Create and configure an analog output object with 2 channels.ao = analogoutput('nidaq','Dev2');addchannel(ao,0:1);set(ao,'SampleRate', 25000);set(ao,'BufferingConfig', [2^18 3]);set(ao, 'RepeatOutput', 29); % Queue data to the device and start the generation.data = zeros(25000, 2);putdata(ao, data);start(ao) % Wait for the generation to complete.fprintf('Generation started, waiting to complete...
')  wait(ao, ao.SampleRate * (ao.RepeatOutput + 1) + 2); % See how many samples we expected and how many were actually output.expectedSamplesOutput = length(data) * (ao.RepeatOutput + 1);fprintf('Expected samples output: %d
', expectedSamplesOutput)fprintf(' Actual samples output: %d
', ao.SamplesOutput)delete(ao) The above code generates:Generation started, waiting to complete...Expected samples output: 750000Actual samples output: 500000 If you change RepeatOutput  from 29 to 28 all samples are output:Generation started, waiting to complete...Expected samples output: 725000Actual samples output: 725000 Similarly, if you change BufferingConfig  property's buffer size from 2^18 to 2^19, all expected samples are output:Generation started, waiting to complete...Expected samples output: 750000Actual samples output: 750000
Workaround: When you manually change BufferingConfig, buffers are not always output as expected. You can work around this issue in the following ways:Do not modify BufferingConfig and let the engine compute the optimum values.Change RepeatOutput, BufferingConfig or the length of the data so that the equation returns a non-zero result: bufferConfigValue = ao.BufferingConfig;mod((ao.RepeatOutput + 1), floor(bufferConfigValue(1) / length(data))

Summary: Using delete_block to remove a block while the Model Explorer is open may result in a crash.
Description: If the Model Explorer is open and the current selection is a child of a block that is removed programatically via delete_block, the Model Explorer may crash. This crash is indeterministic and happens only sporadically.
Workaround: Change the selection or close the Model Explorer before invoking delete_block.

Summary: Incomplete code generated and MATLAB crash for model with Unicode characters in block description
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might generate incomplete C code and crash MATLAB under all of the following conditions: The block descriptions of some blocks in the model contain Unicode characters. The Simulink block descriptions  check box is selected in the Configuration Parameters > Real-Time Workshop > Comments pane.
Workaround: Do either of the following:Change the block descriptions so no blocks in the model contain Unicode characters.Clear the Simulink block descriptions check box in the Configuration Parameters > Real-Time Workshop > Comments pane.

Summary: Using the OpenWatcom 1.3 compiler to build a model that contains a baseboard serial driver block causes errors
Description: Using the OpenWatcom 1.3 compiler to build a model that contains a baseboard serial driver block causes errors.
Workaround: To work around this issue:Stop MATLAB.Download the attached file sertxenablebase.c and save it to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks.Restart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.

Summary: Undocumented compatibility consideration for function prototypes and referenced models
Description: In R2008a, the following compatibility consideration was introduced but not noted in the release notes. Prior to R2008a, the code generator ignored function prototype specifications for reference models. In R2008a, when a referenced model is configured for controlling function prototypes and multi-instance implementations, the code generator reports an error. You can fix the error by doing one of the following: Configure the referenced model for single-instance implementation. Disable controlling function prototypes for the model.

Summary: dfilt.df1 display of states truncated when using compact format spacing
Description: If you set the formatspacing  property of the root to 'compact' , the Denominator property of the states of a dfilt.df1  filter object is not displayed. For example, >>set(0,'formatspacing','compact')>>filt = dfilt.df1(.1,[1 -.9]);>>filt.PersistentMemory = true;>>filt.States.Denominator = 50filt =  FilterStructure: 'Direct-Form I'        Numerator: 0.1       Denominator: [1 -0.9]  PersistentMemory: true            States: Numerator: []                                 <- Denominator: 50 is missing ? ? ?
Workaround: To fix this problem, copy the attached file to your $MATLABROOT\toolbox\signal\sigtools directory, overwriting the previous version of siguddutils.m and then type rehash toolboxcache at the MATLAB prompt.?

Summary: Attempting to copy a port block by dragging can prevent adding blocks to a subsystem
Description: If you attempt to copy a trigger, enable, or function-call generator port block by dragging it, Simulink correctly warns you that the copy is illegal and aborts the copy operation. However, it then incorrectly repeats the warning and aborts the operation if you subsequently attempt to add other types of blocks to the subsystem.
Workaround: To work around this issue, create a new Simulink model and drag any block into it. You will see both the block you added as well as the copy of the original port block. Close and discard this temporary model.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Nested conditionally executed subsystems generate code that gives wrong answer
Description: The Real-Time Workshop code generator might create code that gives the wrong answer for a model that contains a conditionally executed subsystem (for example, Enabled, Triggered, Function-Call, or Action Subsystem Blocks) with all of the following conditions: The conditionally executed subsystem contains If Action Subsystem Blocks, one of which connects to an else port. A Merge block is placed outside of the parent conditionally executed subsystem and combines all of the output signals.  ?

Summary: Generating a Simulink Manifest can give errors when there are dependencies on built-in methods
Description: Generating a Simulink Manifest for a model that has file dependencies upon MATLAB built-in methods can produce an error dialog containing: cannot read input file inputParser is a built-in method ?
Workaround: To avoid this error you can clear the check box for the option "Analyze MATLAB files" in the "Generate Model Manifest" dialog box. You can then generate the manifest without errors.Any MATLAB files that are directly referenced by the model will still be found (for example, any model or block callbacks). However, those MATLAB files are not analyzed further, and some file dependencies may be not be reported.?

Summary: snapnow produces errors or odd output
Description: Including snapnow  in a published MATLAB file sometimes produces these errors: ??? Attempt to reference field of non-structure array.Error in ==> snapnow at 41iCell = data.lastGo;Error in ==> test_snapnow at 5 snapnow; Or strange command line output, such as the following: WDEavRCxrZ0000100012WDEavRCxr...

Summary: Error message about a nonexistent attempt to change the data type of an S-function or Stateflow parameter
Description: If a model contains an S-function block or Stateflow chart that uses an alias to an existing numeric type, and you take any action during simulation that requires Simulink to update the diagram before resuming simulation, Simulink posts an error message and cannot resume simulation. The error message is similar to the following: Stateflow Interface Error: Cannot change data type of parameter 1 in '.../Chart/SFunction'  This error message appears whether or not you actually tried to change the data type of any parameter, which is not allowed during simulation.

Summary: Assertion during code generation for model containing Selector block and Stateflow chart
Description: Generating code for a model containing a Selector block and a Stateflow chart might cause an assertion if the model meets all of the following conditions: The input and output of the Selector block are 1-D (unoriented) vectors. The output of the Selector block is connected to a Stateflow chart. The input of the Stateflow chart is an argument in a function call.  You can use the attached model to see an example of this issue.
Workaround: Enter the following command at the MATLAB command line:sf('feature', 'coder unification', 0)

Description: If you specify an empty string as input to cdfwrite , it might cause a segmentation violation. Sometimes the segmentation violation is immediate; sometimes it happens two or three commands later.

This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.?????? Exit MATLAB.?????? Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.?????? Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: ????????????? ????????????????????
