Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>????????????? ?????? ?????? Extract the files from the .zip file. ????????????? ???????????????????? Windows Systems:?????????????????????????? ????????????????????????????????? Open the .zip file.????????????????????????????????? Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.?????????????????????????? ???????????????????? ???????????????????? Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): ?????????????????????????? cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>???????????????????? ????????????? ?????? ?????? Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: MATLAB stops responding when you run an acquisition or a generation in a loop, over a long period of time
Description: If you create an analog input object and, in a loop, call start  and getdata , MATLAB may stop responding after a long period of time. You will need to end the MATLAB session using the Windows Task Manager. Example code: ai = analoginput('nidaq','Dev1');addchannel(ai,0);  for ii=1:1e8  start(ai);  wait(ai,2);  data = getdata(ai);  end;   You will also need to end the MATLAB session when you create an analog output object and generate data in a loop. ?
Workaround: When the object stops, there is a resource leak that causes this issue.For analog input objects you can work around this issue by using TriggerRepeat, Manual triggering, and a callback function as shown:function triggertestSamplesAcquiredFcn()% This code shows an alternative to running start/getdata repeatedly.ai = analoginput('nidaq','Dev1');addchannel(ai,0);% Instead of executing start repeatedly, we will start the ai object once% and trigger repeatedly. Set the TriggerRepeat to% infinite and the TriggerType to Manual. ai.TriggerRepeat=Inf;ai.TriggerType = 'Manual';% Set up our callback to get all the data for every trigger.% Learn more about callbacks by executing:% web([docroot '/toolbox/daq/f9-34753.html'])% Especially read this section of the Help: % web([docroot '/toolbox/daq/f9-34753.html#f9-119409']) ai.SamplesAcquiredFcn = {@yourDataIsReady}; ai.SamplesAcquiredFcnCount = ai.SamplesPerTrigger;% For this example store iteration count in UserData.ai.UserData = 0;% Start the ai object once.start(ai)% Trigger the ai object. trigger(ai)% The main function will pause, but the callback will execute.seconds = 60;fprintf('Waiting for %d seconds.\n', seconds);pause(seconds)disp('Acquisition complete.') % Stop the device manually since the TriggerRepeat is infinite.stop(ai)% Always release resources by deleting objects you no longer need.delete(ai)endfunction yourDataIsReady(obj,event)% If you are in the callback the count of samples requested is acquired % (as you set SamplesAcquiredFcnCount to SamplesPerTrigger).data = getdata(obj);% Increment the iteration counter and display.obj.UserData = obj.UserData + 1;disp(obj.UserData)% You must trigger every time to get the next set of data.trigger(obj)endAnother option is to use TimerFcn rather than SamplesAcquiredFcn or to leave TriggerRepeat set to zero and set SamplesPerTrigger to Inf. In the second casespecify the number samples to read when you call getdata.For analog output objects use RepeatOutput and the SamplesOutputFcn function.

Summary: In SISO Design Tool, the optimization errors out when it automatically restarts
Description: When optimizing the response of a system in SISO Design Tool, the optimization errors out under the following conditions: The value of the Restarts  field in the Options dialog box is greater than 0, and  The optimization automatically restarts after it does not find a solution during the first run
Workaround: If you are using a previous version, please read the following:1. Replace the following files with the attached files:<matlabroot>\toolbox\sloptim\sloptim\@sroengine\@Optimization\initpar.m and<matlabroot>\toolbox\sloptim\sloptim\@sroengine\@Optimization\optimize.m2. Type the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier fails to generate parameter values for instances of Simulink.Parameter
Description: Simulink Design Verifier ignores its parameter configuration file and fails to generate values for parameters that are instances of Simulink.Parameter or instances of any subclass of Simulink.Parameter. In release 1.2 (R2008a), Simulink Design Verifier uses the initial value of the parameters as a constant during analysis. In releases prior to 1.2 (R2008a) Simulink Design Verifier treats instances of parameter values as unspecified inputs and might generate invalid results.

Summary: Wrong results in linearization and simulation are possible for certain models with differential state constraints
Description: For a very restricted class of models, it is possible to get wrong results in linearization and simulation. In particular, only models with differential state constraints (for instance, capacitors or masses in parallel) sometimes exhibit this bug.
Workaround: If you are using Simscape 2.1, we provide a patch which will trigger an error for exactly the models which exhibit the bug. This patch is applied by copying the attached file, nesl_compile.p, into the following directory:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/network_engine/ne_sli/private/(where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt).Restart MATLAB after copying the patch.If you are using Simscape 2.0, to avoid this bug assure that each output of a Simulink-PS Converter block feeds only one block -- that is, the line from the output of each Simulink-PS Converter does not branch. This can be achieved by doing the branching on the Simulink side and having multiple Simulink-PS Converter blocks which each feed only one block.

Summary: Missing modelname.a2l file in generated code when you select ccslink_grt.tlc as the system target file
Description: When you set the following parameters and values in the configuration parameters for your model, code generation fails to generate the ASAP2 file modelname .a21 : Select ccslink_grt.tlc  from the System target file  list. Select ASAP2  from the Interface  list.  After you generate code from your model, you do not find the file modelname .a21  in the project. Without the file, you cannot use CANape to tune the parameters in your model.
Workaround: Follow these steps to update your installation to generate correctly the ASAP2 file modelname.a21:Downolad and unzip the attached file.Extract the file, getStringFormat.p, to your toolbox\shared\idelinks\pjtgenerator\@LinkTgtPkg\@GRTTgtLink directory.At the MATLAB? command prompt, enter the following commands before you start to generate code:clear all classesrehash toolboxresetNow you can generate code and create the ASAP2 file.

Summary: Timed-based linearization of models with action subsystems may return incorrect results when computing multiple snapshots during a simulation
Description: Linearizing a Simulink? model with an action subsystem using time-based linearization may return incorrect results when the outputs of two or more action subsystems are routed through a merge block. The incorrect result occurs when you compute multiple linearization snapshots. An example of this situtation is shown in the attached model mif.mdl. This problem also occurs when using the Simulink? Control Design? linearize  command with the simulation snapshot option.
Workaround: To work around this issue, do one of the following actions:Perform a single linearization snapshotReplace the merge block, which routes the output of the action subsystems, with a multi-port switch block

Summary: MWArray virtual indexing does not work.
Description: Virtual indexing using the abstract MWArray  class does not work, as illustrated in the following example: // This works:  MWCellArray cellArray= new MWCellArray(new int[]{5});  cellArray[1]= new MWNumericArray(5);  ? // This does not work:  MWArray array= new MWCellArray(new int[]{5});  array[1]= new MWNumericArray(5);  ?
Workaround: Until you install the fixed version, avoid virtual indexing using the MWArray class. Instead, explicitly cast the array to the appropriate type as follows:MWArray array= new MWCellArray(new int[]{5});((MWCellArray)array)[1]= new MWNumericArray(5);?

Summary: Warning about zooming in on docked scope in R2008a
Description: After editing the plot properties of a docked plot with MATLAB 7.6 (R2008a), a warning is issued if you try to zoom in on a second docked scope.

Summary: Segmentation violation when creating certain anonymous functions
Description: A segmentation violation can occur if you attempt to create an anonymous function that has complicated indexing and also involves the use of the end  keyword. For example, a segmentation occurs when you attempt to execute the following statement:    f = @(x) (x{1}(end))
Workaround: You can avoid he use of end by using the numel or size functions.    f = @(x) (x{1}(numel(x{1})))

Summary: Simulink fails to detect invalid connection on branch from asynchronous function call subsystem output port
Description: MathWorks has identified a bug in Simulink related to asynchronous function-call subsystems. This bug has been in releases R2006a to R2008a. When all of these three points are true in R2008a and earlier, Simulink fails to detect an incorrect connection: Output port of an asynchronous function-call subsystem has more than one branch. At least one of the branches has a correct connection: an asynchronous output port connects to a Rate Transition Block or a root port that inherits the sample time. At least one of the branches has an incorrect connection: an asynchronous output drives a non-virtual block that is not a Rate Transition Block or a root port that inherits sample time. In addition, this non-virtual block inherits the sample time.  Simulink does not correctly detect this incorrect connection and does not issue an error message. The bug is that there is inconsistent error checking on different modeling patterns. If the output port does not branch, Simulink successfully detects an incorrect connection. But Simulink does not detect an incorrect connection if the signal branches and one of the branches has a correct connection.When the incorrect connection is to an operational non-virtual block (not a non-operational or NoOp block), the simulation results might be incorrect, or there might be a Segmentation Violation (SegV), or the sample time colors might be misleading, depending on the exact model.This is because the sample time determines the execution of the block and sample time information might be used in the block algorithm. Incorrect sample time information results in incorrect behavior or a SegV. The sample time color may be incorrect and misleading, regardless of whether the block is non-virtual or virtual. This bug was found and fixed as part of code re-factoring and cleanup performed in R2008b to improve Simulink code quality.
Workaround: Currently there is no workaround other than avoiding the design patterns illustrated in the attachment images

Summary: Error: could not recognise token while call graph parsing
Description: The following problem occurs with the PolySpace (TM) for Ada R2008a product. An error popup is displayed when trying to open results in the Viewer: "Error: could not recognise token while call graph parsing. Parsing aborted."
Workaround: Use the file bin.zip in attachment and unzip its content in the folder <PolySpace_Common>/Viewer/bin.On a Standard Windows installation <PolySpace_Common> is located in c:\PolySpace.On a Standard Linux/UNIX installation <PolySpace_Common> is located in /usr/local/PolySpace/PolySpace_Common?

Summary: CAGE Strategies saved as Simulink models before R2008a cannot be imported into later versions of the Model-Based Calibration Toolbox software.
Description: CAGE Strategies developed in Model-Based Calibration Toolbox software before R2008a and saved as Simulink Models cannot be imported into later versions of the Model-Based Calibration Toolbox software. This does not affect strategies within CAGE projects. This is due to a change in parsing behavior. Pre-R2008a, the CAGE strategy parser interpreted 1D tables as normalizers if they fed into a lookup table. In R2008a, a normalizer block was added to the cgeqlib library, and lookup table blocks are now always interpreted as lookup tables. This change creates an incompatibility for old strategies saved as mdl files when you try to reimport them into CAGE.
Workaround: You can use this workaround if you need to parse Simulink model files of CAGE strategies that were built before R2008a.1.) Copy cgStrategyTables.m to <matlabroot>/toolbox/mbc/mbcexpr2.) Copy cgslparser.m and lookup.m to <matlabroot>/toolbox/mbc/mbcexpr/@cgslparser3.) Restart MATLAB.4.) Type 'rehash toolboxcache' at the MATLAB command-line.?

Summary: External mode: target stop crashes ERT-based VxWorks executable
Description: When using external mode to run an ERT-based VxWorks executable, stopping the target or reaching the stop time crashes the executable. External mode cleanup code in MdlTerminate()  tries to delete semaphores already deleted by VxWorks ERT cleanup code in main() , causing an exception.

Summary: web function with Firefox? 3 browser on Windows? platforms does not display page
Description: On Microsoft? Windows platforms, if you have the Mozilla? Firefox 3 browser set as your default system browser, when you run the web  function using the -browser  option, the Firefox browser opens but the requested page does not display.
Workaround: If you are using MATLAB version 7.0 (Release 14) or later, do the following:Download the attached zip file to  matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/helptoolsUnzip the file, replacing the existing web.m file in that directory.

Summary: Error when deselecting Generate makefile option on the Real-Time Workshop configuration pane
Description: Deselecting Generate makefile  option on the Real-Time Workshop configuration pane and generating code gives the following error and warning message if there are blocks from Simscape or any of the Simscape vertical products in the Simulink model. ### WARNING: Unable to run 'rtwmakecfg' file in directory: 'c:\MATLAB\toolbox\physmod\mech\mech' due to error: 'Error using ==> loadTMF at 16File I/O error on file ''Operation that caused an error: open'
Workaround: 1. Make a backup copy of the existing file $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/pm_sli/pm_sli/pmsl_rtwmakecfg.p by renaming it to $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/pm_sli/pm_sli/pmsl_rtwmakecfg.p_8a(where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by enteringmatlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window)2. Download the attached file pmsl_rtwmakecfg.p into the directory $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/pm_sli/pm_sli/.

Summary: Spurious warning about implicit signal resolution with right-click subsystem build
Description: Real-Time Workshop posts a spurious warning message about implicit signal resolution when: Configuration Parameters > Data Validity > Diagnostics > Signal resolution  is set to Explicit and warn implicit . A subsystem exists with root input/output signals explicitly marked as Signal name must resolve to signal object . You right-click the subsystem and choose either of the following from its context menu: Real-Time Workshop > Build Subsystem  Real-Time Workshop > Generate S-function     The warning message appears even if no implicit signal resolution occurs when simulating or generating code for the complete model.
Workaround: Change the value of Configuration Parameters > Data Validity> Diagnostics > Signal resolution to Explicit and implicit.

Summary: Closing a model after searching its model reference hierarchy in the Model Explorer can crash MATLAB
Description: If the results of searching a model reference hierarchy are left open in the Model Explorer, MATLAB can crash when you close the model whose hierarchy was searched.
Workaround: Close the Model Explorer before closing a model that references other models.

Summary: Changing current directory by selecting previous directory from list and pressing Enter does not work
Description: When you select a previous current directory from the dropdown list that is part of the address field in the MATLAB desktop or undocked Current Directory browser, and press Enter , it does not change the current directory but should.
Workaround: To change the current directory to a previous current directory in the list, select the previous current directory and click using your pointing device instead of using the Enter key.

Summary: H5D.read returns incorrect data types for compound datasets.
Description: H5D.read  is returning signed data types for compound datasets when the fields are actually unsigned integer types. Atomic datasets consisting of integer data types are not affected by this.

This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.?????? Exit MATLAB.?????? Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.?????? Summary: Calling a built-in class's empty method can crash MATLAB.
Description: There are several ways to reproduce this crash. They are all related to calling the empty  method of a built-in class. One example is: struct.empty Another example is: [logical.empty true]
Workaround: Call other MATLAB functions that can return empty data for the built-in class.For example:struct([])Or:[logical([]) true]

Summary: Subsystem incorrectly inherits constant sample time causing blocks inside not to run
Description: Sample time propagation incorrectly assigns a constant sample time to a subsystem block if: the subsystem is inside a triggered-type subsystem (e.g., triggered, function-call, etc.) the subsystem contains only: blocks with constant sample time subsystem blocks that contain blocks with triggered sample time   This subsystem should receive a triggered sample time through sample time propagation. The constant sample time of the outer subsystem causes the triggered blocks inside the inner subsystem to never run.
Workaround: Add any nonsubsystem block that has a triggered sample time (cyan sample time color) to the subsystem which is incorrectly inheriting a constant sample time.?

Summary: Accelerated mode simulation errors due to failure in subsystem code reuse
Description: Some models simulate without error in normal mode simulation but error out when run in Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator mode because of a compatability issue with subsystem code reuse. This happens when the model contains a set of subsystems configured to share code for the Real-Time Workshop, and that set of subsystems uses some feature that is not compatible with code reuse in accelerated simulations. An example of such a feature is the embedded MATLAB block.
Workaround: This can be worked around by setting the "Real-Time Workshop function name options" parameter to "auto" for the subsystems itemized in the error message.

Summary: External mode simulation may display incorrect data for uploaded Merge block signals
Description: During external mode simulation of a model in which a Merge block is driving an uploading block (a block such as Scope or Display that uploads target signal data to the Simulink host), the displayed signal data may be incorrect.
Workaround: To work around this bug, add a nonvirtual block that does not alter the signal value between the Merge block and the uploading block. For example, add an atomic subsystem through which the signal value passes unchanged.

Summary: MATLAB Version R2008a can crash when you use the clear classes command, if you have created package level classes.
Description: This sporadic failure can happen when a user puts the class files for both a subclass and its base class in the same package. When the user creates an instance of this subclass and then tries to use 'clear classes  MATLAB can crash. Packages are a new feature for R2008a.
Workaround: There are two ways you can avoid this problem:Use clear all instead of clear classes. In most conditions, clear all has the same effect as clear classes with respect to updating classes, but does not cause MATLAB to crash.Do not put classes under package directories, or put the base class and its subclasses in different packages.

Summary: MATLAB crashes or gives an incorrect answer when Ctrl+C is used or an error is thrown
Description: In rare cases, MATLAB may crash or give an incorrect answer if computation is interrupted with Ctrl+C or an error.  This only happens in the case where the function being interrupted is called from a script.
Workaround: If possible, change the calling MATLAB file to a function instead of a script.  If this is not an option, you can keep the JIT from making function calls from scripts by entering the following command at the MATLAB prompt:   feature jitallow script_calls off

Summary: Segmentation violation possible for some Simscape models containing illegal configurations
Description: In some models containing state constraints (for example, masses or capacitors in parallel, or springs in series), the presence of an illegal configuration (for example, voltage sources in parallel or current sources in series) in the same model can result in a segmentation violation. Note that this only occurs in models that have an illegal configuration, so even if no segmentation fault occurs, simulating such models will normally result in an error.
Workaround: Try to simplify the circuit to avoid the illegal configuration. For more information, see the "Basic Errors in Physical System Representation" subsection of the "Troubleshooting Simulation Errors" section in the Simscape User's Guide.

Summary: The loading of certain MATLAB objects can fail or crash when those objects contain nested or anonymous function handles inside of property values and the class defines a saveobj or loadobj method.
Description: A bug in the load process for objects and nested functions causes failures or crashes when loading certain objects containing nested or anonymous functions and the class of those objects defines either a saveobj  or loadobj  method. The crash or failure is normally repeatable. If a given file loads without problems once, it generally always loads without problems.
Workaround: Work-arounds vary depending on the class of objects experiencing the problem. If nested or anonymous function handles do not need to be saved with an object (for example, if they are the same for all objects or depend solely on the values of other properties), then setting the Transient attribute for properties that hold nested or anonymous function handles avoids this bug.In some cases, avoiding access to the properties that contain the function handles from the saveobj and loadobj methods can also avoid the bug.

Summary: Communications Blockset scopes (Discrete Time Eye Diagram Scope, Discrete Time Signal Trajectory Scope and Discrete Time Scatter Plot Scope) stop running after a few time steps.
Description: Blocks that have multiple sample rates, such as the Buffer and Unbuffer blocks, can start missing sample hits for long-runnning simulations that use a variable-step solver. This is due to the small numerical skew that accumulates between the task times of the different sample rates. Because of this distortion from its true value, a model may encounter a case where its GCD (Greatest Common Divisor) rate does not have a sample hit even though one of its other rates has a hit. If the GCD rate does not have a hit, then Simulink assumes that none of the block rates have a hit and skips executing the block. The Communications Blockset scope blocks use the Buffer block so they are susceptible to this problem.
Workaround: You can avoid this bug by either switching your model to Accelerator mode or by using a fixed-step solver.

Summary: Performance regression in the waitbar function
Description: When you execute the following script in MATLAB, the time of execution is three times more in R2008a than what it was in R2007b. tic;h = waitbar(0,'Please wait...');for i=1:1000, % computation here %waitbar(i/1000)endclose(h)toc;  The elapsed time for this code is of the order of 21 seconds in R2008a. Elapsed time in R2007b is of the order of 7 seconds, indicating a 3x regression in performance.

Summary: Subsystem build process fails for a Stateflow linked library chart with machine-parented data
Description: The Real-Time Workshop subsystem build process fails for a Stateflow chart, under all of the following conditions: You start the subsystem build from a Stateflow linked library chart. The chart uses machine-parented data defined in the library model.
Workaround: Create a virtual subsystem containing the Stateflow linked library chart and generate code from the virtual subsystem.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB functions assert when calling polyfit, mldivide, mrdivide, or qr functions with odd-sized inputs
Description: Simulink or Real-Time Workshop may throw an assertion for models containing Embedded MATLAB functions that meet the following conditions: Call the function polyfit , mldivide , mrdivide , or qr  with odd-sized inputs.The bug is trigged by the following size combinations (where k  is a positive integer, and n  = 2*k +1): R = polyfit(ones(1,n),ones(1,n),k);  R = mldivide(ones(n,k+1),ones(n,1));  R = mrdivide(ones(1,n),ones(k+1,n));  [Q,R,P] = qr(ones(n,k+1),0);   (Inputs can have any value. The function ones  is used for illustration.) Get compiled with optimizations (for non-debug simulation or code generation with Real-Time Workshop)  Assertions caused by this bug contain the following message: CG::ReachingDefinitions::associateNumDefsWithStmts
Workaround: Change your algorithm to avoid the size combinations described above or patch your installation of MATLAB R2008a as follows:Use the ver command in MATLAB to check your version of MATLAB. This patch applies only to R2008a.Back-up $matlabroot$/toolbox/eml/lib/matlab/eml/eml_zlaqp2.m.Replace $matlabroot$/toolbox/eml/lib/matlab/eml/eml_zlaqp2.m with the version in the attached zip file attachment attachment_470596_20090422.zip.Rebuild the model.

Summary: Simulation error after copying the Differential block from Simulink Library Browser
Description: In the R2008a version of SimDriveline, simulating a model containing a Differential block that was copied from the Simulink Library Browser produces this error message: Gear block: 'Differential' needs to be updated to SimDriveline 1.1 Run drive_update('<model_name>') to update the model.
Workaround: There are three workarounds, each separately allowing you to use the Differential block. The third workaround is a permanent fix for the R2008a version of SimDriveline.1. Copy a Differential block from the Library Browser to your model. Then select that Differential block and, at the MATLAB command line, enter>> set_param(gcb,'SimDrivelineLibraryVersion','1.1')2. Avoid copying the Differential block from the Library Browser. Instead, open the Driveline block library by entering at the command line>> drivelineThen open the Gears sublibrary and copy the Differential block from there.3. To permanently fix this problem in the R2008a release, make a backup of the file$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/drive/drive/drive_differentialgearblock.pThen replace it with the drive_differentialgearblock.p from the attached file.

Summary: varargin returns incorrect values in an overloaded numel method of a class
Description: Due to a bug in the MATLAB Accelerator in MATLAB 7.6 (R2008a), varargin  returns erroneous information when used in an overloaded numel  method in a class definition.
Workaround: You may be able to work around this issue by turning off the Accelerator for the overloaded methods that reference varargin. To do this, add the following line at the beginning of the overload method:   %%%BEGIN CODE%%%   feature ('scopedAccelEnablement', 'off');   %%%END CODE%%%

Summary: Custom libraries fail to show up in the Library Browser
Description: If you select "Set installed files permissions to read-only " during installation of MATLAB and Simulink, the Library Browser does not display custom libraries.
Workaround: Set the permission of the following file to writable:cd ( [prefdir,filesep,'sl_library_browser_repos/toolbox/simulink/blocks'] );fileattrib('lbtoplevelmanifest.xml' ,'+w');

Summary: Publishing MATLAB Files: Output text missing when publishing to HTML
Description: If you use a Publish Configuration, the maxOutputLines  option is ignored when you set the output  option to HTML . Therefore, the HTML output may be truncated, or include more lines than you want.
Workaround: Rather than selecting "publish" in the Editor, use PUBLISH directly at the command line.  For example:>> publish introFor more info on PUBLISH, see the documentation:>> doc publish

Summary: imread gives misleading results when trying to read JPEG images with CMYK colorspace.
Description: imread  supports only RGB and grayscale JPEG images. JPEG images with the CMYK colorspace are not supported. imread  appears to read such images, but returns incorrect results.
Workaround: Installation instructions?????? Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.?????? Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.?????? Exit MATLAB.?????? Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.?????? Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: ????????????? ???????????????????? Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>????????????? ?????? ?????? Extract the files from the .zip file. ????????????? ???????????????????? Windows Systems:?????????????????????????? ????????????????????????????????? Open the .zip file.????????????????????????????????? Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.?????????????????????????? ???????????????????? ???????????????????? Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): ?????????????????????????? cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>???????????????????? ????????????? ?????? ?????? Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Misleading ERT code generation error message when Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder software not installed
Description: If you have a license for Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder but have not installed the software, attempting to generate code for an ERT based target should fail with an error message that mentions the software installation issue. Instead, code generation fails with a misleading error message similar to the following: Unable to locate a license for Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder. The current target is: matlabroot /rtw/c/ert/ert.tlc where matlabroot   represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the matlabroot  command.
Workaround: Before generating code for an ERT based target, install the Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder software.

Summary: An error can result if you attempt to access an inherited property with Protected access from a base class method using set.
Description: For a call to set  of the form: set(object,propname,newvalue) where object  is an instance of a MATLAB user-defined class, the set  method might incorrectly indicate that the property is not settable if the following conditions apply: 1. The property (propname ) is defined in a base class as a Protected  property. 2. The base class is derived from hgsetget . 3. A base class method calls the set  method. 4. The object is an instance of a class derived from the base class.
Workaround: Access the property using regular property access notation (i.e., dot-notation):object.propname = newvalue;

Summary: #define preprocessor symbols to enable model callbacks in S-Functions.
Description: When an S-Function defines a callback function such as mdlSetOutputPortDataType(), it should also define a corresponding preprocessor symbol such as MDL_SET_OUTPUT_PORT_DATA_TYPE. If the symbol is not defined, Simulink executes the default behavior instead of the user-written callback function. Before R2008b, the documentation did not mention that it is necessary to define the preprocessor symbols. The corresponding preprocessor symbols and callback functions, as found in simulink.c, are: MDL_GET_INPUT_PORT_WIDTH, mdlGetInputPortWidth() MDL_GET_OUTPUT_PORT_WIDTH, mdlGetOutputPortWidth() MDL_INITIALIZE_PROPAGATION_PASS, mdlInitializePropagationPass() MDL_SET_INPUT_PORT_WIDTH, mdlSetInputPortWidth() MDL_SET_OUTPUT_PORT_WIDTH, mdlSetOutputPortWidth()MDL_SET_INPUT_PORT_DIMENSION_INFO, mdlSetInputPortDimensionInfo()MDL_SET_OUTPUT_PORT_DIMENSION_INFO, mdlSetOutputPortDimensionInfo()MDL_SET_DEFAULT_PORT_DIMENSION_INFO, mdlSetDefaultPortDimensionInfo() MDL_SET_INPUT_PORT_SAMPLE_TIME, mdlSetInputPortSampleTime() MDL_SET_OUTPUT_PORT_SAMPLE_TIME, mdlSetOutputPortSampleTime()MDL_SET_INPUT_PORT_DATA_TYPE, mdlSetInputPortDataType() MDL_SET_OUTPUT_PORT_DATA_TYPE, mdlSetOutputPortDataType() MDL_SET_DEFAULT_PORT_DATA_TYPES, mdlSetDefaultPortDataTypes() MDL_SET_INPUT_PORT_COMPLEX_SIGNAL, mdlSetInputPortComplexSignal() MDL_SET_OUTPUT_PORT_COMPLEX_SIGNAL, mdlSetOutputPortComplexSignal() MDL_SET_DEFAULT_PORT_COMPLEX_SIGNALS, mdlSetDefaultPortComplexSignals() MDL_SET_INPUT_PORT_FRAME_DATA, mdlSetInputPortFrameData() MDL_SET_WORK_WIDTHS, mdlSetWorkWidths() MDL_RTW, mdlRTW() MDL_HAVESIMULATIONCONTEXTIO, mdlSimulationContextIO() MDL_INITIALIZE_CONDITIONS, mdlInitializeConditions() MDL_START, mdlStart()MDL_ENABLE, mdlEnable() MDL_DISABLE, mdlDisable MDL_CHECK_PARAMETERS, mdlCheckParameters() MDL_PROCESS_PARAMETERS, mdlProcessParameters() MDL_SIM_STATUS_CHANGE, mdlSimStatusChange() MDL_UPDATE, mdlUpdate() MDL_DERIVATIVES, mdlDerivatives()MDL_PROJECTION, mdlProjection()
Workaround: When you define a callback function, also define the corresponding preprocessor symbol, as shown in matlabroot/simulink/src/sfuntmpl_doc.c. For example, if your S-Functions defines mdlSetOutputPortDataType(), also define MDL_SET_OUTPUT_PORT_DATA_TYPE.?

Summary: imshow does not honor 'Parent' property when target figure has 'NextPlot' property set to 'New'.
Description: imshow  does not honor the 'Parent ' property used to specify the axes in which to display an image when the 'NextPlot'  property of the axes ancestor is 'New' . Instead, the image specified to imshow  is displayed in a new figure/axes, as in the following example: h_fig = figure('NextPlot','New');h_axes = axes; imshow('pout.tif','Parent',h_axes);  Note that the image is not displayed in h_fig.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: code generation for multitasking model may incorrectly apply for-loop fusion optimization to multitask protection code
Description: During code generation, the Real-Time Workshop software selectively applies for -loop fusion optimizations, such as combining code for multiple blocks into the same for -loop, improving code efficiency and readability. (For a demonstration of the capability, see the demo model rtwdemo_forloop .) However, code generation for a multitasking model may inappropriately apply the fusion optimization to a for -loop in which a global variable accessed between two tasks is used to control a critical code section, thus potentially removing the multitask protection. For example, in the following code, global flag semaphoreTaken  is set to 1  to protect resources used by Code body 1 , such as shared memory, from being modified by code in another thread while the for -loop containing Code body 1  is executing. semaphoreTaken = 1 for(i=1; i<N; i++) {/* Code body 1 */...}semaphoreTaken = 0for(i=1; i<N; i++) {/* Code body 2 */...} However, after for -loop fusion optimization, the two for -loops may be combined in such a way that Code body 1  loses its semaphore protection. For example: semaphoreTaken = 1semaphoreTaken = 0for(i=1; i<N; i++) {/* Code body 1 */.../* Code body 2 */...} This might allow shared memory protected by the semaphore to be modified from another thread while Code body 1  is executing, potentially resulting in data corruption. This bug can cause incorrect code to be generated for the Rate Transition block, if all of the following conditions are true: Rate Transition block option Ensure data integrity during data transfer  is selected. Rate Transition block option Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay)  is cleared. The input port has a faster sample time or a higher priority than the output port. The input signal is a vector (signal width is greater than 1). The model contains at least one other signal with the same width, such that the for -loop fusion optimization can occur.
Workaround: To work around this bug, you can select the Rate Transition block option Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay), which causes the generated code for the Rate Transition block to use an alternative data transfer approach that is not adversely affected by the for-loop fusion optimization. Note that selecting this option generates code that transfers data at the sample rate of the slower of the two blocks connected to the Rate Transition block, which can alter model behavior.

Summary: Wavelet Coefficients Selection 1-D GUI Tool produces different results in different versions of the toolbox
Description: If you use the Manual Selection Method in the Wavelet Coefficients Selection 1-D GUI, the results may differ depending on which version of the Wavelets Toolbox you are using. In particular, the results in R2007b do not match the data as well as they did in versions R2006b and R2007a.
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the cf1dtool.m file in your $ROOT\toolbox\wavelet\wavelet directory with one of the files in the Attachments section below:- For R2007b, replace cf1dtool.m with the file cf1dtool_R2007b.m - For R2008a, replace cf1dtool.m with the file cf1dtool_R2008a.m.Then, rename the downloaded file to cf1dtool.m

Summary: When initializing the zcEvent vector in generated Real Time Code, a basic data type declaration 'int' may be generated.
Description: When initializing the zcEvent vector in generated Real Time Code, a basic data type declaration 'int' is used to defined a loop variable when the length of the zcEvent is longer than the RollThreshold. The correct code would use the default Real Time Workshop alias 'int_T' to define the roll variable.
Workaround: The user can increase the "Loop unrolling threshold" in the Configuration Parameters Optimization pane to avoid generating the roll variable and thus avoid this bug.

Summary: Plot Browser does not show all graphics in a figure containing subplots in MATLAB 7.6 (R2008a)
Description: The Plot Browser sometimes does not show the entire contents of axes in a figure containing subplots. This is an intermittent problem and has been reported only for MATLAB 7.6 It does not seem to occur in later releases of MATLAB.
Workaround: Saving the figure and reopening it can sometimes eliminate the symptoms. Figures are not corrupted by the problem.

Summary: for-loop iteration over embedded.fi objects in Embedded MATLAB functions produces wrong answers
Description: Embedded MATLAB functions do not support for -loop iteration over embedded.fi  objects. When this condition exists, Embedded MATLAB does not detect it at compile time, causing it to generate incorrect code. As a result, your function produces wrong answers, or MATLAB may hang or crash when you try to simulate the model that contains the function.
Workaround: Do not use for-loop iteration over embedded.fi objects in Embedded MATLAB functions.

Summary: Linearization of Simulink models using root-level inports/outport blocks returns incorrect input/output labels when inport/outport properties are specified via bus object.
Description: When a Simulink model is linearized without specifying linearization I/O points, i.e., using root-level inport and outport blocks, as in the following syntax: lin=linearize('sys',op)  the linearization result has incorrect input/output labels if the option "Specify properties via bus object"  is checked for any root-level inport/outport block.
Workaround: Specify root-level inports as linerization input points and root-level outports as lineariazation output points and linearize the system using specified linearization I/O points as in the following syntax:lin=linearize('sys',op,io);Note that one can obtain the specified linearization I/O points in a model by using the GETLINIO function, as in the following command:io = getlinio('sys');

Summary: Incorrect linearization results for models with non-virtual buses with direct connections between root level inport/outports
Description: Using the dlinmod  or linearize  commands to linearize a Simulink? model with root level inport and outport blocks may return incorrect results when the following conditions are true: The inport directly connects to the outport block. The inport block is defined with a non-virtual bus.
Workaround: Eliminate the direct connection between the inport and outport.

Summary: MATLAB might crash during code generation for a model containing a data store with a data type defined by a Simulink.StructType object
Description: MATLAB might crash when Real-Time Workshop generates code for a model that contains a data store that defines: The data type using a Simulink.StructType  object. State properties using a Simulink.Signal  object with initial value 0 .
Workaround: Specify [] as the initial value for the Simulink.Signal object. The generated code is the same as when you specify 0 for the initial value.

Summary: Models with algebraic loops that are driven by atomic subsystems may cause a SEGV during an update diagram.
Description: This bug occurs in the following situation: Let B1 be a block in an algebraic loop that is driven by an atomic subsystem, AS1. Let B2 be the block in the atomic subsystem that drives B1. If the index of the output port of B2 that drives B1 is equal to or greater than the total number of output ports on AS1, a SEGV may occur during an update diagram.
Workaround: For the situation described in the bug, a workaround is to add a Signal Conversation block between block B2 and the output port of AS1, such that the Signal Conversion block is inside AS1.In the attached model, example.mdl: B1 is the "example/Switch"; AS1 is the "example/Atomic Subsystem"; and B2 is "example/Atomic Subsystem/Embedded MATLAB Function". Thus, adding a Signal Conversion block between "example/Atomic Subsystem/Embedded MATLAB Function" and "example/Atomic Subsystem/Out2" will serve as a workaround.

Summary: Reloading a model that contains blocks for the General Standards 24DSI12 and 16AO16 boards might crash
Description: If you download a model that contains blocks for either or both of these boards more than once without rebooting the target PC, you will likely crash the target PC when you try to run the model.
Workaround: To work around this issue:Stop MATLAB.Download the attached files dags16ao16.c and adgs24dsi12.c and save them to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks.Restart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.

Summary: Problem trimming polygons with sparsely-placed vertices
Description: For polygons with sparsely-placed vertices, polygon trimming in R2007b and R2008a for projections with quadrangular map frames can introduce interpolated vertices with incorrect latitudes. In addition to appearing in displays or computations via functions geoshow , patchm , or maptrimp , this problem may occur when the 'FFill' property of a map axes is set to an exceptionally small value.

Summary: Spectrum Scope block stops running after a few time steps
Description: Blocks that have multiple sample rates, such as the Buffer and Unbuffer blocks, can start missing sample hits for long-runnning simulations that use a variable-step solver. This is due to a small numerical skew that accumulates between the task times of the different sample rates. Because of this distortion from its true value, a model may encounter a case where its GCD (Greatest Common Divisor) rate does not have a sample hit even though one of its other rates has a hit. If the GCD rate does not have a hit, then Simulink assumes that none of the block rates have a hit and skips executing the block. The Spectrum Scope block uses the Buffer block, so it is susceptible to this problem.
Workaround: You can avoid this issue by switching your model to Accelerator mode or by using a fixed-step solver.

Summary: Custom code settings in RTW options are not passed to PolySpace
Description: The custom code settings in RTW Embedded Coder options can be used to specify include files, include paths, and extra source files. All of these affect the verification of the generated source code. The PolySpace model link block does not pick up the include paths when it launches a verification. This results in compilation failure if the generated source includes a file in the specified [and ignored] include path. The model link does not pick up additional source files. This results in stubs being used in the verification for code specified in the RTW custom code options. This can be avoided by adding the source again as an additional source in the PolySpace block dialog.
Workaround: Unzip the attached file in <PolySpace_Install_Directory>/PolySpace_Common/PolySpaceModelLink

Summary: Integrator block and Transport Delay block may cause discrepancy of results under certain conditions.
Description: Simulation results may differ from code generation results for models having specific conditions: an Integrator block with a reset port selected; or a Transport Delay block with a buffer of fixed size and having a full state during simulation. The cause of the results discrepancy is that the block (Integrator or Transport Delay) may request a block state change during simulation but not during code generation. The flag generated under these circumstances is not accessible to the user.
Workaround: For releases prior to R2008b, there is no workaround for RSim/Rapid Accelerator mode. For releases prior to R2009a, there is no workaround for GRT/ERT with continuous states.

Summary: Changing the colormap or the axes CLim property may change a colorbar's orientation or axes tick location and labeling
Description: This issue occurs when you set a colorbar's location manually. Create a colorbar and specify its position. cbar = colorbar('Position',[.1 .1 .8 .1] Notice that setting the colorbar's Position  sets its Location  to manual. get(cbar,'Location')ans =manual Examine the YAxisLocation  of the colorbar get(cbar,'YAxisLocation')ans = right Now switch to the cool  colormap. The y-axis of the colorbar has moved from the right side of the colorbar to the left. If you examine the YAxisLocation  property again you will see it has changed. colormap(cool)get(cbar,'YAxisLocation')ans = left

Summary: The CTF file for deployed applications includes unnecessary files from WebRenderer/Cache
Description: mcc  is including any WebRenderer content files cached in the WebRenderer/Cache area of the user's preferences directory in the deployment CTF archive (prefdir ). As this content is not available to deployed applications, its inclusion is unnecessary and needlessly increases the size of the CTF.
Workaround: As a workaround, delete the WebRenderer/Cache files from prefdir prior to compiling an application for deployment. Since the name of this directory appears with different capitaliztion on various platforms, the attached program WRclean.m can be run, which deletes the correct directory, if present. The function runs silently if the directory is successfully deleted or was not present, and generates error output only if there is an error. To run WRclean.m, save it to a directory on the MATLAB path and invoke WRclean at the MATLAB command prompt.

Summary: On Windows, Update Current License and Deactivate Current License may fail if HOSTID in License File uses computer's MAC address
Description: On Windows, standalone license files can be locked to the computer's MAC address or disk serial number. If the license was activated from the activation application, the disk serial number is used by default. If the license was activated from the License Center, the user may enter either the disk serial number or the MAC address. If the activated License File is locked to the MAC address, the license update or deactivate process may not be able to complete successfully, and will generate an error message that a valid License File cannot be found.
Workaround: The license update and deactivate processes can be successfully completed in the License Center.

Summary: The netlist2sl function generates errors on Windows?Operating systems with Chinese locale
Description: When using a Windows?Operating system with the Chinese locale Regional setting, the SimElectronics? netlist2sl  function generates errors. The error message includes a statement similar to '... is not numeric in line ...'.
Workaround: To work around this issue, a downloadable fix is available.  1. Get the name of your MATLAB?installation directory by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command line.2. Exit MATLAB. 3. Summary: Simulink incorrectly prints print frames containing Japanese text on Japanese systems
Description: When printed on Japanese systems, Japanese text appears garbled in Simulink model print frames.

Summary: ldl function generates invalid sparse matrix
Description: The ldl  function may generate an invalid sparse matrix as its first output. The row indices of the output may not be not sorted within the columns of the sparse matrix. As many algorithms for sparse matrix computations in MATLAB? exploit the fact that valid MATLAB? sparse matrices have row indices in ascending order, computations with invalid matrices can result in incorrect results. A = delsq(numgrid('L', 20));         % A is a symmetric matrix [L, D, P] = ldl(A);                  % L has unsorted row indices.disp(norm(L*D*L' - P'*A*P, 'fro'));  % Larger than expected as L is invalid. ?
Workaround: Make the first output of ldl valid by performing some operation that sorts the row indices of the sparse matrix. Do this explicitly by calling find.[i, j, s] = find(L);L = sparse(i, j, s, size(L,1), size(L,2));Do this implicitly by performing two transpose operations.L = (L.').';

Summary: Build process failure when generating code for model with Bus Selector block
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail for a model containing a Bus Selector block if the Reuse block outputs  check box is selected in the Configuration Parameters > Optimization pane. The build process reports the failure message in the MATLAB Command Window similar to the following: CGIR assertion in sl_engin/rtwcg_codegen_create.cpp(1109): 'rtwCtx->getDataConnector(bussel_output) == 0'   [0] libmwsimulink.so:assertion_func(void*, char const*, int, char const*)() + 67 bytes   [1] libmwsimulink.so:RTWCGCodeGeneration::createRTWOpaqueBlock()() + 12580 bytes ...
Workaround: To generate correct code for the Bus Selector block:In the Configuration Parameters > Optimization pane, clear the Reuse block outputs check box.Regenerate the code for your model.

Summary: mbuild fails on non-Windows platforms when source file paths contain one or more spaces.
Description: mbuild  fails on non-Windows platforms when source file paths contain one or more spaces. On non-Windows platforms, mbuild  is implemented as a shell script. mbuild  is used to compile and link C and C++ sources used in the creation of standalone applications. Depending on usage, it may be invoked by mcc  or directly by the user. Using deploytool  from within MATLAB may also result in the invocation of mbuild . If mbuild  is passed source file paths containing one or more spaces, it fails, indicating that the source file cannot be found.
Workaround: Do not place the source file in directories where the path contains one or more spaces.

This issue only occurs on Windows systems running R2008a.

Summary: While BERTool is running a Monte Carlo simulation, clicking on a BERTool figure plot generates an exception error message.
Description: BERTool provides a link between the BER plots in the figure and the table in the GUI window, where all the BER results are listed. If the user clicks on a BER point in the figure, the corresponsing row of the table is highlighted, and vice versa. BERTool updates the figure point-by-point during a Monte Carlo simulation. Summary: Segmentation violation may occur when rate transition auto-insertion attempts unsupported auto-insertion at virtual block
Description: If you select the option Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  for your model, Simulink software automatically inserts hidden Rate Transition blocks between blocks that have different sample rates. However, rate transition auto-insertion does not support block insertion at virtual blocks, such as Bus Creator and Bus Selector. If your model contains a Bus Creator or Bus Selector block, and if inserting a Rate Transition block at the virtual block is the only way to resolve a rate transition without rewiring blocks, Simulink software may insert the Rate Transition block, but will not correctly set the sample time of the inserted block. This causes a segmentation violation during model simulation.
Workaround: To work around this bug, try to avoid branching a signal from an asynchronous subsystem to a virtual block. If the branching is unavoidable, manually insert a Rate Transition block at the virtual block to resolve the rate transition.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: generated code results for Bus Selector block does not match simulation results
Description: Real-Time Workshop and Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder software might generate incorrect code if all of the following apply to your model: The model has a Stateflow chart that contains a bus output signal. The Stateflow chart has exported graphical functions that write to the bus output signal. The bus output signal of the Stateflow chart is directly connected to a Bus Selector block  For example, the attached model gives different simulation and code generation results. The simulation result is correct while the code generation result is wrong.
Workaround: Make the output of the Bus Selector block a test point.

Summary: Segmentation Violation in a GUI at IsMCallbackExecutable in MATLAB 7.5 (R2007b)
Description: After deleting a figure, MATLAB code attempted to delete a callback which had already been deleted.

Summary: Model build within script hangs if directory contains build from earlier version of Simulink
Description: If you interactively initiate a model build in a directory that contains a build from an earlier version of Simulink software, a dialog box alerts you to the version mismatch, and asks you whether to Remove slprj and continue  or Abort . If the same model build is initiated from a script, the dialog box does not appear, but progress is halted while the build command waits for the dialog box response.
Workaround: When upgrading Simulink software, it is required to use a clean build directory. To work around this bug, verify that your build directory is clean before initiating a model build in a script.

Summary: Changing XData, YData and ZData properties of a STEM3 plot may cause MATLAB error
Description: Execute the following code: h = stem3( 1:10, 1:10, rand(1,10));set(h, 'XData', 1:20, 'YData', 1:20, 'ZData', 1:20);  MATLAB throws the following error: ??? In an assignment A(I) = B, the number of elements in B and  I must be the same.  ?

Summary: Downloading a verification through the PolySpace Spooler fails, and the software displays a fork/spawn error message
Description: When trying to download a 'completed ' verification through the PolySpace Spooler, a popup window displays the following message: "Error: Unable to fork/spawn. Please check your memory and swap amount.Error: Could not uncompress the results archive."  This occurs if the 'PolySpace Spooler ' shortcut on the desktop PC does not point to the correct executable.
Workaround: To workaround this issue:Right-click the PolySpace Spooler icon on your desktop, then select Properties.Select the Shortcut tab.In the Target field, replace the existing text:<PolySpace_installation_directory>\PolySpace_Common\RemoteLauncher\bin\PSQueueSpooler.exewith the following:<PolySpace_installation_directory>\PolySpace_Common\RemoteLauncher\wbin\PSQueueSpooler.exe

Summary: imwrite does not support creating GIF files from RGB images
Description: When writing GIF files, the imwrite  function requires an indexed image or a grayscale image. RGB images are not supported. Confusion may arise when reading the imwrite  help text. Only M-by-N-by-1 image arrays (or M-by-N-by-1-by-P multiframe image arrays) are allowed when writing GIF images.
Workaround: Users of the Image Processing Toolbox can use the rgb2ind function to convert RGB images to M-by-N indexed images.

Summary: Target function library fixed-point multiplication or division operator replacement, when using relative scaling factor F and E values 1.0 and 0.0, does not work as expected
Description: In target function library (TFL) table entries for multiplication or division operator replacement, the relative scaling factor F/E parameters used to map a range of fixed-point multiplication or division operations to a single TFL entry (provided that the CheckSlope  and CheckBias  properties for the arguments being matched equal false ) are required to have values other than RelativeScalingFactorF =1.0 and RelativeScalingFactorE =0.0. However, this requirement is not stated in the documentation for TFL multiplication and division operator replacement. Using RelativeScalingFactorF =1.0 and RelativeScalingFactorE =0.0 in a TFL multiplication or division operator replacement entry does not work as expected. In the resulting mapping, all input and output slopes are ignored and the mapping of multiplication or division operators to the TFL entry is based on argument data type sizes and signs only.
Workaround: To work around this bug, do not use RelativeScalingFactorF=1.0 and RelativeScalingFactorE=0.0 (in conjunction with setting conceptual argument properties CheckSlope and CheckBias to false) when defining multiplication or division operator replacement entries. Instead, specify the exact slopes and biases required for each operator input and output. This will require a one-to-one mapping between each multiplication or division operation and a TFL entry, rather than many-to-one mapping.

Summary: Certain types of block comments in MATLAB files may cause errors when compiled by MATLAB Compiler
Description: Block comments placed at the very beginning of an MATLAB file, as illustrated below, may cause the MATLAB Compiler to fail with a memory protection error. %{any comment%}function myFcndisp 'hello'
Workaround: To work around this problem, move the block comment to later in the file, for example:function myFcn%{any comment%}disp 'hello'

Summary: Customer derived numeric-type object errors out in function getdatatypespecs.m
Description: Users can derive their own numeric-type object from Simulink.Numeric and use it in the model. Some Simulink blocks, for example the Satuation Dynamics, call the function getdatatypespecs from within an S-function in order to parse the data-type information from the numeric-type object. The function does not recognize the derived numeric-type objects, and therefore the blocks throw an error.

Summary: Relational Operator block: simulation and code generation results might be different if the output data type is not boolean
Description: If the output of a Relational Operator block is a fixed-point data type, code generation might produce different results from simulation. For example, this can occur if you use "fixdt(1,25,-1)" for Output data type  in the Signal Attributes pane of the Relational Operator block dialog box.
Workaround: In the Signal Attributes pane of the Relational Operator block dialog box, select "boolean" from the drop-down list for Output data type. Then use a Data Type Conversion block to convert the output data type from boolean to the expected data type.

Summary: Continuous Wavelet 1-D and Complex Continuous Wavelet 1-D GUIs Analyze button not visible when resolution is 1440 x 900
Description: The Analyze button may be hidden under the "New Coefficients Line" and "Refresh Maxima Lines" buttons in the Continuous Wavelet 1-D and Complex Continuous Wavelet 1-D GUIs if the resolution is set to 1440x900.
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the current mextglob.m, wtbutils.m , cw1dtool.m and cwimtool.m files in your $ROOT\toolbox\wavelet\wavelet directory with the files in the Attachments section below.

Summary: PolySpace verification of a multi tasking application stops when you use DRS (Data Range Specification) on arrays (in permanent mode).
Description: This problem occurs under the following conditions: A DRS file is used in the project DRS permanent mode is specified for an array Entry points are declared  The verification stops during the IL Compilation phase. The error message appears as follows: Verifying a0 -> Verifier found an error in a0.adb:<line>:<colum>: expected type "T16" defined at t.ads:<line> -> Verifier found an error in a0.adb:<line>:<column>: found type "Trange2" defined at t.ads:<line> ?

Summary: nitfinfo returns error 'Undefined function or variable fid' with a file containing DES

Summary: Calling a function with multiple output arguments may be slower when one of the outputs is subscripted.
Description: In some instances, calling a function with multiple outputs where one output is subscripted may be significantly slower than calling the same function without subscripts on the outputs. This behavior only occurs if one of the input and output variables have the same name. For example: [x, y(1)] = myfunction(x);  may be slower than: [x, y] = myfunction(x);  The interpreter has an optimization that allows it to reuse the memory from a variable when it appears as both a function argument and the left hand side of an assignment. The performance difference occurs because this optimization is not being correctly applied when one of the output arguments is subscripted.
Workaround: Use a temporary variable to avoid subscripts on the output. For example:[x, tmp] = myfunction(x);y(1) = tmp;

Summary: OR block with one non-boolean input may give wrong answer
Description: For some non-boolean input/output data types, an OR block with one input and block reduction selected may give a wrong answer for both simulation and code generation. If block reduction is not selected, only simulation produces a wrong answer for this type of input. This bug also applies to AND blocks.
Workaround: Convert input signal to boolean, and then feed the converted signal into the OR block.

Summary: Keypress callback does not get executed after a single line edit box is converted to a multiline edit box
Description: When a single line edit box is converted into a multiline edit box, the keypress function stops responding. The following code reproduces the issue: fig2 = figure('name','Error: keypressfcn does not execute.');editH2 = uicontrol('parent',fig2,...          'style','edit',...          'units','normalized',...          'position',[0.25 0.25 0.75 0.75]); set(editH2,'max',2); set(editH2,'keypressfcn','disp(''fig2 keypressed'')');
Workaround: The issue does not arise if you set the multiline property of the edit box while creating the control.fig1 = figure('name','OK: keypressfcn does execute'); editH2 = uicontrol('parent',fig1,...          'style','edit',...          'units','normalized', ...          'position',[0.25 0.25 0.75 0.75], ...          'max',2, ...          'keypressfcn','disp(''fig1 keypressed'')');?

Summary: The Enable zero crossing detection parameter of the Compare To Constant block remains greyed out when the solver changes from fixed-step to variable-step
Description: Variable-step solvers support zero-crossing detection, but fixed-step solvers do not. The Enable zero crossing detection  parameter of any block that can provide zero-crossing detection should therefore be greyed out (unavailable) when the solver is fixed-step, and available when the solver is variable-step. However, for the Compare To Constant block, the Enable zero crossing detection  parameter remains greyed out when the solver changes from fixed step to variable-step.

Summary: The isa function may return incorrect result for MCOS classes.
Description: The isa function may return an incorrect result if used on the result of a cell array, structure index, or MATLAB function call.Note that this only occurs if the indexing operation or MATLAB function returned an object of a superclass first. For example,    classdef parent    end    classdef child < parent    end    function x = foo(n)    if n == 1       x = parent;    else       x = child;    end  >> a{1} = parent; >> a{2} = child; >> isa(a{1}, 'parent')         % This must happen first.>> isa(a{2}, 'child')          % Should be true.>> isa(foo(1), 'parent')       % This must happen first.>> isa(foo(2), 'child')        % Should be true.
Workaround: Assign the result of the indexing operation or MATLAB function to a temporary variable before using isa, e.g.>> x = a{1};>> isa(x, 'parent')>> x = a{2};>> isa(x, 'child')Or, turn off jitting of MCOS by using "feature jitallow mcos off".

Summary: Generated code fails to compile when using data type replacements in conjunction with AUTOSAR target
Description: The Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder generated code fails to compile if all of the following conditions apply to your model: The model is configured to use an AUTOSAR system target file. The code generator creates the ERT S-function wrapper. The generated code replaces built-in data type names with user-defined replacement data type names.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB Function blocks leak memory when calling extrinsic MATLAB functions with multiple outputs where some outputs are not used.
Description: When Embedded MATLAB Function blocks call unsupported MATLAB functions using the eml.extrinsic  notation, memory leaks may occur in the following circumstances: If the MATLAB function has multiple outputs One or more of these outputs are not used by the Embedded MATLAB Function block code  Leaks occur because memory is not freed for unused data, causing memory allocation to grow each time you run the model.
Workaround: In the Embedded MATLAB Function block, add code to use all outputs.

Summary: The CDF library has a buffer overflow vulnerability.
Description: The libraries for the scientific data file format, Common Data Format (CDF) version 3.2 and earlier, have the potential for a buffer overflow vulnerability when reading specially-crafted (invalid) CDF files. If successful, this could trigger execution of arbitrary code within the context of the CDF-reading program that could be exploited to compromise a system, or otherwise crash the program. Further information on the vulnerability can be found at http://cdf.gsfc.nasa.gov/CDF32_buffer_overflow.html The vulnerability exists in versions 3.2 and earlier of the CDF library. MATLAB version R2008a is based on version 3.1, so all prior versions of MATLAB are vulnerable.

Summary: Simulink model using analog output block and National Instruments device reports error with parallel port
Description: Running a Simulink model that contains an analog output block, using a National Instruments device, can produce one of the following parallel port warnings and errors, even when the model does not include a parallel port: Warning: Unable to load hardware information for parallel adaptor. Make sure no other program is accessing this adaptor.  or Error reported by S-function 'sdaqao'.Could not create an analog output subsystem.PARALLEL:Device is already in use.  or Error reported by S-function 'sdaqao'.Could not create an analog output subsystem.PARALLEL:Could not start the WINIO.SYS device driver.  This problem is due to a hardware limitation of the National Instruments device. We have encountered this problem in certain National Instruments devices (see the list below), but there may be others. The problem occurs because these devices require a minimum number of samples be written at each time step. If less than the specified minimum number of samples is written to the device, MATLAB will eventually stop responding. If you end the MATLAB session using the Windows Task Manager, the "End Task" command closes the MATLAB window, but the MATLAB process continues to run. If you then open another MATLAB session, you receive the parallel port errors. To clear these error messages, you must restart your computer. National Instruments Devices with Hardware Limitations: PCI-6115PCI-6221PCI-6229PCI-6251PCI-6259PCI-6281PCI-6289PCI-6713PCI-MIO-16XE-10USB-6211USB-6215
Workaround: To work around this problem, modify your Simulink model so that it writes at least the minimum number of samples required by the device at each time step.To find the minimum number of samples required by a National Instruments device, run the following MATLAB commands:>> ao = analogoutput('nidaq', <device ID>) % Substitute the ID of the device for <device ID>>> addchannel(ao,0)>> putdata(ao,1)>> start(ao)Running the start command produces an error message that contains the minimum number of samples that the device requires.If you must queue less than the required minimum number of samples, consider using the Analog Output (Single Sample) block available beginning with the Data Acquisition Toolbox Version 2.12 (R2008a). ?
