Summary: Data corruption might occur with some CAN blocks in xPC Target library
Description: If an xPC Target CAN block sends or receives data that has a pattern of a double precision NAN values, the block might modify that data unintentionally. This issue affects a small subset of possible data patterns. This issue affects the PCI and PC104 xPC Target CAN FIFO send and receive blocks.
Workaround: In some cases it may be possible to work around the problem. For further information, contact MathWorks Technical Support.

Summary: fmincon interior-point sometimes errors when I set a non-default value for option TypicalX
Description: When I set a non-default value for option TypicalX  in the interior-point algorithm of fmincon , I get a size mismatch error unless the TypicalX  value is a column vector.
Workaround: Set TypicalX to a column vector. For example, instead of setting TypicalX to the row vector [2 100 0.01], set it to the column vector [2; 100; 0.01].

Summary: configuremodelsim.m errors out on 64bit Solaris and 64bit Linux machines.
Description: The configuremodelsim.m  does not run on 64bit Solaris or 64bit Linux platforms.
Workaround: configuremodelsim.m is a convenience script used to set up a ModelSim installation for startup with the EDA Simulator Link MQ product in an OS shell. This script has been flagged to be deprecated. It is advised to instead use the vsim.m front-end to generate the necessary scripts for ModelSim startup outside of MATLAB. Please see the product documentation.

Summary: Build process failure produces CGIR assertion error message
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail to generate code and reports the following error message: CGIR assertion in rtwcg_utils/rtwcg_tag.cpp(1001): 'getNodeRTWCGTag(startNode) == getNodeRTWCGTag(endNode) && getNodeRTWCGTag(startNode) == rtwTag'  [0] libmwsimulink.so:assertion_func(void*, char const*, int, char const*)()  [1] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::RTWCGTagCtx::updateTagRegion(RTWCG::RTWCGNodeTag*)()  [2] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::RTWCGTagCtx::traverseUnstructuredCfg(CG_Node*)()  [3] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::RTWCGTagCtx::fcnRefreshTags(CG_Const*)()  [4] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::RTWCGRefreshTags(CG_Scope*)()
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog, clear the Simulink Block / Stateflow object comments check box in the Real-Time Workshop > Comments pane.?

Summary: Text in uicontrol edit text box can be clipped on Intel Mac in MATLAB 7.6 (R2008a)
Description: On an Intel Macintosh, text can be clipped in an edit text uicontrol. h = uicontrol('style','edit','string','abcdef');
Workaround: Set the uicontrol Position width (its third value) to be large enough to hold the string. For example,set(h, 'Position', [200 200 100 50])

Summary: Why am I unable to use my multi-tap camera with a DALSA? frame grabber and Image Acquisition Toolbox??
Description: When I attempt to use a multi-tap camera with a DALSA? frame grabber and Image Acquisition Toolbox? I see number of errors. The VideoResolution  and ROIPosition  properties of the videoinput  object only report half the expected width. When previewing or acquiring data, only half of the field of view is shown. Sometimes I receive the following warning message: Warning: The specified Width was not valid and has been adjusted to the nearest valid value.
Workaround: If possible, operate the camera in single-tap mode. If it is not possible to operate the camera in single-tap mode, some cameras will function by multiplying the actual width by the number of taps and then specifying that value for the width in the camera file while also setting the number of taps to one.

Summary: Simulink deselects an out-of-date library link when another model or MATLAB  editor window is minimized
Description: Simulink may deselect the current selection  if the selection includes a link to a library that needs to be updated and another model editor window or the MATLAB editor is minimized.

Summary: Java exception when selecting Help from the Colormap Editor's menu?
Description: A Java exception occurs when I select Colormap Editor help  from the Colormap Editor's Help  menu. The MATLAB Command Window displays Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.util.MissingResourceException: Can't find resource for bundle... ?

Summary: Incorrect code may be generated for Sum block with both signed and unsigned inputs
Description: If a Sum block has both signed and unsigned inputs, incorrect HDL code may be generated.
Workaround: Cast all the inputs of the Sum block to the same type (signed or unsigned). In addition, if the minus (-) operator is being used, make all the inputs signed.

Summary: Error during code generation for models with nested conditionally executed subsystems
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail for models with nested, conditionally executed subsystems, if any of the output signals of the subsystems reset on disable. The build process reports the failure as an error message in the MATLAB Command Window similar to the following: %closefile, %selectfile or %flushfile argument must be a valid open file
Workaround: Change the Output when disabled parameters of the subsystem Outport blocks to held.

Summary: Glyphs on legends for plots generated with plotyy do not have correct labels
Description: Create a plot using plotyy  and add a legend to each axes individually. AX = plotyy([1:10],rand(10,1),[1:10],rand(10,1));legend(AX(1),'left axes','Location','NorthWest')legend(AX(2),'right axes','Location','NorthEast') Examining the axes handles in AX  returned by plotyy , we see that AX(1)  is the left axes, which is blue, while AX(2)  is the right axes, which is green. get(AX(1),'YColor')get(AX(2),'YColor') However, the glyph in the legend for AX(1)  is green, while the glyph in the legend for AX(2)  is blue.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can call legend with the line objects from each axes explicitly. For example,[AX,H1,H2]=plotyy([1:10],rand(10,1),[1:10],rand(10,1));axes(AX(1))legend(H1,'left axes','Location','NorthWest')axes(AX(2))legend(H2,'right axes','Location','NorthEast')

Summary: Generated code for a model that is referenced in Normal mode and uses a global parameter may be uncompilable
Description: When Real-Time Workshop generates code for a model, the code may be uncompilable if all of the following are true: The model contains a Model block that is set to Normal mode. The referenced model has a function-call port at the root level. A block in the referenced model is using a Simulink.Parameter that has a global storage class (for example, SimulinkGlobal  or ExportedGlobal ).  No warning or error message appears during code generation, but trying to compile the generated code causes an error.

Summary: Generated ERT example main function may incorrectly report overrun if multirate multitasking model has only one subrate
Description: If you generate code for a multirate multitasking model that has only one subrate, the generated ERT example main function will incorrectly report an overrun error when the subrate task is preempted by the base rate task. Specifically, in the function rt_OneStep  in the generated ert_main.c  or .cpp , the following code if (OverrunFlags[1]) return; is missing before the code /* Step the model for subrate */  if (eventFlags[1]) {  ...}
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2008a, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the matlabroot command.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following files, for example, by adding .old to the end of each file name.matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/bareboard_mrmain.tlcmatlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/ertcppmainlib.tlcmatlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/ertmainlib.tlcDownload the attached files and place them at the same location as the files you renamed.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Performance regression in num2str.
Description: For matrices of real numbers, num2str  may show a significant performance slowdown from R2007b.
Workaround: The attached file, num2str.m, optimizes calls to num2str that have real number input. The optimization is limited to the single input version of num2str and the numeric precision second input version of num2str. Using the sprintf format second argument version of num2str is not improved by this change.

Summary: Model reference build generates error for model with no inputs or outputs
Description: The model reference build process generates an error for a model with no inputs or outputs when the model utilizes function prototype control. The error is similar to the following: Error using ==> tlc_new Error: File: C:\matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\codeinfomdlreflib.tlc Line: 119 Column: 28 Arguments to ISFIELD must be records

Summary: The frame rate for a multiple hardware trigger single-frame per trigger acquisition is slow when using QImaging cameras.
Description: When acquiring images with QImaging cameras using hardware triggering and acquiring a single-frame per trigger, the maximum rate at which capture can occur is slower than it should be. For example, for the following code: vid=videoinput('qimaging'); vid.TriggerRepeat = 19; vid.FramesPerTrigger = 1; triggerconfig(vid,'hardware','risingEdge','TTL'); start(vid); while isrunning(vid)   pause(.0001); end [d t]=getdata(vid,20); hardwareTriggerFrameRate=1/mean(diff(t))  while applying a 1 kHz square wave for a hardware trigger, I find that the frame rate is about half of what I can achieve using manual triggering and the code below: vid=videoinput('qimaging'); vid.TriggerRepeat = 19; vid.FramesPerTrigger = 1; triggerconfig(vid,'Manual'); vid.FramesAcquiredFcnCount = 1; vid.FramesAcquiredFcn = @(varargin)trigger(vid); start(vid); trigger(vid); while isrunning(vid)  pause(.0001); end [d t]=getdata(vid,20); manualTriggerFrameRate=1/mean(diff(t))

Summary: The commcdma2000 demo gives wrong, nonzero BER results when the model has no channel impairments.
Description: When the demo is configured to have no channel (i.e. no impairments of any kind), the BER at the output of the receiver is about 0.01. It should be 0. This occurs because certain power control bits overwrite some data bits, per the CDMA2000 standard. However, the model erroneously includes those overwritten bits when it calculates bit errors.

Summary: Generating code for a model that is referenced in Accelerator mode and uses a global parameter may cause a segmentation violation
Description: When Real-Time Workshop generates code for a model, a segmentation violation may occur if all of the following are true: The model contains a Model block that is set to Accelerator mode. The referenced model has a function-call port at the root level. A block in the referenced model uses a Simulink.Parameter  that has a global storage class (for example, SimulinkGlobal  or ExportedGlobal ).

Summary: Incorrect code generation: missing code for signals specified by custom storage class
Description: The Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder software might not generate code for signals in your model that are specified by custom storage classes.

Summary: Data leaks across uitables in a figure
Description: When a figure contains multiple editable uitables, data is copied from one table to another when editing a table.
Workaround: To avoid crosstalk between data in uitables, after you edit a table commit the changes to it before editing another table.If your GUI uses a table's CellEditCallback, note that the CellEditCallback does not fire if the user edits a cell and then clicks in another figure window or desktop tool. If the user then returns to the table to edit a different cell, the previous CellEditCallback still does not fire. Even though the table displays the results of the previous edit, the underlying data matrix (the table's Data property) is not updated to contain the value that appears in the cell.To save changes to a table you make using the Table Property Editor, click OK, or click Apply to commit changes and continue using the Table Property Editor.

Summary: VHDL code generated from the PN Sequence Generator block for non-ASIC/FPGA device types can get compilation errors
Description: When the Device vendor  and Device type  parameters on the Simulink Hardware Implementation page are not set to 'ASIC/FPGA', Simulink HDL Coder can generate illegal VHDL code for the PN Sequence Generator block in the following cases: The output type is set to 'Smallest unsigned integer', and the outputs are not bit-packed The output type is set to 'Smallest integer' and the outputs are bit-packed  As an example of case 1, consider The Simulink hardware Device type  is a Generic 32-bit Embedded Processor The output is set to 'Smallest unsigned integer' Outputs are not bit-packed  In this case, the generated VHDL code illegally attempts to assign a std_logic signal to an M-bit vector, where M >1. (M is controlled by the device type setting.) As an example of case 2, consider The Simulink hardware Device type  is a Generic 32-bit Embedded Processor The output is set to 'Smallest integer' Outputs are bit-packed.  In this case the generated VHDL code attempts to assign an N-bit vector to an M-bit vector. (M is controlled by the device type setting.) If N does not equal M, this produces illegal VHDL. This problem does not affect generated Verilog. ?
Workaround: Setting the Simulink hardware Device type to 'ASIC/FPGA' will always produce legal VHDL code for the PN Sequence Generator. This is an effective workaround for both bit-packed and non-bit-packed outputs. In general it is recommended that you use the 'ASIC/FPGA' setting for HDL Code generation.If some device type other than ASIC/FPGA is required, you can use the following workarounds:Where outputs are not bit-packed, the output is only one effective bit, with the most significant bits padded with zeros. Therefore, changing the output type from 'Smallest unsigned integer' to 'boolean' will produce the same numeric output with a different data type. By routing the PN Sequence Generator output to a Data Type Convert block, you can convert the boolean output to uint8 or any other desired data type. This configuration will produce legal VHDL that will compile and match simulation.Where outputs are bit-packed: if the number of output bits is not a positive power of 2, the only workaround is to set the Device type to 'ASIC/FPGA.' This configuration will produce legal VHDL that will compile and match simulation.?

Summary: An instance of a MATLAB user-defined class saved to a MAT file using Version 7.6 (R2008a) might not load properly if one of its property values is an instance of a different MATLAB class.
Description: Given an instance of a MATLAB user-defined class with a property that contains an instance of a different class, saving the top-level object to a file using MATLAB Version 7.6 (R2008a) results in the object being loaded incorrectly. After loading, the variable is of the type of the second (inner) class. The error occurs while the object is being saved using MATLAB Version 7.6 (R2008a); thus, the problem manifests itself regardless of the MATLAB version used to load the file.
Workaround: For a property whose value is an instance of a different class, set the initial value of that property in the class constructor rather than as a default value in the properties definition block.

Summary: Incorrect test cases for models with certain counting operations
Description: Simulink Design Verifier generates incorrect test cases for models that contain certain patterns of counting operations. These operations occur when the value of a discrete state or persistent Stateflow variable is incremented by a fixed constant and compared against a threshold value to determine a timer condition. In cases where the initial value of the discrete state or persistent variable is less than 0, Simulink Design Verifier might generate an invalid test case that is too short to satisfy the timer condition.

Summary: The width of the first pulse generated by the Pulse Generator block can be one period wider than specified
Description: The width of the first pulse generated by the Pulse Generator block can be one period wider than specified when its phase delay is different from zero. This problem is only present with variable-step solvers.
Workaround: Configure the pulse generator in "Sample based" mode rather than "Time based" mode.

Summary: Bad tick order for multivarichart and interactionplot plots
Description: When a grouping variable supplied to the multivarichart  or interactionplot  function is ordinal, the tick labels may appear in alphabetical order instead of the order specified by the ordinal grouping variable.

Summary: Variable Editor incorrectly truncates display of character arrays
Description: The Variable Editor incorrectly truncates the display of character arrays. For example, the following code creates and displays a 79-character array: for i=1:79, var(i)=char('0'+i-1); enddisp(var) MATLAB displays all 79 characters in var : 0123456789:;<=>?@ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ[\]^_`abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz{|}~ Open var  in the Variable Editor: openvar var The Variable Editor shows only the first 65 characters of var : 0123456789:;<=>?@ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ[\]^_`abcdefghijklmnop
Workaround: Display character arrays in full by typing the variable name in the Command Window, or by running disp for the variable name.

Summary: Longitude wrapping omitted in forward transformation for certain projections
Description: An explicit longitude-wrapping step is omitted in the implementation of the following projections: eqaconicstd  eqdconicstd  lambertstd  polyconstd  tranmerc  utm   For input data sets that are not already wrapped in longitude, this can cause some latitude-longitude pairs to project to invalid locations with eastings (map x-coordinate values) that are exceptionally large in absolute value.
Workaround: To work around these issues, replace the applyProjection.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapproj/private/applyProjection.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: applyProjection.m.oldCopy the version of applyProjection.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt. ?

Summary: Discrete Event Subsystem initial output value does not appear on scopes
Description: If you connect a Discrete Event Subsystem block to a Signal Scope or X-Y Signal Scope block, the resulting plot does not show the subsystem's initial value of zero.
Workaround: Insert an Initial Value block between the Discrete Event Subsystem block and the scope block.

Summary: Controls are displaced when a menu is added to a GUI created in GUIDE
Description: I have created a GUI with GUIDE. When I use the uimenu  function from the GUI's OpeningFcn , other components get displaced such that the controls at the bottom of the GUI shift downward and are almost not visible.

Summary: The behavior of the integer version of "Remainder after division", rem(x,y), has been changed in R2008b
Description: The behavior of the integer version of "Remainder after division", rem(x,y), has been changed when the second argument, y, is equal to zero. Prior to R2008b, the result of the integer version of rem(x,0) in Simulink was the first argument, x. To be consistent with MATLAB, in R2008b the result of rem(x,0) has been changed to zero.

Summary: Variable Editor does not display array elements in the format specified in Variable Editor preferences
Description: The Variable Editor does not display the format specified in the Variable Editor Preferences pane. Instead, it displays array values using the short  format, unless you change the format using the View  menu.
Workaround: Change the Variable Editor display format using the View menu.

Summary: The PolySpace Model Link SL product requires a server license to perform a local verification
Description: When using the PolySpace Model Link SL product, if you start a PolySpace verification from Simulink and deselect the "Send to PolySpace Server" checkbox (to perform the verification locally), you get the following license error: %%%BEGIN ERROR%%%Checking license ...Unable to checkout feature PolySpace_Server_C_CPP for version 19: Nosuch feature exists.Feature: PolySpace_Server_C_CPPLicense path: C:\PolySpace\PolySpace_Common\etc\license.dat;C:\PolySpace - \PolySpace_Common\etc\*.lic;C:\PolySpace\PolySpaceForCandCPP_R2008a - \Verifier\etc\license.dat;C:\PolySpace\PolySpaceForCandCPP_R2008a - \Verifier\etcFLEXnet Licensing error:-5,357For further information, refer to the FLEXnet Licensing End User Guide,available at "www.macrovision.com".*** License error*** Aborting.%%%END ERROR%%% The message says that the feature "PolySpace_Server_C_CPP" is required in this case. ?
Workaround: To work around this issue, perform the following steps:In the Simulink model, double-click the PolySpace button to bring up the PolySpace Analyser dialog box.Deselect the Send to PolySpace Server checkbox.Click the Configure button in the Project Configuration section to open the Configuration dialog box.In the new dialog, navigate to PolySpace inner settings > Others > other options.Specify -desktop for 'other options'.Save the settings and close the dialog.Start the analysis by clicking the Start button.A local verification is performed on your machine.

Summary: LTI System block parameters do not update after tuning in Simulink Compensator Design Task
Description: After tuning an LTI System block (which is located in Control System Toolbox library) and clicking the Update Simulink Block Parameters  button in Simulink Compensator Design Task of Control and Estimation Tools Manager, the LTI System block dialog does not update with the designed compensator.
Workaround: Follow these steps to update the LTI system block parameters.Quit MATLAB?.Rename the LTIBlock.m file to a new name, such as LTIBlockold.m. The file is in the folder MATLABROOT\toolbox\slcontrol\slcontrol\@LinearizationObjects\@blockconfig where MATLABROOT is the installation directory for MATLAB, which can be obtained by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Download the attached file LTIBlock.m to the folder in step 2.Restart MATLAB.Issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Incorrect simulation result in the Robot Axis Control Using Brushless DC Motor Drive demo model (cs_robot.mdl)
Description: If you open and run the cs_robot.mdl demo, then open the scope, cs_robot/Measurement center/Motor Drive 1 Measurements T1, you will notice that the first subplot (Vab) always has a value of 0. This is incorrect simulation result.
Workaround: To work around this issue, two multimeter entries need to be updated:1) Double-click on the first Multimeter block (cs_robot/measurements T1/Multimeter). Then select the signal "Uab: Motor Drive T1/Three-phase Inverter/Universal Bridge".2) Double-click on the second Multimeter block (cs_robot/measurements T2/Multimeter). Then select the signal "Uab: Motor Drive T2/Three-phase Inverter/Universal Bridge".3) Save the demo model.The demo will now work as intended.?

Summary: NNTrainTool and network VIEW generate java exceptions.
Description: NNTrainTool and network VIEW generate java exceptions when attempting to display a network with the SATLINS transfer function.
Workaround: Use a different transfer function than SATLINS.

Summary: Data cursor remains invisible after being moved from an invisible line to a visible one
Description: If a line containing a data cursor is hidden by turning its visibility off, any data cursor attached to it becomes invisible as well and remains invisible even after moving it to a different, visible line in Data Cursor mode. Plot several lines using the following code hl = plot(rand(5)); dcm = datacursormode(gcf); set(dcm,'Enable','on');  and then place a data cursor on one of the lines. If you then make the line containing the data cursor invisible using plotting tools, set,  or with the following code, dcmStruct = getCursorInfo(dcm); set(dcmStruct.Target, 'Visible', 'off');  the data cursor will become invisible along with the line. If you then move the data cursor to any other visible line (by clicking that line in Data Cursor mode), the data cursor moves to the new line but remains invisible.
Workaround: To work around this issue, the data cursor can be made to reappear if the visibility of the visibile line is toggled, as in the following code:dcmStruct = getCursorInfo(dcm); set(dcmStruct.Target, 'Visible', 'off'); set(dcmStruct.Target, 'Visible', 'on'); ?

Summary: Real-Time Workshop may fail to build subsystems containing very large Stateflow charts for 16-bit and 8-bit targets when Stateflow debugging is turned on
Description: In rare situations, Real-Time Workshop may fail to build a Simulink model that contains Stateflow charts with more than 655536 elements (combined count for states, transitions, junctions, data, and events) if both  of these conditions are true: You set Device type  to 8-bit Embedded Processor  or 16-bit Embedded Processor  in the Hardware Implementation pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box You turn on the Stateflow debugger.  The error message during code generation due to this bug appears as follows: ### Starting Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: largemodel### Generating code into build directory: c:\temp\largemodel_ert_rtwStateflow/Embedded MATLAB parsing for model "largemodel"...DoneStateflow/Embedded MATLAB code generation for model "largemodel"..........failed assertion in B:\matlab\toolbox\stateflow\src\utils\object.cpp at line 2911:id<pool_capacityStack Trace: [0] sf.mexw32:0x18843dc9(0x188da0d4 "id<pool_capacity", 0x188e6080 "B:\matlab\toolbox\stateflow\src\..", 2911, 0) [1] sf.mexw32:0x18848b65(0xffff933b, 8, 0x00cdb3b8 "???", 0x187792bc) [2] sf.mexw32:0x187b0efe(0xffff933b, 0xb0ec4877, 0x2aa482a8, 0x00cdb400) ?
Workaround: Turn off the Stateflow debugger or change the device type to an embedded processor with word size larger than 16 bits.

Summary: The commgmsksoftdecision demo gives erroneously poor BER results.
Description: The demo gives poor BER results for the following reasons:1. The noise scaling in the AWGN block is 10 dB higher than it should be. In other words, if the Es/No parameter of the AWGN block specifies 16 dB, the Es/No is actually 6 dB.2. The matched filter is specified with a Gaussian pulse shape. However, the GMSK signal uses a Gaussian frequency pulse, not a Gaussian amplitude pulse. Thus, the matched filter is not truly matched.

Summary: fminimax and fgoalattain error when I set a non-default value for option TypicalX
Description: When I set a non-default value for option TypicalX  in fminimax  or fgoalattain , I get a size mismatch error.
Workaround: Set the TypicalX option to a vector with one extra element, and set the value of this extra element to 1. For example, if your problem has 3 variables and you want to set TypicalX = [2; 100; 0.01], instead set TypicalX = [2; 100; 0.01; 1].

Summary: Cannot scroll model list in the Export to CAGE dialog box
Description: When you export models to CAGE, there is no scrollbar for the list of models to replace in the Export to CAGE dialog box.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can use the cursor keys to scroll selected items into view.

Summary: The use of try/catch in a parfor loop might cause an 'undefined variable' error.
Description: The following use of try /catch  in a parfor  statement produces an 'undefined variable'  error: parfor i = 1:10  try  error('an error');  catch err  disp(err.getReport);  endend ?
Workaround: To work around this problem, predefine the variable used by the catch statement:err = '';parfor i = 1:10  try  error('an error');  catch err  disp(err.getReport);  endend ?

Summary: Unprojecting non-vector input with the tranmerc projection results in error
Description: If you have a map axes with its mapprojection  property set to 'tranmerc'  (or an mstruct with 'tranmerc'  in the mapprojection  field) and you apply function minvtran  to (x,y) coordinate arrays that are 2-D or higher, the following error is thrown in subfunction phicentral  in function tranmerc : "Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values."
Workaround: You can work around this problem by reshaping your inputs into vectors, calling minvtran, and then restoring their original shape.Or, you can replace the tranmerc.m function file in your current installation with the version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapproj/tranmerc.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: tranmerc.m.oldCopy the version of tranmerc.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt. ?

Summary: Compiling functions with structure variables using emlc or emlmex may cause segment violation
Description: Using emlc  or emlmex  to compile M-functions with structure variables may cause a segment violation under the following conditions: The function has inputs or outputs of structure type There are more than 16 structure fields in all inputs and outputs Some structure fields are assigned, but never used by the function  This bug occurs during optimization when emlc  and emlmex  attempt to generate efficient code by splitting each structure variable into several scalar variables. The bug causes MATLAB to quit with an "assertion failed" message and produces a stack trace that refers to StructExplosion.cpp .
Workaround: Identify the problematic function myFcn and structure parameter myStruct. One method is to incrementally comment out parts of your source code until compilation succeeds.Apply one of the workarounds described below to isolate the structure parameter myStruct from the optimizer.Workaround #1. If you are generating a MEX file using emlmex or emlc -T MEX:Download the attached workaround_mex.zip, extract files workaround.m and dummy.m, and save them to the directory where you run emlmex or emlc.Add the statement workaround(myStruct); at the beginning of the function myFcn (see example below).Compile the program again; if the problem persists, apply the workaround to other suspcious functions.Workaround #2. If you are generating a C executable or library using emlc:Download the attached workaround_c.zip, extract files workaround.m and dummy.h, and save them to the directory where you run emlc.Add the statement workaround(myStruct); at the beginning of the function myFcn.Compile the program again, adding dummy.h to the emlc command:emlc myFcn.m myOptions dummy.hExample of using the workaround: suppose you have a function foo with two structure inputs in1 and in2. To isolate both of them from the optimizer, edit function header to be similar to the following:function foo(in1, in2)  workaround(in1);  workaround(in2); % Function body follows.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: incorrect code for Bus Selector block
Description: The Real-Time Workshop and Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder software might generate incorrect code for the Bus Selector block if the Reuse block outputs  check box is selected in the Configuration Parameters dialog box.
Workaround: To generate correct code for the Bus Selector block:In the Configuration Parameters > Optimization pane, clear the Reuse block outputs check box.Regenerate the code for your model.

Summary: mmreader on Windows fails when reading non-overlapping frame ranges
Description: Using mmreader on Windows platforms to read video frames in chunks can generate unexpected warnings and can crash MATLAB. For example, this code: % Create an mmreader object: vidObj = mmreader('xylophone.mpg')% Read video as ten-frame chunks:for ii = 0:10:100??fprintf('%d %d\n',(ii+1),(ii+10));??frames = read(vidObj,[(ii+1) (ii+10)]);end produces the following output: 1 1011 20Warning: The end of file was reached before the requested frameswere read completely. Frames 11 through 16 were returned.21 3031 40Warning: The end of file was reached before the requested frameswere read completely. Frames 31 through 36 were returned.
Workaround: To work around this issue, read frames one at a time:% Create an mmreader object:vidObj = mmreader('xylophone.mpg')% Read frames one at at time:for ii = 1:vidObj.NumberOfFrames ??frame = read(vidObj, ii);   %... process frame here ...end?

Summary: Build process failure when generating code for model with SimViewing devices
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail while generating code for a model with subsystems or blocks that have the SimViewingDevice block parameter enabled. The build process reports the failure message in the MATLAB Command Window similar to the following: [2] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl WritePrmReferencedBy(struct RTWInfo *,struct slAggParamTableEl_tag *,struct slParamBlockRefNode_tag * * *,int * *,int * *,int *)[3] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl WriteModelParameters(struct RTWInfo *)[4] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl WriteRTWFile(struct RTWInfo *)
Workaround: Find all blocks that have the SimViewingDevice parameter enabled by entering the following at the MATLAB command line: find_system(bdroot, 'LookUnderMasks', 'on', 'FollowLinks', 'on', 'Type', 'block', 'SimViewingDevice', 'on')Delete the blocks.

Summary: Changing map axes UTM zone quietly resets reference ellipsoid
Description: If you construct a map axes using the UTM system and set its reference ellipsoid (via the 'geoid'  property) to a non-default value (with either axesm  or setm ), and then you reset the UTM zone, the reference ellipsoid reverts to the default. Also note that the WGS 84 ellipsoid is not the default. The default ellipsoid depends on both the latitudinal and longitudinal zone.
Workaround: If you don't want to use the default reference ellipsoid when working with UTM, make sure that whenever you reset the UTM zone of your map axes you also reset the 'geoid' property to the value you want.

Summary: Data Object Wizard may overwrite a non-numeric item with a signal object that has the same name as the item
Description: When the Data Object Wizard lists a signal that has the same name as a non-numeric item in the base workspace, and you use the wizard to create a data object for the signal, the new signal object will overwrite the non-numeric item. No warning occurs, but subsequent model updates will fail if the model uses the overwritten non-numeric item.
Workaround: For R2006b, R2007a, R2007b, and R2008a,replace matlabroot/toolbox/simulink/simulink/dataobjectwizard/dow_callback.p:Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.Exit MATLAB.In the directory matlabroot/toolbox/simulink/simulink/dataobjectwizard, rename the file dow_callback.p to dow_callback.pOldDownload the attached file and place it in the directory matlabroot/toolbox/simulink/simulink/dataobjectwizard.Restart MATLAB.Enter rehash toolbox at the MATLAB command line.Enter rehash toolboxcache at the MATLAB command line.

Summary: Problem trimming polygons in azimuthal projections when inner rings intersect frame limit
Description: In an azimuthal projection, a problem can occur when an inner ring intersects the edge of the circular map frame. (An inner ring is a counter-clockwise ring that outlines a void within a polygon, like the shore of a lake or an inland sea.) Under these circumstances, for certain data sets, the designated face color may fill all or part of the exterior of the polygon, instead of filling just the interior.
Workaround: To work around these issues, replace the trimPolygonToSmallCircle.m function file in your current installation with the version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapproj/private/trimPolygonToSmallCircle.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: trimPolygonToSmallCircle.m.oldCopy the version of trimPolygonToSmallCircle.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt. ?

Summary: Embedded MATLAB functions with large numbers of switch cases run out of memory during compilation.
Description: Embedded MATLAB functions that contain switch statements with thousands of cases run out of memory during compilation in Simulink, Stateflow, emlc , and emlmex . This bug may also be triggered by functions with complex control flow or machine-generated MATLAB files.
Workaround: If working in Simulink, follow these steps:Execute the following command each time you start MATLAB: sf('Feature',' Local Dead Code Enhanced','off')Recompile the Embedded MATLAB Function blockRunning this command may produce less efficient code.

Summary: Build process failure when generating code for model with nested If Action or Switch Case Action Subsystems
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail to generate code for a model under all of the following conditions: An If or Switch Case block triggers a subsystem. The subsystem contains a Switch Case block that specifies a range of case conditions, for example, case [2 4] . A model option specifies that Simulink block comments are not inserted in the generated code.  The build process reports the failure message in the MATLAB Command Window similar to the following: CGIR assertion in .\dom_core\cg_lower_switch.cpp(67): 'region != 0'
Workaround: Turn on Simulink block comments by selecting the Simulink block / Stateflow object comments check box in the Real-Time Workshop > Comments pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Unexpected results in Signal Builder block when changing signal ordering
Description: When using a Signal Builder block with multiple signal groups, changing the index of a signal in the Signal Builder block produces the expected results in the active signal group. However, it does not correctly reorder the waveforms in other groups. With multiple groups in a Signal Builder block, reordering signals in the active group should rename signals and adjust the line properties in other groups.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download and install the attached files.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/ folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file: /toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/change_channel_index.p/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/new_channel.pTo see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/ folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/ folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/ folder.

Summary: Incorrect treatment of some occurrences of X.m in a script.
Description: If X is the name of a class with a static method m, but X is also a variable hiding the definition of the class, then MATLAB incorrectly treats X.m in a script as a use of the static method.
Workaround: Choose a variable name that is not the name of a class.

Summary: The importdata function sometimes returns an incorrect number of header lines in text files
Description: If a text file contains blank header lines, importdata  recognizes only the lines before the first blank line as headers.
Workaround: Remove blank lines from the file header.

Summary: The SCI module cannot re-establish a connection after a brief disconnection.
Description: The SCI host and target blocks cannot resend commands when they are in "Protocol" communication mode. As a result, any interruption to the communications produces a time-out error, even if the interruption is shorter than the time-out period.

Summary: Segmentation violation occurs during code generation when model contains Math Function and Matrix Concatenation blocks
Description: A segmentation violation might occur when generating code with Real-Time Workshop or Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder software for a model that contains both a Math Function block and a Matrix Concatenation block if all of the following conditions are true: The Matrix Concatenation block is in vector mode. The output of the Math Function block is directly connected to the Matrix Concatenation block.  ?
Workaround: To prevent the segmentation violation, do the following:Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Math Function and Matrix Concatenation blocks.Select the Override optimizations and always copy signal check box in the Signal Conversion Block Parameters dialog box.

Summary: deconvwnr(I,PSF,NSR) errors if I, PSF, and NSR are row vectors.
Description: deconvwnr(I,PSF,NSR) throws a matrix dimensions mismatch error if I, PSF, and NSR are row vectors. The problematic line is: Denom = abs(otf(nojunk(:))).^2 + K(:);
Workaround: To work around these issues, replace the version of deconvwnr.m in your current installation with the updated version contained in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/images/images/deconvwnr.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: deconvwnr.m.oldDownload the attached file, extract deconvwnr.m, storing it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.The denominator calculation is now robust to any size of image data.

Summary: An error can occur if a model contains a Switch block that has a non-inherited output data type or bus signals connected to inputs 1 and 3
Description: If a Simulink model contains a Switch block whose output data type is not set to "Inherit", the following error can occur: "Cannot change port data type of '.../Switch' while simulation is running"  If a model contains two bus signals connected to inputs 1 and 3 of a Switch block, the following error can occur: "Cannot change bus data of '.../Switch' while simulation is running"  These errors can occur under either of the following conditions: The user performs an Update Diagram on the model while simulation is running. The model is referenced by a Model block in a top model, and the user performs an Update Diagram or starts simulation on the top model.
Workaround: You can work around the first issue in some cases by setting the output data type to one of the "Inherit" modes. You can work around the second issue by connecting identical bus signals to ports 1 and 3 of the Switch block.

Summary: Categorical arrays appear empty in the Variable Editor
Description: Categorical arrays appear empty when viewed in the MATLAB Variable Editor.
Workaround: In the MATLAB Command Window, type the name of the categorical array whose contents you want to view and press Enter. MATLAB displays the correct values there.

Summary: Simulink may exit with a segmentation violation when updating a mask parameter while simulation is running
Description: Simulink may exit with a segmentation violation under the following circumstances: The model has a masked subsystem with at least one tunable mask parameter. The subsystem has at least one Inport block, which may be nested several levels deep within the subsystem. A mask parameter of the masked subsystem is changed while simulation is running.  A stack trace similar to the following may be displayed: Stack Trace:[0] libmwsimulink.dll:bool __cdecl InportIsABusSource(class slBlock const *)(0x1d6ff720, 0, 0x1d6ff720, 0x00d3e8fc) + 97 bytes[1] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl InportEvalParamsFcn(class slBlock *)(0x1d6ff720, 0x1d739a00, 0x1d6ff720, 0x1f810020) + 265 bytes[2] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl EvalBlockAndPortParams(class slBlock *)(0, 0x1d6ff720, 0x048fbc20, 0x6faeef01) + 219 bytes[3] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl BlockEvalParams(class slBlock *)(0x1d6ff720, 0x00d3e980, 0x6f626443, 0x1d6ff720) + 986 bytes[4] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl EvalSubsystemChild(struct slBlockDiagram_tag *,struct slGraph_tag *,class slBlock *,double)(0x1d6ff720,0, 0x1d6ff580, 0x047d4300) + 94 bytes[5] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl EvalSubsystemChildren(class slBlock *)(0x1d6ff580, 0, 0x1d6ff580, 0x00d3e9b0) + 307 bytes[6] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl SubsystemEvalParamsFcn(class slBlock *)(0x1d6ff580, 0x047d4300, 0x1d6ff580, 0x1f810020) + 78 bytes[7] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl EvalBlockAndPortParams(class slBlock *)(0x1d6ff580, 0x1d6ff580, 0x00d3ea2c, 0x27fd3ee0 "__ehhandler$??1RTWInfo@@QAE@XZ") + 219 bytes[8] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl BlockEvalParams(class slBlock *)(0x1d6ff580, 0x27fd3ee0 "__ehhandler$??1RTWInfo@@QAE@XZ", 0x00d3ea88 "?????"", 0x6f6873df) + 834 bytes[9] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl BlockEvalParamsOutsideDiagramUpdating(class slBlock *)(0x1d6ff580, 0x046c0230, 0xffffffff, 0x00d3eb64) + 54 bytes[10] libmwsimulink.dll:void __cdecl matl_set_param(int,struct mxArray_tag * * const,int,struct mxArray_tag * * const)(0, 0x00d3eb64, 0x1a146520, 0x00d3ebc4) + 1727 bytes
Workaround: Do not update mask parameters for subsystems with Inport blocks while simulation is running.

Summary: Generated code for model containing reusable subsystem might not compile
Description: The Real-Time Workshop code generator implements a subsystem as a function with arguments if either of the following is true: The Real-Time Workshop system code  parameter for the subsystem is set to Reusable function . The Real-Time Workshop system code  parameter for the subsystem is set to Auto  and Simulink reuses the subsystem for multiple instances  When a subsystem is implemented as a function with arguments, the generated code is invalid if all of the following are true: One of the inputs to the subsystem is connected to a block output that is defined as a nonaddressable storage class. The storage class of that block output is a signal with a custom storage class that is not addressable, such as BitField  or GetSet . The subsystem input is passed to its function by pointer.  The generated code does not compile because it attempts to take the address of the variable or expression corresponding to the input of the Subsystem block.
Workaround: Do one of the following:Use a Signal Converstion block: Place a Signal Conversion block at the forementioned input to the Subsystem block.Select the Override optimizations and always copy signal check box for the Signal Conversion block.Change the Real-Time Workshop system code parameter for the subsystem to Inline or Function.
Fixed In: R2007b web release, R2008a web release, R2008b

Summary: dicominfo returns Curve Data with the wrong datatype
Description: dicomfino  ignores the Data Value Representation field when returning the Curve Data, resulting in the Curve Data always being returned as uint8  or uint16  values.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the version of the dicominfo function in your current installation with the updated version contained in the attached file. dicominfo now uses the Data Value Representation attribute to cast the Curve Data into its correct data type.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot\toolbox\images\medformats\dicominfo.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, dicominfo.m.OLD.Download the attached file, extract dicominfo.m, and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Sort block can give wrong results for Boolean inputs
Description: The Sort block does not support Boolean inputs. However, instead of issuing a warning when its input is Boolean, the block silently gives wrong results for code generation and in accelerator mode. In code generation, the block incorrectly uses a runtime function that is designed to work with uint32  inputs. Therefore, you see the following warning when you are generating code: 'incompatible types - from 'const boolean_T *' to 'const uint32_T *.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use a data type other than Boolean for the input to this block. You can use the Simulink Data Type Conversion block to convert an upstream signal from Boolean to another data type, if necessary.

Summary: Code generation results in the error 'Cannot evaluate "Timer Resolution" parameter of an Idle Task block'
Description: When you generate code for a model that includes the Idle Task block, code generation stops and you receive the following error in Simulink?: 'Cannot evaluate "Timer Resolution" parameter of an Idle Task block'  The code generation process cannot locate the Timer Resolution parameter in the block. The missing parameter prevents code generation from continuing.
Workaround: Download the attached files patch_installer.m and patch_458020.zip. Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch:patch_installer('patch_458020.zip', 'install')Follow the instructions printed at the MATLAB command line to complete the installation. The patch modifies the following file in your MATLAB installation:<matlabroot>\toolbox\shared\idelinks\pjtgenerator\mdlinfo\util_BlockManagesOwnTime.m

Summary: BLASTNCBI and GETBLAST fail due to a change in the RID (Request ID) format by the NCBI BLAST Server.
Description: BLASTNCBI and GETBLAST fail to recognize the RID (Request ID) returned by the NCBI BLAST Server because of recent changes in the RID format.
Workaround: Follow these steps as a workaround:1. Quit MATLAB.2. Rename the MATLAB file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\blastncbi.m to blastncbi.m.old.Rename the MATLAB file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\getblast.m to getblast.m.old.(where $MATLAB$ is the MATLAB root directory on your machine)3. Download the attached MATLAB files to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\4. Restart MATLAB.5. After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Code generation returns VisualDSP++ linker error li1060
Description: When you generate code from a model, you receive the li1060 error in VisualDSP++?. This problem affects code generation for BlackFin?, SHARC?, and TigerSHARC? processor families. Using the Sections  pane options of the Target Preferences block to define and place a custom memory section in one of the default memory blocks causes the error.
Workaround: Download the attached patch installer patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive patch_457747.zip. Start MATLAB? and enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch:patch_installer('patch_457747.zip', 'install')Follow instructions printed out on the MATLAB command line to complete the installation. The attached patch modifies the following files in your MATLAB installation:<matlabroot>\toolbox\rtw\targets\vdsplink\vdsplink\blackfin\blackfin_ldf.tlc<matlabroot>\toolbox\rtw\targets\vdsplink\vdsplink\sharc\sharc_ldf.tlc<matlabroot>\toolbox\rtw\targets\vdsplink\vdsplink\tigersharc\tigersharc_ldf.tlc

Summary: Having if.exe on the PATH, when creating an executable or C/C++ shared library using MATLAB Compiler, causes link step to fail.
Description: Having if.exe  on the PATH, when creating an executable or C/C++ shared library using MATLAB Compiler, causes link step to fail.
Workaround: For release R2008a (version 4.8), download the attached mbuild options files and place in <matlab root>/matlab/bin/win32/mbuildopts.

Summary: A file with a different file extension, but the same base name, can prevent MATLAB from finding a super class file (e.g., Super.m and Super.asv)
Description: Suppose your current directory is: /myPath/workingDir  and this directory contains a file called Super.asv . Also, assume there is a file /myPath/baseDir/Super.m , which defines a base class, Super , and a file /myPath/subDir/Sub.m , which defines class Sub  that is a subclass of Super . The following code produces an error in R2008a. >> addpath /myPath/baseDir >> addpath /myPath/subDir >> Sub ??? Error using ==> SubThe specified super-class 'Super' cannot be found on MATLAB's search path orcontains a parse error.  MATLAB should not issue this error here because Super  is a valid class on the path. This bug is caused when MATLAB attempts to execute Super.asv as a legal Matlab file. This bug also occurs if /myPath/workingDir  is ahead of /myPath/baseDir  on the search path.
Workaround: The workaround is to move the file, Super.asv, so that it does not hide the super class definition file Super.m.

Summary: Noncompilable code generated for model containg nonvirtual bus
Description: The Real-Time Workshop and Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder software might generate noncompilable code if both of the following apply to a block in your model: An output port of the block is a nonvirtual bus and it is connected to the root outport. The RTW storage class  property of the output signal of the same block is set to ImportedExternPointer .

Summary: Simulink Bus Editor may report an error when exporting bus objects to MATLAB files on Japanese Windows machines
Description: If you export Simulink.Bus objects to a MATLAB file on a Japanese Windows machine, Simulink may report the following error: ??? Ą¨Ąé©` ==> Simulink.Bus.save at 842 ·¬Äż¤ÎČëÁ¦¤Ďˇ˘'cell' ¤Ţ¤ż¤Ď 'object' ¤Î¤¤¤ş¤ě¤«¤Ç¤Ę¤±¤ě¤Đ¤Ę¤ę¤Ţ¤»¤óˇŁ
Workaround: Use Simulink.Bus.save from the MATLAB command prompt to export bus objects to a MATLAB file.

Summary: Labels incorrect on box plots
Description: Box plot labels may be incorrect when: The 'positions'  parameter contains NaN s The grouping variable contains NaN s or blanks  The problem does not occur if labels are explicitly specified via the 'labels'  parameter. Example:  load carsmallboxplot(MPG,{Origin,Cylinders},'position',[1:10])figureboxplot(MPG,{Origin,Cylinders},'position',[1:8 NaN 10])  In the second plot, the last box is incorrectly labeled with the second-to-last label of the first plot.
Workaround: Avoid using NaN position values to omit groups. Instead, omit rows of the input X and corresponding elements of the grouping variables.

Summary: You cannot call the superclass subsasgn or subsref method from the subclass of a built-in class.
Description: Due to a bug in MATLAB, calling the subsasgn  or subsref  superclass method with @ syntax does not work for subclasses of built-in classes. For example, if you have a class named mydouble  that inherits from the built-in class double , and a method of mydouble  calls subsasgn  using the following syntax: obj = subasgn@double(obj, s, b); you get an error: ??? One or more output arguments not assigned during call to "feval".
Workaround: For the above example, replaceobj = subasgn@double(obj, s, b);withobj = mydouble(builtin('subsasgn',double(obj),s,b));

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Code fails to link models with exported graphical functions in Stateflow charts
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might not generate code for exported graphical functions in Stateflow charts when the model or subsystem only contains either of the following: Stateflow charts with exported graphical functions that call function-call subsystems Stateflow charts with exported graphical functions
Workaround: Add a Real-Time Workshop custom code block that contains a comment to the subsystem that contains the Stateflow chart with the missing exported graphical functions.

Summary: Build process failure when generating code for model with Stateflow charts that export graphical functions
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail to generate code for a model under all of the following conditions: The model contains multiple Stateflow charts that are in different, noninlined Subsystem blocks (Subsystem blocks with Real-Time Workshop system code  parameter set to Function  or Reusable function ). The Stateflow charts use and export graphical functions.  The build process reports the failure message in the MATLAB Command Window similar to the following: Stack Trace:CGIR assertion in c:\work\acgir\matlab\src\cg_ir\bundles\../base_core/expr/AbstractCallExpr.cpp(74): 'f!=0' [0] libmwsimulink.dll:void __cdecl assertion_func(void *,char const * const,int,char const * const)() + 46 bytes[1] cg_ir.dll:void __cdecl client_assertion_failed(char const *,int,char const *)() + 30 bytes
Workaround: Enter the following at the MATLAB command line:sf('Feature', 'Coder Unification', 0)Note: Entering this command disables enhanced inlining and cross block optimizations added for Stateflow charts in R2008a.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Missing index expression for accessing N-D tunable parameter in Stateflow chart
Description: The Real-Time Workshop code generator eliminates code for second and higher dimension index expressions if the expressions represent a Stateflow chart accessing N-D tunable parameters. For example, the following matrix element access: y = P[2][3];  is generated as: y = P[2];  ?
Workaround: Avoid this by entering the following at the MATLAB command line:sf('Feature', 'Coder Unification', 0);

Summary: Printing current view stateflow subcharts throws an error
Description: Opening a stateflow subchart and doing File->Print current view to any format throws an error

Summary: deploytool lists wrong default MCR installer filename on MATLAB 7.6 (R2008a) for MACI
Description: The default location populated in deploytool  settings on Intel Mac points to a non-existing file. This causes the Settings panel to throw an error when clicking the OK  button after accepting the default location for the MCR Installer.
Workaround: Open Settings.Select Packaging in the left pane.Select Include MATLAB Compiler Runtime (MCR).Click Browse and select MCRInstaller.dmg from matlabroot/toolbox/compiler/deploy/maci/MCRInstaller.dmg?

Summary: blackmanharris and nuttallwin functions return incorrect answers
Description: An incorrect implementation of the private function min4termwin.m  causes incorrect coefficients to be returned by the blackmaharris.m  and nuttalwin.m  functions.
Workaround: Edit min4termwin.m in $ROOT/toolbox/signal/signal/private and replace:  x = (0:N-1)' * 2.0*pi/(N-1);with  x = (0:N-1)' * 2.0*pi/N;

Summary: While launching the Scope Parameters Dialog, changing the 'DefaultFigureWindowStyle' to 'docked' results in errors.
Description: While launching the Scope Parameters Dialog, changing the 'DefaultFigureWindowStyle' to 'docked' results in errors.
Workaround: Set the 'DefaultFigureWindowStyle' back to 'normal' when using the Scope Parameters Dialog.

Summary: The behavior of the Fixed-Point Toolbox? pow2 function has changed in R2007b and R2008a
Description: The Fixed-Point Toolbox? pow2  function stopped obeying the RoundMode  property of fi  objects in the R2007b release. In both the R2007b and R2008a releases, the pow2  function behaves like the bitshift  function, and only obeys the OverflowMode  property of the fi  object. When run using an R2007a or earlier version of Fixed-Point Toolbox software, the following example produces the expected result:a = fi(0.5,1,8,7,'RoundMode','ceil');b = pow2(a,-8) % The result should have a value of 0.0078ans =0.0078 DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: binary point scalingSigned: trueWordLength: 8FractionLength: 7 RoundMode: ceilOverflowMode: saturateProductMode: FullPrecisionMaxProductWordLength: 128SumMode: FullPrecisionMaxSumWordLength: 128CastBeforeSum: true When the same commands are run using an R2007b or R2008a version of Fixed-Point Toolbox software, the resulting fi  object b  has an incorrect value of 0:a = fi(0.5,1,8,7,'RoundMode','ceil');b = pow2(a,-8) % The result should have a value of 0.0078ans = 0 DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: binary point scalingSigned: trueWordLength: 8FractionLength: 7 RoundMode: ceilOverflowMode: saturateProductMode: FullPrecisionMaxProductWordLength: 128SumMode: FullPrecisionMaxSumWordLength: 128CastBeforeSum: true
Workaround: If you are using an R2007b or R2008a version of Fixed-Point Toolbox software, please read the following:To workaround this issue, use the multiplication operation instead of the pow2 function.In order to do this, you must create a fi object c and assign it the following properties:Value = 2kSigned = 0WordLength = 1FractionLength = -kWhere k is the integer value that would have been an input to the pow2 function.For example:a = fi(0.5,1,8,7,'RoundMode','ceil');c = fi(2^k,0,1,-k,'fimath',fimath(a));b = a;b(:) = a*c;Note: If you are running this code using the Embedded MATLAB? subset, the value of k must be constant and defined at compile time.

Summary: Stateflow charts using unary minus expressions may generate spurious errors
Description: The Stateflow debugger throws a spurious divide by zero  error and stops simulation under the following conditions: The chart uses a signed integer variable in a unary minus expression The integer has a run-time value of zero at some point in the simulation  The attached model divbyzero_bug.mdl  illustrates how this error occurs. Notice that the input u2  is of type int32  after type propagation is complete.
Workaround: Use the expression 0-data rather than the unary expression -data. The attached model divbyzero_bug_workaround.mdl illustrates this workaround.

Summary: Unusually complicated expressions that use the end keyword may fail
Description: Unusually complicated expressions that include the end  keyword have been seen to fail. This failure may cause the return of incorrect answers or may cause MATLAB to terminate prematurely. An example of such an expression is     Data.data{n}.DIM(end).label(c,:)
Workaround: The work-around is to break the expression into simpler pieces. The above example could be written as    tmp = Data.data{n}.DIM;    x = tmp(end).label(c,:);

Summary: RCOSFLT function errors out if 'filter_type/filter' syntax is used and 'num' has values greater than 1
Description: When using RCOSFLT syntaxes y = rcosflt(x,Fd,Fs,'filter_type/filter',num,den)y = rcosflt(x,Fd,Fs,'filter_type/filter',num,den,delay)y = rcosflt(x,Fd,Fs,'filter_type/filter/Fs',num,den...) the user gets the error "The rolloff factor in RCOSFLT cannot be greater than 1" if the num input has element(s) greater than 1, since the error checking mistakes the num input for the rolloff factor.
Workaround: Download the attached file and copy to (MATLABROOT)/toolbox/comm/comm.Follow these steps to replace the MATLAB file imscrollpanel.m.1. Quit MATLAB?. 2. Rename the rcosflt.m file in your current MATLAB installation to another name, such as rcosflt.m.old. This file is in MATLABROOT\toolbox\comm\comm, where MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by using the matlabroot function at the MATLAB command prompt. 3. Download the attached file rcosflt.m to the directory in step 2. 4. Restart MATLAB. 5. Issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Compiled GUIDE GUIs that contain UITables do not show table data at run time
Description: The data in a uitable in a MATLAB compiled GUIDE GUI is not visible when the compiled application executes.

Summary: CPU overloads might occur when the Measurement Computing PCI-CTR05 interrupt input is used to run a model
Description: CPU overloads might occur when the Measurement Computing PCI-CTR05 interrupt input is used to run a model. If the interrupting signal is active when you start the model, a CPU overload might happen during the first time step of execution. While the immediate overload can occur at any input frequency; it is more likely to occur at high frequencies.
Workaround: If you are using Release R2007b or R2008a, perform the following:Stop MATLAB.Download the attached file xpccbpcictr05hooks.c and save it to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\src.Restart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.

Summary: Error when panning or zooming in an axes containing an image which is a child of an HGGroup
Description: Attempting to use the pan or zoom tools in an axes containing an image which is a child of an HGGroup  object, as in the following code: hGr = hggroup;hIm = image(magic(5),'Parent',hGR) will cause the following error to appear repeatedly in the command window. ??? Attempted to access lims(:,1); index out of bounds because size(limes)=[0,0]. ?

Summary: Extraneous warnings appear in the text box of a FIGURE window
Description: Plot a line using the function: line  In the FIGURE window, click on Insert -> TextBox and then click on the figure. A text box is created. If you type any characters in the text box, MATLAB generates the following messages at the command prompt: Warning: uncompleted command in TeX text string

Summary: Changing the Seed parameter of a Random Number block or Uniform Random Number block forces rebuilding the model before code-generation-based simulation can begin?
Description: In R2008a, changing the value of the Seed  parameter of a Random Number block or a Uniform Random Number block forces an automatic rebuild to occur before simulation starts in Accelerator mode, Rapid Accelerator mode, or External mode, all of which use generated code for simulation. In previous releases, changing the value of the Seed  parameter between simulations that use generated code did not force a rebuild.

Summary: When using National Instruments frame grabbers and setting a specific region of interest (ROI) a warning is sometimes given.?
Description: Sometimes when using a National Instruments frame grabber, after setting a specified region of interest, when the device is next started users may see the following warning: Warning: The specified ROI was not valid and has been adjusted to the nearest valid value.  However, the National Instruments Measurement & Automation Explorer does allow the specified region of interest. ?
Workaround: Acquire the frames at a larger region of interest, and then crop the region in MATLAB after the acquisition.For example, for a set of ten RGB frames, to obtain a 800 x 599 pixel image, acquire at 800 x 600 and then crop:dCropped = d(1:599, :, :, :);

Summary: LDL and LINSOLVE may not work with full symmetric matrices that contain NaN entries.
Description: LDL and LINSOLVE may not work with full symmetric matrices that contain NaN entries. Using full symmetric matrices that contain NaN entries may cause MATLAB? to hang.

Summary: In the Image Tool (imtool), the cropping rectangle can move outside the image extent with certain image size/magnification combinations.
Description: When viewing an image in the Image Tool (imtool ) at 67% magnification, the cropping rectangle can move outside the boundaries of the image. The following example illustrates this problem. Open pout.tif  in the Image Tool: imtool('pout.tif');  Set the magnification to 67%. Click the Crop Image  button in the Image Tool toolbar. Drag the cropping rectangle and note how you can drag it outside the image boundaries.

Summary: The ROI tools that are placed via click and drag (impoint, imline, imrect, imellipse, and imfreehand) get into a strange state if you left click to begin placement and then hold both mouse buttons down before ending placement.
Description: If you place an ROI interactively and begin placement by depressing the left mouse button, then (while holding the left mouse button) also press the right mouse button, interactive placement will get into a strange state. You will effectively re-start placement but some graphics from the original ROI will remain on the screen.

Summary: Adding lines from all ports to a digital I/O object in Data Acquisition Toolbox version 2.12 (Release 2008a) generates an error
Description: You cannot add all available lines with one addline  command when you create a digital I/O object for a device with multiple ports. It generates this error: The specifed line could not be found on any port.  For example, with a device that has two digital I/O ports of four lines each, totaling eight lines, executing the following code in Release 2008a generates an error:dio = digitalio('mcc',0);addline(dio,0:7,'out'); ??? Error using ==> digitalio.addline at 211The specifed line could not be found on any port.  Adding all available lines with one addline  command works in Release 2007b.
Workaround: You can work around this problem by adding all the lines on each port separately.addline(dio, 0:3, 0, 'out')addline(dio, 0:3, 1, 'out')This produces the same result as adding all the lines for all ports with a single addline command.

Summary: When running mcc from MATLAB, it either does not find mcc or picks up an instance of mcc from a previous version of MATLAB.
Description: If there are multiple versions of MATLAB installed on a machine and code is compiled using mcc  from R2008a. the following error occurs: C:\PROGRA~1\MATLAB\R2007B\BIN\MEX.PL: Error: Compile of'magicsquare_main.c' failed.Error: An error occurred while shelling out to mbuild (error code = 2).Unable to build executable.??? Error using ==> mccError executing mcc, return status = 1.  With only MATLAB 7.6 (R2008a) installed, the following error occurs when using mcc  from MATLAB: 'mbuild' is not recognized as an internal or external command,operable program or batch file.Error: An error occurred while shelling out to mbuild (error code = 1).Unable to build executable.??? Error using ==> mccError executing mcc, return status = 1.  This is due to the matlabroot /bin  directory, containing the mbuild  script, not being present on your system path. Subseqeuently, when mcc  calls mbuild , it fails to find it or picks it up from a pervious version of MATLAB where the bin directory is on the path.
Workaround: When running R2008a, add matlabroot\bin to the system PATH as follows:On the desktop, right-click My Computer and select Properties.Click on the Advanced tab, then click Environment Variables.Look for PATH under the Variable column in the System Variables section.Select the row containing PATH and click Edit.Add the matlabroot\bin location for R2008a to the existing value of the PATH environment variable.Restart MATLAB. This will allow the modified Path to be picked up.Ensure you add matlabroot\bin before matlabroot\bin\win32. Also ensure you add matlabroot\bin for R2008a before matlabroot\bin of any previous releases of MATLAB.You can verify the successful addition of matlabroot\bin to your system path by typing:getenv('Path')at the MATLAB command prompt.

Summary: Simulink mask icons using very large images may cause Library Browser slowdowns in R2008a.
Description: The new Simulink Library Browser can slow down very badly for masked block icons containing large image files.
Workaround: Use smaller images for masked block icons.

Summary: Merge, Switch, and Multiport Switch blocks may not accept valid nonvirtual bus inputs
Description: The Merge block, Switch block, and Multiport Switch block accept virtual and nonvirtual bus signals, with limitations as described in the block documentation. However, a model that contains one or more of these blocks may fail to update when a nonvirtual bus signal is connected to the block, even though no error actually exists. Simulink will issue a spurious error message similar to: Block  does not accept signals of data type 'BusObject '. 'model_name/Block ' accepts only the MATLAB 'Boolean','uint8', 'uint16', 'uint32', 'int8', 'int16', 'int32', 'single', and 'double' data types, and the fixed-point data types.  The error message references the name of the bus object as the data type of the signal.
Workaround: Use a Signal Conversion block to convert the nonvirtual bus to a virtual bus before inputing the bus to the Merge block, Switch block, or Multiport Switch block named in the error message.

Summary: Models referenced in Normal mode with S-function blocks may incorrectly require TLC files
Description: A referenced model that executes in Normal mode and contains an S-function block may incorrectly require a TLC file. Some situations where this problem can occur are: S-functions that permit constant sample times on their ports S-functions that have port-based sample times and have specified the option SS_OPTION_ALLOW_PORT_SAMPLE_TIME_IN_TRIGSS  If this problem occurs, a spurious error message describing a need for a TLC file appears when you try to update or simulate the diagram. No referenced model that executes in Normal mode actually requires a TLC file.

Summary: The NCO block can produce incorrect results in floating-point override mode
Description: The NCO block can produce incorrect results or a segmentation violation when the following conditions are met: Input port  is selected for the Phase offset source  block parameter. True doubles  or True singles  is specified for the Data type override  parameter on the Fixed-Point Tool. Use local settings  or Force off  is selected for the Logging mode  parameter on the Fixed-Point Tool.  You can access the Fixed-Point Tool by selecting Tools > Fixed-Point > Fixed-Point Tool in your model.
Workaround: You can work around this issue and use the True doubles or True singles mode for the Data type override parameter in the Fixed-Point Tool by selecting Minimums, maximums and overflows or Overflows only for the Logging mode parameter.

Summary: The datestr function incorrectly parses certain date formats
Description: The datestr  function returns incorrect output when given certain date formats that contain no spaces. For example, the following two commands convert a date from its string format to a numeric format, and then back again. The resulting string, '040807' is incorrect. The string value returned should be '040708'. datenum('07-Apr-2008')ans =    733505datestr(733505, 'mmddyy')ans =    040807
Workaround: Use a different format. For the example above, you could use the following command which returns the correct result.datestr(733505, 'mm dd yy')ans =    04 07 08

Summary: Why can a test element not be renamed with a double-click?
Description: Attempting to rename a test element by double-clicking on the name in the Test Browser tree is not working properly. An edit box will briefly appear, but then go away, preventing a new name from being entered.
Workaround: In order to rename a test element, you need to double-click on the last letter of the test element name.

Summary: Incorrect assignment with complicated left-hand side from a right-hand side with multiple values.
Description: Assignment to certain complicated left-hand side expresssions from a right-hand side expresssions of the form x.a , where x  is non-scalar may yield an incorrect result or a crash. For example,    params.test1 = 1;    fieldnamelist = {'subfield1','subfield2'};    newstruct2.(fieldnamelist{1}(1:end)) = params.test1    newstruct2 =       subfield1: {} The correct value of newstruct2.subfield1  is 1, not {}.
Workaround: Divide up the assignment using a temporary variable:   tmp = params.test1;    newstruct2.(fieldnamelist{1}(1:end)) = tmp;

Summary: The eCAN TX mailbox may not work correctly, depending on how it is initialized.
Description: The eCAN TX mailbox relies on a reset state to initialize correctly. If the user boots via CAN or changes the CAN mailbox direction register value before init time, the registers may be set incorrectly, causing the CAN transmit mailbox to operate as a receive mailbox.
Workaround: This bug has been fixed in release R2008b. You solve this bug in MATLAB/Simulink releases R2007b and R2008a by installing patch files attached to this EBR.WARNING: This patch is for MATLAB/Simulink releases R2007b and R2008a ONLY. Do not install it with any other release, or you may damage your MATLAB/Simulink software.To install the patch files:To determine which MATLAB/Simulink release you are using, start MATLAB and enter ver at the command prompt.Download patch_installer.m and the patch file that matches your release:patch_455459R2007b.zip patch_455459R2008a.zipAt the MATLAB command prompt, enter the command that matches the patch filename:patch_installer('patch_455459R2007b.zip', 'install')patch_installer('patch_455459R2008a.zip', 'install')To uninstall the patch:At the MATLAB command prompt, enter the command that matches the patch filename:patch_installer('patch_455459R2007b.zip', 'uninstall')patch_installer('patch_455459R2008a.zip', 'uninstall')

Summary: fzero does not recognize when an initial guess is an infinite interval, meaning an endpoint is ˇŔInf.
Description: fzero  accepts a vector of length 2 describing an interval over which to search for a root of the input function. This interval should be finite. fzero  does not catch infinite cases, and then loops indefinitely, without progress.
Workaround: fzero is not designed to handle infinite initial guesses or intervals. Do not pass infinite intervals as initial guesses.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: incorrect code for Hit Crossing block
Description: The Real-Time Workshop and Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder software might generate incorrect code for the Hit Crossing block if the Reuse block outputs  check box is selected in the Configuration Parameters dialog box.
Workaround: To generate correct code for the Hit Crossings block:In the Configuration Parameters > Optimization pane, clear the Reuse block outputs check box.Regenerate the code for your model.

Summary: The Gain block can give wrong results when the input is complex and the gain is real
Description: The Gain block can give wrong results with certain combinations of complex inputs and real gains. For example, when the gain is a real number and the input is complex with its real part equal to infinity and its imaginary part equal to zero, the imaginary part of the output is wrongly set to NaN.
