Summary: SimElectronics does not appear in the Simulink? Library Browser
Description: When you install the R2008a+ web release of SimElectronics? after using the Simulink?  Library Browser with previously installed Simscape? products, SimElectronics does not appear in the Simulink Library Browser. All other SimElectronics features are available.
Workaround: To work around this issue in R2008a+, perform the following steps:If you have a MATLAB? session open, save your work and close MATLAB.Download the attached files patch.dat, unpatch_simscape.m, and patch_simscape.m and save them in the same directory.Start a new MATLAB session. Do not run any commands or load any models. Do not open the Simulink Library Browser.Navigate to the directory where you saved the downloaded files and run patch_simscape.m at the MATLAB command line.Open the Simulink Library Browser and click on the Simscape entry in the contents tree. This will regenerate the repository, and may take a few minutes to complete.Once the regeneration has completed, SimElectronics will be visible under the Simscape entry in the Simulink Library Browser.

Summary: A model containing AWGN block keeps rebuilding in the accelerator mode when a variable specified on the edit box for the tunable parameter "EbNo" is changed in the workspace.
Description: Changing tunable parameter value should not cause a model to rebuild in the accelerator mode. The rebuild occured because a common variable name, "EbNo ", was used to specify the Eb/No(dB) , Es/No(dB) , SNR(dB) , and Variance  parameters on the block mask dialog. When one mode was chosen, e.g. Signal to noise ratio (Eb/No) , the disabled parameters: Es/No(dB) , SNR(dB) , and Variance , are set to be untunable. However, when the variable "EbNo " value was changed, the rebuild was caused by the detected value changes for the Es/No(dB) , SNR(dB) , or Variance  parameters, even though they were disabled.
Workaround: In R2008a and before, do not use a common parameter to specify the Eb/No(dB), Es/No(dB), SNR(dB), and Variance parameters.

Summary: Scaling information can be missing on a Repeating Sequence Stair block dialog or a Weighted Moving Average block dialog
Description: If the output data type of a Repeating Sequence Stair block or a Weighted Moving Average block is specified as a fixed-point data type with non-trivial scaling, and the output type is specified using the obsolete parameters OutDataType  and OutScaling , then the block dialog displays the wrong data type: the scaling information is missing. The problem affects only information displayed in the block dialog, it does not affect simulation results.

Summary: Calls to the xpcGetElapsedTime function from a custom device driver might cause the target PC kernel to stop responding
Description: Calls to the xpcGetElapsedTime  function from a custom device driver might cause the target PC kernel to stop responding. You will only see this issue if you compile and download these models with the Open Watcom compiler.
Workaround: As a workaround,Do not use the return value from xpcGetElapsedTime.Instead, pass a pointer to an xpcTime structure.Convert the nanosecond return value to seconds. For example: real_T timestamp; xpcTime newtime; xpcGetElapsedTime( &newtime );timestamp = ((double)newtime.U64.NanoSecondsHi * 65536.0 * 65536.0 + (double)newtime.U64.NanoSecondsLo)/1e9;

Summary: Certain Designated Computer licenses do not allow you to use the local scheduler
Description: With some Designated Computer licenses, the local scheduler feature of Parallel Computing Toolbox fails to correctly start the MATLAB worker processes. The result of this bug is that all tasks that are submitted to the local scheduler come back with their State  property set to failed . To check if you are actually encountering this bug, run the following in a cleanly restarted MATLAB session: setSchedulerMessageHandler(@disp)matlabpool open local 1 The result of these commands should output something like the following to your command window. Of particular interest is that the cause of this issue is indicated by the messages License Manager Error -9  and A Designated Computer installation is in use by another user . LOG FILE OUTPUT:License checkout failed.License Manager Error -9This error may occur when:-The hostid of this computer does not match the hostid in the license file.-A Designated Computer installation is in use by another user.If no other user is currently running MATLAB, you may need to activate.Troubleshoot this issue by visiting:https://www.mathworks.com/support/lme/R2008a/9Diagnostic Information:Feature: MATLABLicense path: C:\WINNT\Profiles\username\Application Data\MathWorks\MATLAB\R2008a_licenses;C:\MATLAB\R2008a\licenses\license.dat;C:\MATLAB\R2008a\licenses\*.lic;FLEXnet Licensing error: -9,57.License checkout failed.License Manager Error -9This error may occur when:-The hostid of this computer does not match the hostid in the license file.-A Designated Computer installation is in use by another user.If no other user is currently running MATLAB, you may need to activate.Troubleshoot this issue by visiting:https://www.mathworks.com/support/lme/R2008a/9
Workaround: Contact the MathWorks Customer Support Team for a new license file that will allow local schedulers to work.

Summary: Parallel jobs on WIN32 fail under LSF, PBS Pro, and mpiexec schedulers
Description: Parallel jobs using mpiexec.exe  and smpd.exe  fail to run correctly on WIN32 systems. This affects LSF, PBS Pro, mpiexec, and generic schedulers. When attempting to install the smpd service, you might see a dialog box describing a failure to find "MSVCR80.dll ". When using the mpiexec scheduler, you might see an error message similar to the following: >> pjob.submit??? Error using ==> dct_psfcnsCould not find process 4660 ?
Workaround: Download the attached zip-file containing updated smpd.exe and mpiexec.exe files. Install these files into %MATLABROOT%\bin\win32 on both client and cluster machines. When these files are installed, you can install the smpd service as usual, and the mpiexec scheduler should work correctly.

Summary: Function wrapTo180 should always result in an exact identity for input in [-180 180]
Description: If lon falls in the closed interval [-180 180], then wrapTo180(lon) should be exactly equal to lon. However, in this interval, the output of wrapTo180 differs slightly from the input for some values of lon that are not "round" numbers. (One such number is 115.8323, for example.) The differences are on the order of 2*eps(lon). A corresponding problem occurs with function wrapToPi for some input values in the interval [-pi pi].
Workaround: To work around these issues, replace the wrapTo180.m and wrapToPi.m function files in your current installation with the versions in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/maputils/wrapTo180.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: wrapTo180.m.oldCopy the version of wrapTo180.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Repeat the preceding two steps for wrapToPi.mRestart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB functions using persistent variables cause compilation failures with model reference or Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder
Description: This bug causes compilation failures when the following conditions apply: You have an Embedded MATLAB function that contains persistent variables, but is not inlined in the generated code. This function is called by other functions that do not contain persistent variables Shared utilities are enabled. For model reference, shared utilities are enabled by default. In Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder, you enable shared utilities in the Configuration Parameters dialog.
Workaround: Use eml.inline('always') in the function that contains persistent variables to force it to be inlined.

Summary: Error status initialization appears in generated code when model optimization parameter Remove internal state zero initialization is on
Description: The real-time model (rtM ) data structure contains global data (for example, timing, solver, and logging settings) associated with a root model. If the global data consists of only the global variable ErrorStatus , the code generator ignores the setting of the model configuration parameter Optimization  > Remove internal state zero initialization  when initializing the structure, and generates ErrorStatus initialization code even when the parameter is set to on.

Summary: MEX errors when the environment variable TEMP contains a path with a space.
Description: If you have the environment variable TEMP defined as a path with a space (" ") in the path, then MEX may fail in various ways. MEX may fail to build a MEX-file or MEX -SETUP will fail to setup your preferred MEX compiler. One common error message is: "The system cannot find the file specified."
Workaround: Redefine the TEMP environment variable to a directory that does not have a space in the path.

Summary: ssIsFirstInitCond can be misleading
Description: The S-function macro ssIsFirstInitCond  may be true even after mdlInitConditions  function has been called for the first time. As explained in the documentation, this macro only returns false if the current simulation time is not equal to the start time. It is therefore possible to create a model where mdlInitConditions  is called more than once during the initial time step.
Workaround: If you need to ensure that your data is initialized only once during simulation, then place your initialization code in the mdlStart function rather than the mdlInitializeConditions.

Summary: Model Advisor might generate error when running on subsystem that contains Divide block
Description: An error might occur if both of the following conditions apply: You open the Model Advisor for a subsystem that contains a Divide block. You select and run the check By Product  > Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder  > Identify blocks that generate expensive saturation and rounding code   The error occurs because a subsystem does not have a TargetIntDivRoundTo  parameter. The message that appears when the error occurs is Warning: Unable to perform Model Advisor fixed-point rounding check for 'Product blocks', error occurred: 'Errorusing ==> get_paramInvalid Simulink object specifier.

Summary: Function daspectm may fail when using azimuthal projection or units of radians
Description: If you try to set the scale reference location to the pole when using a polar azimuthal projection, you'll get an error because daspectm  tries to set the DataAspectRatio  property of the axes to zero. Function daspectm  converts the latitude and longitude of the scale reference location to degrees even if the map axes uses angle units of radians. Thus, the effective location may be very different from the one you specified.
Workaround: To work around these issues, replace the daspectm.m function file in your current installation with the version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdisp/daspectm.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: daspectm.m.oldCopy the version of daspectm.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: SW2DTOOL GUI slider controls do not display correctly when GUI maximized
Description: If you maximize the SW2DTOOL GUI window and attempt to change the number of levels on the right side, one or more slider bars appear under the De-noise and Residuals pushbuttons and are unusable.
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the current sw2dtool.m and utthrw2d.m files in your $ROOT\toolbox\wavelet\wavelet directory with the two files in the Attachments section below.

Summary: SW2DTOOL errors when loading a TIF image
Description: If you attempt to load a TIF image into SW2DTOOL, an error occurs.
Workaround: To work around this problem, download the attached file into your $ROOT\toolbox\wavelet\wavelet directory.

Summary: Error dialog displays after changing the limits of an axis to small numbers in plot tools
Description: When using Plotting Tools to change default axis limits to small values (in the order of [.495e-14 to .505e-14]) the limits take effect on the plot, but the display in the plot tools shows zero for both limit values. Then, after clicking the plot itself, an error dialog box displays with text ?Limits are out of order. Previous values were restored.  This error dialog box only closes after clicking on OK  several times. ?

Summary: Compiled applications leak thread handles (one every five seconds) on the Windows platform.
Description: This behavior can be observed using Process Explorer, Task Manager, or another similar tool that displays the number of open handles owned by a particular Windows process. The thread handle leak is related to lock file refreshing used in the MCR component cache. Therefore, only applications compiled with the embedded CTF option (on by default) are affected.
Workaround: Download the patch and copy the file libut.{so|dll|dylib} to the platform specific directories shown below:In MATLAB:/bin/{win32|win64}/In MCR:/bin/{win32|win64}/?

Summary: The colon operator, when used in for loops, does not work on MATLAB classes defined using classdef.
Description: If C  is the name of a class defined in MATLAB R2008a using the classdef  keyword, then the following code produces an error even if class C  defines a colon  method: a = C;b = C;for i = a:b , disp(i), end
Workaround: Using square brackets around the colon expression removes the error:a = C;b = C;for i = [a:b], disp(i), end?

Summary: Start menu shortcuts to NCTool and NPRTool actually call NFTool.
Description: Start menu shortcuts to NCTool and NPRTool actually call NFTool.
Workaround: Call the NNT commands "nctool" and "nprtool" directly fom the command line.

Summary: Opening or updating a model that contains some xPC Target driver blocks with a parameter set with an undefined variable might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Opening or updating a model where some specific parameters are set with a workspace variable that is undefined in some xPC Target driver blocks might cause MATLAB to crash. The following blocks and their parameters might cause this situation: General Standards 16AO16, Frame size  General Standards 24DSI12, Frame size  FIFO ASCII read, Maximum read size  FIFO bin read, Maximum read size  Ascii Encode, Max output string length  and Number of variables  Audio-PMC+ and Audio-PMC+1 (analog input and output), Frame Size  Diamond MM-32 Frame Analog Input, Number of channels  and Number of scans per frame  UEI Frame Analog Input (all board types), Frame size
Workaround: As a workaround, do one of the following:Before you open your model, ensure that you define all variables used as block parameters.To prevent the issue, define default values for variables in a PreLoadFcn callback for your model.The crash on open may also occur if you have created a mask over a subsystem that contains one of these blocks and you are setting one of the named parameters with a variable from the mask.  In that case, you can use set_param in your mask initialization routine to set the parameter in the block instead of using a variable.

Summary: Function uiputfile overwrites an existing file on Linux in R2008a without warning first
Description: The uiputfile dialog function fails to warn before saving a file when the user selects an existing file on Linux in R2008a. The lack of warning makes it easier to inadvertantly overwrite an existing file.

Summary: Calling a void function by handle redundantly prints 'ans' if not terminated with semicolon.
Description: If you call a void function by means of a handle and do not terminate the statement with a semicolon, as shown below, MATLAB redundantly prints 'ans' to console.    ans = 1    x = struct_of_handles;    x.void_func(100)           % No semicolon
Workaround: Terminate the statement with a semicolon.

Summary: Data Type Override option in the Fixed-Point Tool sometimes has no effect
Description: The Data Type Override option in the Fixed-Point Tool (invoked through Tools > Fixed-Point Settings  in R2007b or Tools > Fixed-Point > Fixed-Point Tools  in R2008a) controls how user-specified data types can be overridden. For the Output Data Type  of the Gain, Multiport Switch, Switch, and Relay blocks; and the Parameter Data Type  of the Gain block; the Data Type Override option has no effect if the data type specified by the user is a built-in data type. ?
Workaround: Replace built-in types on which the Data Type Override option has no effect as shown in this table:Built-in TypeReplacementsinglefixdt('single')doublefixdt('double')int8fixdt(1,8,0)uint8fixdt(0,8,0)int16fixdt(1,16,0)uint16fixdt(0,16,0)int32fixdt(1,32,0)uint32fixdt(0,32,0)

Summary: Running PIL on a DSP-BIOS?-enabled application always reports 100% stack usage
Description: When you run PIL on DSP/BIOS-enabled generated code, the stack usage report always indicates that your application used 100% of the system stack. The apparent incorrect stack utilization value occurs because stack profiling conflicts with DSP/BIOS. The profiling mechanism initializes the stack with known values, and then DSP/BIOS overwrites those values with different values. To assess stack usage, the profiling process compares the system stack values it used to initialize the stack to the values in the stack after executing the program. When the process sees new values in all stack locations, either the DSP/BIOS values or execution values, it reports 100% stage usage. Stack profiling cannot determine whether the new values were caused by initialization or were stored and changed during run-time.?
Workaround: To use stack profiling, disable DSP/BIOS in your project and regenerate the code.Double-click the Target Preferences block in your model to open the Target Preferences block dialog box.Select None from the Operating system list.Click OK to close the dialog box.Regenerate your project.Run the project to do the profiling.To use a DSP/BIOS project, you can disable the stack profiling report. Follow these steps to apply a patch that lets you disable stack profiling.Download and unzip the attached file.Extract the files, patch_installer.m and patch_453029.zip, to the MATLAB? root directory.Start MATLAB.Enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch:   patch_installer('patch_453029.zip', 'install')Follow the instructions printed on the MATLAB command line to complete the installation.?

Summary: While using the Accelerator Mode, I encounter the error, "The path 'C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2008a\toolbox\rtw\dspblks\c' does not exist".
Description: When I try to run "vipsurveillance.mdl" in Accelerator Mode, I receive the following error message during the compilation: error ==> accelbuild_private at 31error ==> getPathNameThe path 'C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2008a\toolbox\rtw\dspblks\c' does not exist This problem only occurs when Real-Time Workshop is not installed, and the MATLAB installation folder contains spaces. ?
Workaround: To resolve this issue, please replace the rtwmakeconfig.m file located in the $MATLABROOT/toolbox/vipblks/vipmex/ directory with the file contained in the zip file attached to this bug report.$MATLABROOT/toolbox/vipblks/vipmex/rtwmakeconfig.m?

Summary: Compiling shared library with mcc fails on Windows using MSVC 9.0.
Description: Compiling shared library with mcc  fails to create a .def  file on Windows using MSVC 9.0 during the mbuild  step. Users see the following error: '-e' is not recognized as an internal or external command,operable program or batch file.  ? ?
Workaround: To workaround this problem, perform the following steps:(1) Back up <install area>\matlab\bin\win64\mbuildopts\msvc90compp.bat.(2) Download the attached msvc90compp.bat file and place in <install area>\matlab\bin\win64\mbuildopts.(3) Run mbuild -setup.?

Summary: Function uiputfile adds '*' to file name if the filter used is '*'
Description: If the filter for uiputfile  is specified as '*' , the file saved has .*  appended to its filename. [filename]=uiputfile('*','Select a File for Testing') produces filename = myfile.* The extension '.*'  is incorrect.
Workaround: Use '*.*' for the filter.

Summary: Logging nested bus signals may fail to log some elements within the nested bus
Description: When a model logs a bus signal that contains a nested bus, the logsout  structure may omit some elements of the nested bus if: The source of the nested bus is a Model block. The port that outputs the nested bus is not the Model block's first output port.
Workaround: Insert signal conversion blocks (with output set to "Bus copy") at the output ports of the model block that output a nested bus. This way, all elements of the nested bus will be logged.

Summary: Using the Microsoft Visual Studio 2005 (v8.0) C/C++ compiler to build a target application might cause loss of communication between host PC and target PC (TCP/IP timeouts) when the target application is downloaded
Description: Using the Microsoft Visual Studio 2005 (v8.0) C/C++ compiler to build a target application might cause loss of communication between host PC and target PC (TCP/IP timeouts) when the target application is downloaded. This issue most often occurs: For certain sized target applications When the target application is downloaded after another application has been previously downloaded to the same target PC  When a target application is the first downloaded target application to the target PC, this behavior does not occur.
Workaround: As a workaround, perform one of the following:If you have xPC Target version 3.4 (R2008a), run xpcbench with the reboot option. For example: xpcbench('this','-reboot')If you have an xPC Target version earlier than 3.4 (R2008a), try to use another compiler supported by xPC Target. For example: Microsoft Visual Studio (v6.0) C/C++ compilerMicrosoft Visual Studio .NET 2003 (v7.1) C/C++ compilerOpen Watcom (v1.3) C/C++ compiler

Summary: Assigning MCOS objects into arrays may discard recent property value changes in the assigned object.
Description: Assigning MCOS objects into arrays may discard recent property value changes in the assigned object. This only affects value classes; handle classes and arrays of handles are not affected. In this example, the Scaled property is set to true, but then reverts to false:    classdef myclass       properties          Scaled = false;       end    end    function x = myfun(Data)    D = myclass;    D.Scaled = true;    Data(1) = D;    x = Data.Scaled;       % x reverts to false
Workaround: Disable JITing of MCOS using "feature jitallow mcos off".

Summary: Callout legends missing in PDF reports on Stateflow? charts.
Description: The number images for callouts for Stateflow? charts do not appear in the legend in PDF reports.
Workaround: R2006bBack up matlab/sys/namespace/docbook/v4/xsl/fo/param.xsl.Download param_6b.xsl and rename to param.xsl.Copy renamed file to matlab/sys/namespace/docbook/v4/xsl/fo/.Download callouts.zip and extract contents to a temporary directory.Copy extracted GIF files to matlab/sys/namespace/docbook/v4/xsl/images/callouts/.Back up matlab/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rpt_xml/@db_output/convertReport.p.Download convertReport.p.Copy downloaded file to matlab/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rpt_xml/@db_output/.Download urn2file.p.Copy downloaded file to matlab/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rptgen/.Download MS_Gothic_vista.xml.Copy downloaded file to matlab/sys/namespace/docbook/v4/xsl/tools/fontmetrics/.R2007a and laterBackup matlab/sys/namespace/docbook/v4/xsl/fo/param.xsl.Download param_7a.xsl and rename to param.xsl.Copy renamed file to matlab/sys/namespace/docbook/v4/xsl/fo/.Download convertReport.p.Copy downloaded file to matlab/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rpt_xml/@db_output/.Download urn2file.p.Copy downloaded file to matlab/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rptgen/.Download MS_Gothic_vista.xml.Copy downloaded file to matlab/sys/namespace/docbook/v4/xsl/tools/fontmetrics/.

Summary: One dimensional colon indexing can return incorrect values instead of erroring out.
Description: The following code does not throw an out of bounds error, even though it should:    A = rand(3);    t = A(1:100);     % out of bounds subscript
Workaround: To work around this bug, turn off the MATLAB Accelerator as shown here:   feature accel off

Summary: Attempting to save, in an earlier version format of Simulink, a Simulink model that contains a Signal Builder block whose dialog box is open, creates a model that generates warnings when opened in earlier version of Simulink
Description: Attempting to save, in an earlier version format of Simulink, a Simulink model that contains a Signal Builder block whose dialog box is open, creates a model that generates warnings when you try to open it in earlier version of Simulink. The warning will be: Warning: Error evaluating 'LoadFcn' callback of Sigbuilder block block (mask) 'Signal Builder'. Reference to non-existent field 'allXData'.
Workaround: As a workaround, do one of the following:Close the Signal Builder block GUI, then save model in older version format.Download the attached files and update your MATLAB installation, as follows:Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file: /toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/create_save_struct.p/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/dataSet_store.p/toolbox/simulink/simulink/slsaveassup.mTo see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: CONTOURF may assign colors to NaN regions
Description: Execute the following: a = [1 2 3 2; 1 3 NaN 3; 4 4 4 4; 10000 1 1 1];levels = 0:2;contourf(a, levels);  Notice that the diamond-shaped NaN region is blue.

Summary: FINDPEAKS fails to deploy with MATLAB Compiler
Description: The findpeaks function throws a runtime error when run in a standalone application compiled with the MATLAB Compiler: ??? Error using ==> fevalUndefined function or variable 'dspopts.findpeaks'. Error in ==> uddpvparse at 96 Error in ==> findpeaks at 43
Workaround: The following line needs to be added just above the uddpvparse command in matlab/toolbox/signal/signal/findpeaks.m:%#function dspopts.findpeaks

Summary: The axes created while using the plotyy function will disappear when the "Units" property of the axes is set to anything other than "Normalized".
Description: If the "Units" property of an axes created using the plotyy  command is changed to anything other than "Normalized", as in the following code:figure;ax = plotyy(1:10,rand(1,10),1:10,rand(1,10)*20);set(ax,'Units','Points'); The axes will disappear.

Summary: The textscan function may crash when it tries to read numeric data that contains the word 'Infinity'.
Description: The more general problem is that the algorithm to convert from real-valued to complex data type in the middle of reading a large amount of data, was incorrectly allocating memory, leading to a crash. This algorithm happened to be triggered in this case.
Workaround: The best workaround is to divide reading the data into smaller chunks of up to 1024 lines of data.

Summary: Java and MATLAB? software interaction: problems with too many lines in java.opts or classpath.txt files
Description: The MATLAB program terminates unexpectedly if there are too many lines in the java.opts  file. MATLAB produces a warning if there are too many lines in the Java classpath.txt  file. There is a fixed limit on the number of options that can be passed to Sun Microsystems software from MATLAB. If this limit is exceeded, MATLAB might crash. There is a fixed limit on the number of characters that can be included in the Java classpath  passed to Java from MATLAB. MATLAB ignores characters beyond the limit and issues a warning.

Summary: File menu not visible on Ubuntu glnx86 platforms
Description: If you start the MATLAB? program on Ubuntu version 7.04 platforms using the glnx86  flag, the MATLAB File  menu does not appear.
Workaround: Try either of these workarounds:Use a different window manager on Ubuntu.Use a newer JRE. The JRE supplied with MATLAB version 7.5 (R2007b) is JRE 1.6.0. The problem was resolved in JRE 1.6.0_03. Install JRE 1.6.0_03 or later for use with MATLAB.

Summary: Error attempting to simulate SimMechanics model before opening visualization window
Description: An error can occur when you attempt to simulate SimMechanics models if: The model's Configuration Parameters  (SimMechanics  pane) specify to show animation during simulation, and A visualization window for the model is not already open when you start the simulation.  The complete list of conditions triggering this bug is unknown. Apparent triggers include: The presence of multiple machines The presence of a Joint Spring & Damper block  A known type of error is: Error evaluating function mech_compilesupport: Error using ==> get_param Invalid Simulink object handle. However, other failure modes, including a MATLAB crash, are possible.
Workaround: There are several workarounds.The simplest workaround is to open a visualization window before beginning the simulation. You can do this most easily by entering Ctrl-D (Update Diagram) from your keyboard with the Simulink model window in focus. The visualization window must be open at the instant you start the simulation. If you later close the visualization window, the problem will return.If it is not necessary to animate during simulation, then clearing the animation check box in the Configuration Parameters/SimMechanics pane also eliminates the problem. With animation disabled, the simulation proceeds normally regardless of whether a visualization window is opened or not.You can also work around this problem in R2006a and later versions of MATLAB and Simulink by applying a code patch.To implement the code patch workaround, follow these steps.In your MATLAB installation, backup the original version of the file matlab/toolbox/physmod/mech/mech/mech_hgdraw.p.Download the code file patch appropriate to your version of MATLAB:R2006a or R2006b: mech_hgdraw_R2006ab.pR2007a: mech_hgdraw_R2007a.pR2007b: mech_hgdraw_R2007b.pR2008a: mech_hgdraw_R2008a.pRename the downloaded patch file to mech_hgdraw.p (i.e., remove the version specifier suffix). Place it in the directory matlab/toolbox/physmod/mech/mech.If MATLAB is already running, apply the change at the command line by entering rehash toolboxcache. Alternatively, closing and restarting MATLAB will also apply the workaround.

Summary: uiimport and importdata do not work on Excel XLSX files.
Description: The uiimport  and importdata  functions do not support the new Excel format, XLSX.
Workaround: In MATLAB versions 7.6 and earlier, you must change the filename extension of these files from .XLSX to .XLS.

Summary: System error resulting in a corrupted FIG-file when building a GUI in GUIDE
Description: When building GUIs in GUIDE, saving the GUI or performing edits in GUIDE can generate an exception that displays in a MATLAB System Error dialog, and result in a corrupted GUI FIG-file file. The corrupted GUI FIG-file cannot be restored, causing the GUI to become unusable; if you attempt to open the GUI, the following error might display: ??? Error using ==> hgload at 53 invalid Figure file format
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the version of the guidemfile.m function in your current R2008a installation with the updated version attached, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\guide\guidemfile.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, guidemfile.m.OLD.Download the attached file, guidemfile.m, storing it the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.The workaround prevents the corruption of new GUIs. It does not repair old GUIs that are already corrupted.

Summary: Repeating Sequence block may output incorrect results
Description: In R2008a, the Repeating Sequence block may output incorrect results if you tune parameters during simulation.
Workaround: If your model contains a Repeating Sequence block, avoid tuning the block's parameters during simulation.

Summary: Repeatedly running parfor leads to increased memory usage on workers
Description: Repeatedly executing parfor -loops with the MATLAB pool open leads to increased memory usage on the workers. This is particularly noticeable when working with large arrays.
Workaround: The attached patch significantly slows the increase in memory usage on the MATLAB workers when executing multiple parfor-loops. To install the patch, perform these steps on the MATLAB client and the workers:Rename the file matlabroot/toolbox/distcomp/remoteParallelFunction.m to matlabroot/toolbox/distcomp/remoteParallelFunction.org.m where matlabroot is the directory in which the MATLAB client or the worker is installed. On the MATLAB client, matlabroot can be determined by running matlabroot in the Command Window.Download the attached file and save it as  matlabroot/toolbox/distcomp/remoteParallelFunction.mRestart MATLAB. Also restart the MATLAB workers running under the jobmanager, which you can do by stopping and restarting the mdce service.

Summary: The polyspace daemon sometimes errors out with a 'match exception' message
Description: Submitting a remote job can sometimes cause the following error: Adding the analysis to the queue ... Error: An I/O error occured in connection. The Queue Manager log file shows : 29-Feb-2008, 16:53:05 : Addition of project "My_project" (lang=C ; author=someone) : ID=1241Error: An I/O error occured in connection.Error: An I/O error occured in connection.Error: The daemon is not running on the server server_pst. polyspaced starts on the machine server_pst, but fails when a job is assigned to it with the following error (from the log file): Listening to port 12427 ...exception Match(Match) raised.raised at: polyspaced.sml:2512.50 polyspaced.sml:30998.17 This problem is a robustness issue caused when the server receives invalid data from the network. This issue is highly sporadic and not easy to reproduce (the verification will run most of the time if it is relaunched).

Summary: Changing component stacking order in GUIDE can cause components with Visibility 'off' to disappear
Description: In GUIDE, a component should always remain visible in the Layout Editor even if you set its Visible  property to 'off'  with the Property Inspector. However, if you change the stacking order of the components, for example, using Layout -> Send to back , the component with Visibility off  disappears. You can click over the invisible component, and it will partially appear, with handles. You can resize, move, and inspect its properties, but it will not have a normal appearance until you restore its Visible  property to 'on' .
Workaround: Before using any GUIDE command to change object stacking order, such as Send To Back, make sure the components in your GUI have their Visibility set to 'on'. After you have changed the stacking order, reset the visibility settings back to 'off' for those components that originally had their visibility off.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop may fail to regenerate code after you modify an MATLAB file used from an Embedded MATLAB Function block.
Description: Real-Time Workshop may fail to detect changes in external MATLAB files used from within an Embedded MATLAB Function block. As a result, it may incorrectly use cached information and thus generate code for the old version
Workaround: Force a rebuild by using one of these methods:Remove the slprj and sfprj directories if they are present.Modify all Embedded MATLAB Function blocks that call the modified MATLAB file.

Summary: The To Audio Device block reports that the audio device has become unresponsive
Description: The To Audio Device block of the Signal Processing Blockset can intermittently produce the following error: "Error reported by S-function 'sdspToAudioDevice'Audio output device has become unresponsive." This error is most often encountered after opening the Simulink Library Browser. ?
Workaround: To work around this issue, first rename the original files listed below, and then replace them with the files in the zip file attached to this bug report.bin/win32/libaudiodevice.dllbin/win32/libportaudio.dlltoolbox/dspblks/dspmex/sdspToAudioDevice.mexw32toolbox/dspblks/dspmex/sdspFromAudioDevice.mexw32toolbox/dspblks/dspmex/dspAudioDeviceInfo.mexw32

Summary: CAGE items used multiple times in a strategy do not parse correctly
Description: If you use a single From block in the CAGE Project library as an input to more than one block in a feature, then the feature will not parse. The error message suggests that the likely cause is an algebraic loop. The following steps describe a simple reproduction of this problem. 1. Create variables A, B, C in CAGE.2. Create a new feature.3. Feed B and C into tables T1 and T2.4. Construct feature out = T1 + T2 * A (for an illustration, see the file feature.png  that is listed in the Attachments section)
Workaround: To avoid this problem, use multiple copies of the From block. Do not use a From block as an input to more than one block.Alternatively, a patch is available that will fix this error in Version 3.4. To install this fix:1. Rename the file <matlabroot>/toolbox/mbc/mbcexpr/@cgslparser/FromCAGE.m to <matlabroot>/toolbox/mbc/mbcexpr/@cgslparser/FromCAGE.m.old. 2. Download the file FromCAGE.m that is listed in the Attachments section and save it to <matlabroot>/toolbox/mbc/mbcexpr/@cgslparser/FromCAGE.m. 3. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Simscape node in the Simulink Library Browser shows incorrect libraries
Description: Simscape node in the Simulink Library Browser shows incorrect libraries. This typically happens when you add or remove a Simscape vertical product (SimHydraulics, SimMechanics, SimDriveline, SimElectronics) after installing Simscape. Simscape node in the Simulink Library Browser may show more libraries than you have installed or it may not show some of the libraries that you have installed.
Workaround: Perform the following steps to update the Simscape node in the Simulink Library Browser to show the correct entries:Download the attached patch.dat, patch_simscape.m and unpatch_simscape.m files and save them all in the same directory.Save your work and close the MATLAB session if you have it open.Start MATLAB. Do not run any commands or load any models. Please do not open the Simulink Library Browser.Go to the directory where you downloaded the files.Run patch_simscape.m at the MATLAB command line to apply the patch.Start the Simulink Library Browser.Click on the Simscape node to regenerate the repository. Regenerating the repository may take a few minutes to complete but is typically less than 1 minute.After applying the patch, the Simulink Library Browser will automatically regenerate the Simscape repository and display the correct entries if there are future changes to your installation. The patch only needs to be applied once.This issue was fixed as part of the bug fixes to the R2008a versions of core Simulink products.

Summary: Classes whose names end with the name of a MATLAB built-in class can cause errors
Description: Defining a class having a name that ends with the name of a MATLAB built-in class can lead to unexpected/unpredictable behavior. For example, the class name, foodouble , ends in the same name as a MATLAB built-in class, double , and therefore can cause unexpected behavior.  Classes named foosingle , foouint8 , foochar , etc., also lead to potentially unpredictable behavior. The unpredictable behavior sometimes takes the form of the following error  ??? Undefined command/function 'functionName '.  Where functionName   is a method of the class, but the actual operation being executed is an operation of the built-in class. For example  ??? Undefined command/function 'plus'.  Where @xyzdouble/plus.m  is defined but the double version of the plus operation is being executed.
Workaround: The workaround is to rename the class such that its name does not end with the name of a MATLAB built-in class.

Summary: Anchors not created for Mask blocks causing broken links in the Simulink Summary Table
Description: Anchors are not created for Mask blocks causing links to not work in the Simulink Summary Table.
Workaround: Backup and replace the file at:Matlab/toolbox/simulink/components/@rptgen_sl/@csl_sys_snap/gr_preCreateAction.pThis patch works with releases R2006b. There is no patch available for other releases.

Summary: When running Simulink on a Unix system, text in some menus and dialog boxes displayed by the Block Diagram Editor appear as boxes, rather than as legible words
Description: Simulink asks the X Server for a font matching *-helvetica-*  and assumes the font uses an ASCII character set (iso8859). The menu is unreadable if the font instead uses a Unicode character set (UCS, iso10646).
Workaround: Arrange for the X Server to find an iso8859 font when asked for a font matching *-helvetica-. Doing so is system dependent, so consult your system's manual pages for X, mkfontdir, and xset. Here is one possible procedure:1. Run xset q to see the directories on the font path.2. Each directory on the font path should have a fonts.dir file. The first line tells how many fonts are listed. Each line after that lists a filename and a font name. Look in the fonts.dir files for a font name with "helvetica" after the second dash and "iso8859" before the last dash. For example, a fonts.dir file might contain a line like:helvO10-ISO8859-1.pcf.gz -adobe-helvetica-medium-o-normal--14-100-100-100-p-78-iso8859-13. Create a new directory <dir>, and copy the corresponding file to that directory. In the above example, you would copy file helvO10-ISO8859-1.pcf.gz to the new directory.4. Run mkfontdir <dir> to create <dir>/fonts.dir.5. Run xset +fp <dir> to reload the font database.?

Summary: The bloomberg.fetch method is not compilable.
Description: The bloomberg.fetch method is not compilable, due to an issue with an internal call to dftool.
Workaround: Copy the attached version of fetch.m to $MATLAB/toolbox/datafeed/datafeed/@bloomberg.

Summary: Documentation errors for the use of the import command include the following:You cannot import constant properties.The import command does not support the class key word.
Description: The MATLAB documentation sections, Importing Class Members  and Defining Named Constants , contain errors with respect to using the import  command. Examples that uses the following code to import class members do not work: import class package.class.* % class keyword in not supported  Also, the constants defined in the Constants.AstroConstants  class cannot be imported. The following code does not work: import class Constants.AstroConstants.* % Cannot import constant properties  MATLAB Version 7.6 does not support the importing of constant properties (properties with the Constant  attribute set to true ).
Workaround: You must use the fully qualified class name to refer to constant properties. For example:a = Constants.AstroConstants.G;assigns the value of the property G in the class AstroConstants, which is in the package Constants, to the value to a.To import a class that is contained in a package directory:import package.classnameTo import all classes and functions from a package, you can use a wildcard:import package.*

Summary: Right click on multi-line edit box does not display context menu
Description: When right-clicking within multiline edit box in a MATLAB GUI, its context menu, if it has one, does not display.

Summary: A multi-rate S-Function used in a model referenced in Normal mode may produce incorrect results
Description: A multi-rate S-Function may produce incorrect results if: The S-Function is used in a model referenced in Normal mode. The referenced model uses a Multitasking solver. The parent model uses a variable-step solver or a Singletasking fixed-step solver.
Workaround: Set all parents of the referenced model that contains the S-Function to use a Multitasking solver.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Real-Time Workshop might generate incorrect code for nested action subsystems
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process generates incorrect code for action subsystems if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains nested subystems with action blocks (for example, a Switch Case block with action subsystems, at least one of which contains another Switch Case block with action subsystems). Inside the action subsystems, the Action Port block parameter States when execution is resumed  is set to reset , which means that the states of the action subsystem is reinitialized when the subsystem is reenabled. The nested action subsystems contain blocks with states, such as Unit Delay blocks.  If all of the listed conditions apply, the resulting generated code does not reset the states of the nested action subsystems if the parent action subsystem resumes after being disabled.

Summary: Math Function block can incorrectly produce an error when configured with heterogeneous input and output data types
Description: When you configure the Math Function block with heterogeneous input and output data types, the block can behave as follows for the magnitude^2, square, sqrt, and reciprocal functions: Give wrong answers in Normal simulation mode Incorrectly produce an error in code generation modes
Workaround: Configure the Math Function block with homogeneous input and output data types. Then insert Data Type Conversion blocks to convert data types to the desired types.

Summary: Some MATLAB instrument drivers generated from an IVI instrument driver cannot access multiple items in a collection data type
Description: The Instrument Control Toolbox provides the capability to automatically generate a MATLAB instrument driver from an IVI instrument driver. You can then use the MATLAB instrument driver together with the IVI instrument driver to communicate with the instrument using MATLAB. (See https://www.mathworks.com/ivi for additional information on this functionality.) Some MATLAB instrument drivers generated from an IVI instrument driver in R2008a cannot access any information other than the first item in a collection. If the IVI instrument driver does not use collection data types, this problem probably does not apply to you. As an example, using the MATLAB instrument driver called AgilentSAn.mdd generated from with the IVI instrument driver in MATLAB R2008a will generate the following error when attempting to communicate with an Agilent signal analyzer: >> deviceObject = icdevice('AgilentSAn.mdd','TCPIP::172.31.57.119::INSTR','options','');>> connect(deviceObject);  >> var1 = invoke(deviceObject.BasicWaveformtraces(2),'Read',10000);  Index exceeds matrix dimensions  ? ? ?
Workaround: 1. Install the driver compliance package called R2008a_2_43_runtime.zip:>> cd(matlabroot)>> unzip <path>\<to>\R2008a_2_43_runtime.zip>> rehash toolbox2. Acquire from The MathWorks, the instrument manufacturer, etc. a MATLAB instrument driver for your instrument generated from the IVI driver.Note: If you need to generate this MATLAB instrument driver yourself (if the driver is not provided to you), you also need to install the R2008a_2_43_generation.zip before generating the driver.

Summary: Categorical array labels contain leading spaces.
Description: When creating a categorical array from numeric data, if you do not specify labels as the second input argument to the nominal or ordinal constructor, the automatically-generated labels may contain leading spaces. For example:>> a = nominal([1 12 123])a =1 12 123>> getlabels(a)ans =' 1' ' 12' '123' This behavior occurs only when the strings representing the unique values in the numeric data are of different lengths.
Workaround: Specify the desired labels as the second input argument.For example:>> a = nominal([1 12 123],{'low','medium','high'})a =low medium high>> getlabels(a)ans ='low' 'medium' 'high'

Summary: Browsing a model containing configurable subsystems can crash MATLAB
Description: Using the Simulink Model Browser to traverse various levels in a model that includes a library link containing configurable subsystems can crash MATLAB.

Summary: User-defined windows in FDATool produce an error
Description: In FDATool if you use the FIR design method and specify a user-defined window, an error occurs.

Summary: Compiled application terminates with a segment violation on 64-bit Linux in mkl.so when doing left division.
Description: When doing a left divide on 64-bit Linux, compiled applications sometimes terminate with segment faults in mkl.so  (optimized Intel blas package). ?
Workaround: If application performance is not an issue, the failure can be avoided by using the reference blas shared library.To do this, set the BLAS_VERSION environment variable prior to launching the compiled application.For csh,setenv BLAS_VERSION refblas.so?

Summary: Multiport Switch block does not support out-of-bounds inputs in minor time steps
Description: The Multiport Switch block does not support out-of-bounds inputs in minor time steps. Out-of-bounds inputs are not allowed in major time steps. However, in minor time steps, these inputs can be necessary to detect zero crossings.

Summary: johnsrnd errors for equally-spaced normal quantiles
Description: The quantiles  input to johnsrnd  may be a 2-by-4 matrix whose first row contains four equally-spaced standard normal quantiles, and whose second row contains the corresponding quantiles of the desired distribution. Even when you create the first row using linspace , and the four normal quantiles are as equally-spaced as possible, johnsrnd  may error. For example, qn = linspace(-2,2,4);  p = normcdf(qn);  qx = quantile(randn(1000,1),p)  [r,type,coefs] = johnsrnd([qn;qx],0)  returns the error ??? Error using ==> johnsrnd at 97 The first row of QUANTILES must contain evenly-spaced normal quantiles.
Workaround: Modify the line in johnsrnd that readselseif ~(range(diffz) < eps(max(diffz)))(line 96 in version 1.1.4.1) to readelseif ~(range(diffz) <= 10*eps(max(diffz)))

Summary: Signal Builder block performance has slowed
Description: Creating and viewing data in the Signal Builder block takes longer than in previous releases.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download and install the attached files.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file: /toolbox/shared/sigbldr/@SigSuite/SigSuite.p/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/@SigSuiteGroup/SigSuiteGroup.p/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/@SigSuiteSign?al/SigSuiteSignal.pTo see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Receive connection warnings when starting MATLAB.
Description: When starting MATLAB, you might receive warnings like this: Mar 4, 2008 12:34:27 PM org.apache.commons.httpclient.HttpMethodDirector executeWithRetryINFO: I/O exception (java.net.ConnectException) caught when processing request: Connection refusedMar 4, 2008 12:34:27 PM org.apache.commons.httpclient.HttpMethodDirector executeWithRetryINFO: Retrying request Note: This is not an error. It is a harmless informational message having to do with proxy authentication.
Workaround: To workaround this issue:1. Create a text file named java.opts in the $MATLABROOT/bin/$ARCH directory (or in the MATLAB startup directory).Note: $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB installation directory and $ARCH is your system architecture. To find the MATLAB installation directory on your machine, type matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.2. Open the java.opts text file and put the following lines of text in the file:-Dorg.apache.commons.logging.Log=org.apache.commons.logging.impl.SimpleLog-Dorg.apache.commons.logging.simplelog.log.org.apache.commons.httpclient=error

Summary: Minimum and Maximum blocks can error when back-propagating signal dimensions
Description: When the input to a Minimum or Maximum block is a Simulink 1-D signal and the block is configured to find the extremum over each column, the output of the block is a 1-D signal of size one. However, if that output dimension is back-propagated, the block gives the following error: Output port dimensions are not valid for specified Row, Column or Dimension parameter value. The Minimum and Maximum blocks should not error for this case.
Workaround: You can work around this problem when you know the dimensions of the input to the block. You can insert a Simulink Signal Specification block on the input line of the Minimum or Maximum block in your model, and set the Dimension parameter to the known dimension.

Summary: UserData from a timeseries DataInfo object from a MATLAB 7.1 timeseries object is not restored in MATLAB 7.3-7.6.
Description: If a MATLAB 7.1 timeseries object contains DataInfo  object with a non-empty UserData  property, that property will not be restored when the object is loaded in MATLAB 7.3 or beyond.
Workaround: For MATLAB 7.4-7.6, use the attached MATLAB file loadobj.m to replace the file of the same name in the directory matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/timeseries/@tsdata/@timeseries. You will need to restart MATLAB for the changes to take effect.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/timeseries/@tsdata/@timeseries/loadobj.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, loadobj.m.OLD.Download the attached file, loadobj.m, storing it the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: When using the PolySpace Model Link SL product, the main generator does not consider public variables as potentially written.
Description: When using the PolySpace Model Link SL product, the option -main-generator-writes-variables is set to 'none ', not to 'public '. Therefore, the global public variables are not considered as full range. For this reason, public variables are not tested exhaustively. ?
Workaround: Attached is a modified polyspace_update_config_file.m that will set the main-generator-writes-variables option correctly.Note that following workaround is only available for release R2008a.To workaround this issue: 1. Browse to the Polyspace installation folder (C:\Polyspace by default) 2. Create a backup copy of the following file: <installation_folder>\PolySpace_Common\PolySpaceModelLink\private\polyspace_update_config_file.m 3. Download the attached file attachment_450200_client_server_8a_win_2008-06-19.zip. 4. Unzip the file in the installation folder (see point 1) and confirm the replacement

Summary: The grpstats method for dataset arrays errors when grouping variables are not specified
Description: The dataset array grpstats  method errors out if you do not specify one or more grouping variables in the second input argument. Typically, the second input argument is used to compute statistics on groups of observations within your data, but it is not required. Omitting it should compute statistics across all observations.
Workaround: Modify line 82 (in the current version, 1.1.4.1) of the file dsgrpstats.m in the ${MATLABROOT}/toolbox/stats/private directory to readgroup = ones(a_nobs,1);

Summary: MATLAB crashes when two or more physical signal input ports are connected together in the absence of an output port
Description: If you connect two or more physical signal input ports, but do not connect them to an output port, MATLAB will crash.
Workaround: Remove the connection lines connecting the input ports together.

Summary: bitshift function no longer obeys the RoundMode property of the input fi object
Description: Prior to R2007b, the bitshift  function obeyed both the RoundMode  and OverflowMode  properties of the input fi  object. In R2007b the behavior of the Fixed-Point Toolbox? bitshift  function changed such that the RoundMode  is always floor . However, the documentation was not updated to reflect this change until R2008b. If you are using an R2007b or an R2008a version of Fixed-Point Toolbox software, the documentation incorrectly states that the bitshift  function obeys the RoundMode  of the input fi  object. It should say the RoundMode  of the bitshift  function is always Floor .

Summary: Font size bleeds when modifying zoom factor in Stateflow subcharts
Description: Modifying the zoom factor within a subchart may cause two issues when you go back up to the parent. Issue 1:If you return immediately to the subchart and then zoom, the label of the subchart ignores the new zoom factor. Issue 2:If you un-subchart  the state, the transition labels and the tail of the default transition retain the subchart zoom factor instead of using the zoom factor defined in the chart.
Workaround: For Issue 1:Modify the subchart zoom factor.Go up to the parent.Open the subchart.For Issue 2:Adjust the chart zoom factor using the R, F, V, or spacebar shortcuts.

Summary: Unable to deploy applications that contain uitables
Description: You can build a deployed application that contains one or more uitables. However, the deployed application fails when you attempt to run it with the following error message: com.mathworks.jmi.MatlabException: Undefined function or method 'arrayviewfunc' for input arguments of type 'hg.uitable'
Workaround: Download and extract the attached patch archive into your matlabroot directory, restart MATLAB and then recompile your code. The patch does not overwrite any installed files.matlabroot is the directory path returned when you typematlabrootat the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Partially displayed blocks in the Simulink Library Browser can degrade CPU performance
Description: Simulink Library Browser repeatedly rerenders blocks that do not entirely fit into the Browser window, degrading CPU performance, especially on single-processor machines.
Workaround: Keeping blocks entirely in view prevents or stops the problem. If the CPU load on a single-processor machine is 100% and the user interface is unresponsive, try the following steps:Maximize or resize the Library Browser so that all blocks fit or so that no blocks only partially fit. This is easiest to accomplish in Grid Layout view.Minimize the browser (so that it is, for example, only an icon in the task bar). This should free up the CPU.Restore the browser.Switch the browser to Grid Layout view to facilitate keeping blocks entirely in view.

Summary: Several Image Processing Toolbox functions do not work correctly while figure mode (Zoom/Pan) is active.
Description: Several interactive Image Processing Toolbox functions do not work correctly when called with figures who have an active figure mode such as "Zoom In", "Zoom Out", "Pan", "Rotate 3D", etc. These functions return the following error when called on a target figure after a user has activated one of these figure level interactive modes: >> imshow('peppers.png');>> zoom on >> impixelinfo ??? Error using ==> iptaddcallback at 69 iptaddcallback cannot set callbacks on figure with an active mode. Error in ==> impixelinfoval at 79 callbackID = iptaddcallback(hFig,'WindowButtonMotionFcn', @displayPixelInfo); Error in ==> impixelinfo>createPanel at 143 hPixelInfoValue = impixelinfoval(hPixInfoPanel,imageHandles);Error in ==> impixelinfo at 86 hPixInfoPanel = createPanel;  Functions that are potentially affected by this issue include: imcrop         imdisplayrange  imellipse     imfreehand     imline          impixelinfoimpixelinfoval impoint         impolyimrect         roifill         roipoly
Workaround: To workaround this issue, deactivate the figure mode before calling the Image Processing Toolbox functions.This can be done interactively by clicking the mode button on the figure window, or programmatically, as in the following example which uses "Zoom In" mode:>> imshow('peppers.png'); >> zoom on >> % user zooms the image >> zoom off >> impixelinfo

Summary: Compile error when using custom storage classes with Lookup Table (2-D)
Description: The code generated for a Lookup Table (2-D) may be uncompilable if all the following are true: The Table  parameter is specified using a custom storage class. Inline Parameters  is On . Both block input signals are integers. Both the Row index input values  and the Column index input values  are evenly spaced with a spacing of 1.
Workaround: Select a storage class other than Auto for the Row index input values or the Column index input values, or a non-custom storage class for the Table data.

Summary: Macintosh? platforms: starting the MATLAB? program by double-clicking the icon in the installation folder might start the MATLAB program that is in a different installation folder
Description: If you start the MATLAB program on an Apple? Macintosh platform by double-clicking the MATLAB icon in the installation folder, and if you have more than one installation folder for MATLAB V7.6 (R2008a), the MATLAB program that starts might not be for the one in the installation folder in which you double-clicked.
Workaround: If you do not need more than one installation of MATLAB V7.6 (R2008a), remove the installation you do not need.If you do want more than one installation of MATLAB V7.6 (R2008a), change the file associations so that the correct MATLAB starts. To do so, follow these instructions:In the Finder, select bin/maci/StartMATLAB.smat for one of the MATLAB V7.6 (R2008a) installations.Select File > Get Info.In the resulting Get Info dialog box, for Open with, select Other.Navigate to and select bin/maci/Start MATLAB Settings for the same MATLAB V7.6 (R2008a) installation.Close the Get Info dialog box.Repeat the instructions for each MATLAB V7.6 (R2008a) installation.

Summary: Selecting a new target in Pattern Matching demo errors out
Description: Closing the target selection figure for the Pattern Matching demo errors out.
Workaround: You can correct this problem by replacing the vippattern_gettemplate.m file in the $matlabroot/toolbox/vipblks/vipdemos directory with the file extracted from attachment_449421_20080408.zip attached to this bug report.

Summary: Class definition files using many method prototypes can cause strange behavior, including possible MATLAB crashes.
Description: The new MATLAB object system allows methods to be described by prototypes with the body of the method specified elsewhere. A bug causes problems when you have more than 16 prototypes in a class definition and attempt to edit the file. The bug causes any further FUNCTION keywords in the class definition to appear to be invalid. In some cases, the attempt to recover from this situation is unsuccessful, leading MATLAB to crash. This bug affects the MATLAB editor (more specifically, M-Lint) but does not affect MATLAB.
Workaround: By moving the method block containing the prototypes to the end of the class definition, you can often avoid this problem.

Summary: impoly, imfreehand, roipoly, and roifill error when called from compiled stand-alone application.
Description: When impoly , imfreehand , roipoly , or roifill  are called in a compiled stand-alone application, the following error occurrs: ??? Error using ==> imread at 315File "C:\Temp\imfreehand_test_mcr\toolbox\images\icons\point.png" does not exist. Error in ==> polygonSymbol>makePointInsertCursor at 71Error in ==> polygonSymbol>initialize at 64Error in ==> basicPolygon at 27Error in ==> imfreehand at 189Error in ==> imfreehand_test at 3 ?
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the -a flag to explicitly include the file point.png that is missing in the deployed application.mcc -mv myApplication -a $MATLABROOT/toolbox/images/icons/point.pngIn this example, myApplication is the name of the MATLAB file you wish to compile and $MATLABROOT refers to the root directory of your MATLAB installation, which may be determined by using the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.

Summary: Code generation error when building a model with multiple instances of Convert from Rotation Matrix to Virtual Reality Toolbox block
Description: If a model has two or more instances of the Convert from Rotation Matrix to Virtual Reality Toolbox (RotationMatrix2VR) block, converting the model to code causes the following error: Error building Real-Time Workshop target for block diagram 'test2'. MATLAB error message:Error using ==> genMakefileAndBuild at 1138Error(s) encountered while building model "test2".
Workaround: This error can be resolved by replacing the mech_pose2vr.tlc code generation file.Make a back-up copy of the file mech_pose2vr.tlc in the directory matlabroot/toolbox/physmod/mech/mech/tlc_c.Download the attached mech_pose2vr.tlc and place it the directory matlabroot/toolbox/physmod/mech/mech/tlc_c.matlabroot is the directory returned by the MATLAB function matlabroot.

Summary: Execution profiling results might be inaccurate in models that contain an Idle Task block that configures one or more preemptible tasks
Description: C28x models that contain an Idle Task block with at least one preemptible task might generate inaccurate execution profiling results. Incorrect profiling results might occur because code inserted in the generated code to provide profiling might be preempted during execution, causing profiling errors. Afer you generate code from your model, you see the following lines of code in the generated file MW_c28xx_csl.c : Line 1: void idletask_num1(void)  Line 2: {  Line 3:          profile_task_start(513U);  Line 4:          idle_num1_task_fcn();  Line 5:          profile_task_end(513U);  Line 6: }  Lines 3 and 5 are instrumentation code inserted during code generation to collect real-time execution profile data. These calls are not protected against preemption. If these functions are interrupted by an asynchronous task, profiling data might be corrupted. Lines 3 and 5 appear in the generated code only when you select Real-time execution profiling  in the Configuration Parameters for your model.

Summary: The Sum block can produce a MATLAB assertion in some cases
Description: The Sum block produces a MATLAB assertion (with a stack trace) when: The block's Input data type is determined via back-propagation The block's Output data type is set to Inherit: Same as accumulator  The Accumulator data type is set to Inherit via internal rule
Workaround: Set the Output data type of the Sum block to be Inherit: Inherit via internal rule.

Summary: Attempting to Build xPC Target Models in Standalone Mode Fails
Description: Attempts to build xPC Target StandAlone models will fail because the generated .rtb  file cannot boot on the target PC. Otherwise, xPC Target works as expected (including DOSLoader and BootFloppy modes). This is a known issue with the xPC Target Embedded Option. See https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/details.html?rp=448529  for more details, including download instructions.
Workaround: The release of xPC Target Embedded Option version 3.4.1 (R2008a+) resolves this issue. xPC Target Embedded Option version 3.4.1 (R2008a+) works with xPC Target 3.4 (R2008a).See https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/details.html?rp=448529 for more details.

Summary: 'Assembly item name used twice' error is issued for some models containing Simscape blocks
Description: Some models with deep hierarchy or long block names can encounter an error that reads 'Assembly item name used twice ' during compilation.
Workaround: The workaround depends on your software version:- If you are using R2008a, download the attached file nesl_connectivitytonetwork.p_8a- If you are using R2007b, or a previous version, download the attached file nesl_connectivitytonetwork.p_7bCopy the downloaded file into the following directory:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/network_engine/ne_sli/private/ (where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by enteringmatlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window)Rename the file to nesl_connectivitytonetwork.p, overwriting the existing file.

Summary: Attempting to Build xPC Target Models in Standalone Mode Fails
Description: Atempts to build xPC Target StandAlone models will fail because the generated .rtb  file cannot boot on the target PC. Otherwise, xPC Target works as expected (including DOSLoader and BootFloppy modes). If you have installed xPC Target Embedded Option version 3.4, you do not need to uninstall it. ?

Summary: Exception reported after clicking in empty Date Modified column of Current Directory browser
Description: The Current Directory browser does not display information in the Date Modified column for some files in directories on a network. If you then click in that column, the MATLAB? Command Wndow displays the following exception: Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.lang.NullPointerException at com.mathworks.mde.filebrowser.FileView$FileListTable$MouseL.mousePressed(FileView.java:5337) You can ignore this message. There is no impact on MATLAB or your work.

Summary: Using an ALTER command in a SQL statement returns the error "Cannot issue data manipulation statements with executeQuery()".
Description: Using an ALTER command in a SQL statement returns the error "Cannot issue data manipulation statements with executeQuery()".

Summary: Code for a reusable subsystem may be invalid if the subsystem is connected to a Merge block
Description: The code generated for a reusable subsystem is invalid if an output of the subsystem is connected to a Merge block whose output has a non-addressable custom storage class. Background:  Real-Time Workshop implements a subsystem as a function with arguments if either of the following is true: The subsystem's "Real-Time Workshop system code" parameter is "Reusable function" The subsystem's "Real-Time Workshop system code" parameter is "Auto" and Simulink reuses the subsystem along with at least one other indentical instance  Bug:  When a subsystem is implemented as a function with arguments, the generated code is invalid if both of the following are true: One of the subsystem's outputs is connected to a Merge block The output of the Merge block is a signal with a custom storage class that is not addressable, such as "BitField" or "GetSet"  In R2006b and earlier, the generated code compiles but the compiled code is incorrect and has the wrong behavior. In R2007a and later, the generated code does not compile, because it attempts to take the address of the variable or expression corresponding to the output of the Merge block.
Workaround: Two different workarounds exist. One workaround technique is:Place a Signal Conversion block after the Merge block.Move the label from the output of the Merge block to the output of the Signal Conversion block.Select the Signal Conversion block's parameter "Override optimizations and always copy signal"Alternatively, set the subsystem parameter "Real-Time Workshop system code" to "Inline" or "Function".

Summary: 'Error using ==>findstr' in DFTOOL gui - Datafeed Toolbox 3.1 (R2008a).
Description: After selecting "Get Data" in the DFTOOL gui in the Datafeed Toolbox 3.1, a dialog box appears with the following message: Error using ==> findstr Not enough input arguments.  ?
Workaround: ?To work around this issue, turn the accelerator off by executing the following command at the MATLAB prompt:feature accel off

Summary: Spurious parse errors about unresolved symbols in library charts under rare circumstances
Description: Under the following conditions, simulating a model containing link charts from a library model may cause spurious unresolved symbol errors: A library model (lib.mdl) contains multiple charts (for example, c1 and c2). Chart c1 either calls an exported graphical function or uses a functional-form operator, such as hasChanged() . A main model (main.mdl) contains a link chart pointing to chart c2 from the library model and does not contain a link to chart c1. When the main model is simulated, chart c1 is also parsed partially and causes an unresolved symbol error pointing to the function call or a hasChanged()  operator.
Workaround: A workaround for this bug is to remove all unneeded charts from the library model and place them in a different library model.

Summary: Fmincon interior-point sometimes errors out when I choose option Hessian = 'fin-diff-grads' and I pass additional parameters
Description: The interior-point algorithm in fmincon sometimes errors out when I choose options Hessian = 'fin-diff-grads' and I pass additional parameters, say alpha, at the end of the list of inputs of fmincon: x = fmincon(@obj,x0,A,b,Aeq,beq,lb,ub,@nonlcon,options,alpha)  Here the objective and nonlinear constraints depend on x as well as on the additional parameter alpha. The error is: ??? Input argument "alpha" is undefined.
Workaround: Pass additional parameters using either anonymous functions or nested functions, which are the preferred ways of passing additional parameters to the optimization functions.

Summary: Top model may not simulate or update after a referenced model's configuration set is overwritten
Description: If a model hierarchy contains a submodel that executes in Normal mode and uses a configuration reference to access a configuration set, the following sequence of events will not succeed: Simulate the top model or execute Update Diagram on it. Overwrite the Normal mode submodel's configuration set with a different configuration set that has the same name as the original set. Simulate or update the top model  This sequence of events will fail with the followings warnings: The configuration set 'configset ' referred to by the active configuration reference 'configref ' was out of date. The configuration set has been updated, so model compilation was stopped to avoid inconsistencies. Restart the operation from the beginning. A configuration set reference does not allow writing to parameters in the source configuration set.  Generating code for a model does not initiate or encounter this problem.
Workaround: After overwriting the Normal mode submodel's configuration set, closing and reopening the submodel will avoid the warning. Alternatively, if you start the simulation or update the model a second time, you will not encounter this problem.

Summary: The robustdemo function errors when given more than 100 data points
Description: If the number of data points passed to robustdemo  exceeds 100, the following error occurs: ??? Cell contents reference from a non-cell array object.
Workaround: Edit the subfunction getcoords in robustdemo.m as follows:function [x,y,h] = getcoords(ax)%GETCOORDS Get coordinates of points now on the graphh = findobj(ax, 'Type', 'patch');x = get(h,'XData');y = get(h,'YData');% With fewer than 100 points, each point is a separate patch,% and x and y are cell arrays if ~isscalar(h) % Equivalent to cell2mat x = cat(1,x{:}); y = cat(1,y{:});end

Summary: Clicking the Save button in the Code generation control file section of the Simulink HDL Coder options does not enable the Apply button, so the control file is not associated with the model
Description: If you click the Save  button in the Code generation control file  section of the Simulink HDL Coder options in the Configuration Parameters  dialog, the Apply  button is not activated. Consequently, the change is not applied and the association between the model and the control file is not saved. If the model is closed and reopened, the expected control file name is not displayed in the File name  field.
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the following file with the file attached to this bug report. Then restart MATLAB.MATLABROOT\toolbox\shared\hdlshared\@hdlcoderui\@hdlcc\getTabDialogSchema.p

Summary: The function celintensityread fails due to a ChipType mismatch between and CEL files and the CDF library file.
Description: The function celintensityread checks the ChipType for both the CEL files and CDF library file. If there is a mismatch, the function errors. This check is overly strict. In some cases, celintensityread fails even when the CDF library file is the correct file for the CEL files.
Workaround: Follow these steps as a workaround:1. Quit MATLAB.2. Rename the MATLAB file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\celintensityread.m to celintensityread.m.old.(where $MATLAB$=the MATLAB root directory on your machine)3.
