If you have MATLAB R2007b or earlier, copy the attached file celintensityread_R2007b.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray, and rename the fie to celintensityread.m.If you have MATLAB R2008a, copy the attached file celintensityread_R2008a.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray, and rename the fie to celintensityread.m.4. Restart MATLAB5. After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Fixed-Point Advisor generates errors when inputs of Stateflow charts are not bus objects
Description: If your model contains a Stateflow chart and the inputs to the chart are not defined as bus objects, the Fixed-Point Advisor generates an error when you run task 3.3 Propose scaling for blocks . For example: Error using ==> slResolve at 57 Can not resolve Inherit: Same as Simulink in ... (full block path )
Workaround: Replace matlabroot/toolbox/fixpoint/fpca/private/utilGetInforFromBusObject.p, matlabroot/toolbox/fixpoint/fpca/private/checkOutputDTInheritance.p, matlabroot/toolbox/fixpoint/fpca/private/checkInportBlockScaling.p, and matlabroot/toolbox/fixpoint/fpca/private/actionRemoveAllOutputDTInherit.p as follows:Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.Exit MATLAB.In the directory matlabroot/toolbox/fixpoint/fpca/private, renameutilGetInforFromBusObject.p to utilGetInforFromBusObject.pOld.checkOutputDTInheritance.p to checkOutputDTInheritance.pOld.checkInportBlockScaling.p to checkInportBlockScaling.pOld.actionRemoveAllOutputDTInherit.p to actionRemoveAllOutputDTInherit.pOldDownload the attached files and place them in the directory matlabroot/toolbox/fixpoint/fpca/private.Restart MATLAB.Enter rehash toolbox at the MATLAB command line.Enter rehash toolboxcache at the MATLAB command line.

Summary: Sign block can generate non-integer code even if you specify only fixed-point data types in the model
Description: The Sign block can generate non-integer code even if you specify only fixed-point data types in the model. The Support floating-point numbers  check box on the Real-Time Workshop > Interface  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box is ignored.

Summary: Code generation error for referenced model
Description: When you generate code for a referenced model that contains the LibWriteModelOutputs  function, the following TLC error occurs because this function does not support referenced models. ### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: 'model_name ' aborted due to an error.??? Error using ==> tlc_newError: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2008a\rtw\c\tlc\lib\utillib.tlc Line: 438 Column: 8%exit directive: Real-Time Workshop Fatal: Encountered 'Unknown' arg

Summary: C2833x GPIO blocks using MUX pins 40-47, 56-63, and 72-79 fail to clear control register bits for the specified pins and erroneously clear the control register bits for pins 32-39, 48-55, and 64-71.
Description: When the 2833x GPIO blocks use MUX pins 40-47, 56-63, and 72-79, the generated code fails to clear the control register bits for the specified pins. As a result, depending on the initial state of these registers, GPIO blocks may not work correctly. In addition, the GPIO blocks erroneously clear control register bits for pins 32-39, 48-55, and 64-71. Depending on the initial state of these registers, the peripherals using the respective MUX pins (e.g. SCI and EMIF) may not work correctly.
Workaround: 1. In your MATLAB installation, find the following folder:<$MATLAB_HOME>\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000blks\tlc_cHere, <$MATLAB_HOME> represents the MATLAB installation folder.2. Backup the following files for your reference:c280xgpio_di.tlcc280xgpio_do.tlc3. Copy the attached files to the folder listed above:<$MATLAB_HOME>\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000blks\tlc_c

Summary: Accelerator mode simulation fails to occur if referenced model contains signal processing block and Real-Time Workshop not installed
Description: Attempting to simulate a model fails with a MEX file creation error if all of the following conditions are true: Accelerator mode is selected for the model. The model references a model that contains a Signal Processing Blockset block or a Video and Image Processing Blockset block. Real-Time Workshop software is not installed.  Before simulation can occur, Simulink displays an error similar to the following: Problem creating Accelerator MEX file formodel 'mymodel'. Error returned is:Error using ==> accelbuild_private at 31Error using ==> addFiles at 27Incorrect arguments have been passed to: RTW.BuildInfoBuildObj.addFiles
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2008a, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following file, for example, by adding .old to the end of the file name.matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/rtw_c.mDownload the attached file rtw_c.m.R2008a.Rename the downloaded file to rtw_c.m and place it in the same directory as the file you renamed.Restart MATLAB.To work around this bug in R2007b, perform the following steps:In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following file, for example, by adding .old to the end of the file name.matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/rtw_c.mDelete the following file, which is the P-file equivalent of the MATLAB file you renamed.matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/rtw_c.pDownload the attached file rtw_c.m.R2007b.Rename the downloaded file to rtw_c.m and place it in the same directory as the file you renamed.Issue the following MATLAB commands to regenerate the P-file equivalent of the MATLAB file you downloaded.>> cd matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/>> pcode('rtw_c.m')Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Error when using the SWAPBYZERO function when end of month rule is not in effect.
Description: Customer has a maturity date that falls on the last day of a month with less than 31 days (for instance, 28-Feb-2007), and wants to price a swap using a set of zero curves and have the end of month rule not in effect.
Workaround: There is no work around.

Summary: Function ecef2geodetic errors on 2-D input
Description: Function ecef2geodetic errors on 2-D and N-D input coordinate arrays; it can only be used with vectors and scalars. It should accept coordinate arrays of arbitrary dimension (as long as the sizes are consistent across the inputs).
Workaround: To avoid the need to reshape your coordinate arrays, replace the ecef2geodetic.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/shared/mapgeodesy/ecef2geodetic.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: ecef2geodetic.m.oldCopy the version of ecef2geodetic.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: gcs, gcb, and gcbh can return the wrong value when invoked by an S-function
Description: On non-Windows platforms, the gcs, gcb, and gcbh functions can return the wrong value when invoked by an S-function.

Summary: Function str2angle errors on input strings containing the character '6'
Description: Function str2angle  errors on input strings containing the character '6' .
Workaround: To work around this problem, either insert a '6' in the string '012345789NSEW+-. ' on line 182 of str2angle.m, or replace the str2angle.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/map/str2angle.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: str2angle.m.oldCopy the version of str2angle.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: read() method fails when you read negative data values
Description: MULTI? Debugger allows you to display all numbers in hexadecimal format. If you select this option, trying to read negative numbers with read()  causes the Embedded IDE Link MU Service to stop. You see the following error message in the MATLAB? command window: An exception occurred while performing this operation. Server shutdown unexpectedly.
Workaround: To prevent the exception from occurring, do not display all numbers as hexadecimal in the debugger. Perform the following steps to turn off hexadecimal display for all numbers:In the MULTI IDE, choose Config > Options from the menu bar.In the Options dialog box, click Debugger.Clear Display all numbers/characters as hex.Verify that you selected Save configuration as default.Click OK.

Summary: Using mswcmp with L2_perf compression produces an error
Description: If you us the L2_perf  compression method, mswcmp  produces an error when computing the compression scores.
Workaround: Copy the files in the Attachment section below to your $ROOT\toolbox\wavelet\wmultisig1d directory.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: In Embedded MATLAB, using loops to initialize fields of structures in arrays may lead to wrong answers in generated code
Description: If your MATLAB code contains a loop that initializes a field of a structure in an array, a faulty analysis may incorrectly conclude that the structure array is fully initialized. As a result, subsequent optimizations may yield incorrect results. Here is an example of code that may trigger this error: for i = 1:10  s(k).a(i) = 4; end The body of your loop may contain additional assignments, but no control-flow statements.
Workaround: Rewrite your code to initialize a single structure element and then assign it into the array.For the above example, the following code corrects the problem:sk = s(k);for i = 1:10  sk.a(i) = 4;ends(k) = sk;?

Summary: Calling COM object method which returns a SAFEARRAY of COM object interfaces using dot notation can result in a SEGV
Description: ? If a COM object exposes a method which returns a SAFEARRAY of COM object interfaces, calling such method using dot ('.') notation can result in a fatal segmentation violation error. For example, a COM object implements a method with the following IDL signature: HRESULT GetObjectArray([out, retval] SAFEARRAY(ISomeOtherObject*)* pRetVal); The following command results in a SEGV: objArr = myObj.GetObjectArray();
Workaround: ?Use the invoke function with a case missmatch (in at least 1 of the letters) in the method name instead of the dot notation. For example, use a lowercase 'g' in the method name:objArr = myObj.invoke('getObjectArray');instead of:objArr = myObj.GetObjectArray() ;?

Summary: interp2 may run more slowly than expected for data type single.
Description: The function interp2  may perform unnecessary preprocessing of single -type data. As a result, the function may not run faster for data inputs of type single  than for data inputs of type double . This occurs because the test to check if the input values are equally spaced can fail due to the tolerance scaled by the function eps .

Summary: PolySpace software reports a syntax error when using a post processing command using the Perl command provided in the product
Description: When using a post processing command with certain statements, PolySpace will stop with the following message: Verifying cross-files ANSI C compliance  Stubbing standard library functions ...Line 78: syntax error: inserting INFINITY----------------------------------------------------------------------@FFT: SUIF integrity trouble: Infinity detectedAn internal consistency problem has been detected.In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information,the computation has been halted.Please complete a Bug Report at The MathWorks Support sitehttps://www.mathworks.com/support.Please remember to attach the log file from this analysis to the report.----------------------------------------------------------------------  --------------------------------------------------------------------- ------ Verifier has encountered an internal error. ------ Please contact your technical support. ------ ----------------------------------------------------------------------  This problem is occurs because the Perl program used in the post processing command generates a file in DOS format. This causes an internal parsing problem that leads to the syntax error.
Workaround: To avoid this error, add the line:binmode STDOUTto the Perl script, immediately after the line:#!/usr/bin/perlIf you do not have this line in your script, at it at the at the beginning of the script.This will change the text format and remove the extra characters in the preprocessed files.

Summary: Lognormal variance formula incorrect in documentation
Description: The formula for the variance v  of the lognormal distribution, given in terms of the distribution parameters ¦Ě  and ¦Ň , is incorrect in the Statistics Toolbox documentation in the following places: The reference page for lognstat  The reference page for lognpdf  The reference page for lognrnd  The entry for the lognormal distribution in the Distribution Reference
Workaround: The correct formula is:v = exp(2¦Ě + ¦Ň2)(exp(¦Ň2) - 1)

Summary: Build process failure with error message: Error: File: matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\lib\blkiolib.tlc
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail to generate code and reports error messages similar to the following: Error: File: matlabroot \rtw\c\tlc\lib\blkiolib.tlc Line: 1329 Column: 26The specified index (2) was out of the range 0 - 1Error: File: matlabroot  \rtw\c\tlc\lib\blkiolib.tlc Line: 1329 Column: 33Ambiguous reference to DataOutputPort - must use array index to refer to one of multiple scopesError: File: matlabroot  \rtw\c\tlc\lib\blkiolib.tlc Line: 1329 Column: 33The left-hand side of a . operator must be a valid scope identifier  ? ?

Summary: Export of models for multimodel tradeoffs can initialize lookup tables with incorrect values
Description: When you export models for a multimodel tradeoff, CAGE does not initialize lookup tables to correct values if the table axes are transposed and you used automatic input ranges to fit multiple local models.

Summary: A bug in MATLAB Version 7.6 (R2008a) release can cause erroneous behaviors when program execution hits a breakpoint in a class method.
Description: You might experience problems when program execution hits a breakpoint set within a class method. The problems can be either of the following: MATLAB repeatedly displays the breakpoint information, which eventually leads to a MATLAB crash. MATLAB encounters an errors having the following error message when you type dbquit  at the debug prompt:  Severe:Error: An InternalException was thrown.Caught unexpected MathWorks::System::InternalException
Workaround: There is no known workaround.

Summary: MCC GPIB drivers are not always detected
Description: With some versions of the MCC GPIB driver, the GPIB board and connected instruments will not be detected. You may receive an error similar to the following when you create a GPIB object. This can prevent you from communicating with instruments connected to the GPIB adaptor. g=gpib('mcc',1,6);  ??? Error using ==> gpib.gpib at 152Unable to load MCC GPIB interface. Please check that the necessary drivers are installed on your machine.

Summary: Compilation failure for models with non-Boolean logic signals
Description: When you invoke Simulink Design Verifier on a model that has the Optimization setting "Implement logic signals as boolean data (vs. double)" disabled, you might encounter the following error: Simulink Design Verifier failed to initialize: Unsupported model item(s) due to compilation problem(s) --> Data type mismatch. Input port 1 of 'mLogicSignalDouble/Sum1' expects a signal of data type 'boolean'. However, it is driven by a signal of data type 'double'. ? ? ? ?

Summary: Models that Contain the Simulink Signal Builder Block Might Cause Simulation Errors
Description: If Group 1 is not the active signal group when you save a model that contains a Signal Builder block, the last signal of Group 1 will be corrupted. If the length of the corrupted signal differs from the original signal length, the issue can manifest in the following ways: Simulation results are unexpected when using Group 1. For example, if you open a model and run Group 1 as the model input, an error like the following is generated: Error in 'mpc555_fuelsys_vv/Test Cases': Initialization commands cannot be evaluated. MATLAB error message: Error using ==> horzcat CAT arguments dimensions are not consistent.  Simulation results are unexpected when you click the Run all  button. For example, if you open the model and run all test cases from the Signal Builder block, an error like the following is generated: Simulation failed: Error using ==> cvsim at 65 Simulation failed   If the length of the corrupted signal is the same as the original signal, the simulation will be successful, but the resulting data will not be as expected. Check your data if you suspect corruption.  ?
Workaround: To work around this issue by download the attached files.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/ folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file: /toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/new_channel.p/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/restore_from_saveStruct.pTo see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/ folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/ folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/ folder.

Summary: Incorrect product name on Help roadmap page
Description: The name of the link product is incorrectly stated on the Help roadmap page. It is stated as "EDA Simulator Link MS". The correct name is "EDA Simulator Link MQ". Note that the name of the product is correctly stated in the User's Guide & Reference, the Release Notes, and in online material.

Summary: The example section of the PDE doc, "Conductive Media DC", is erroneous and confusing.
Description: The example is solved correctly, and then solved again (a duplicate solution) with an incorrect figure at the end. The duplicate solution and erroneous figure should have been removed from the documentation.
Workaround: Ignore everything in the doc from PDF pages 1-113 bottom to 1-115 top.

Summary: TCP/IP functionality does not work with TI's NDK version 1.92 or above
Description: In R2008a release, the supported versions of the NDK are 1.71 to 1.91. In NDK versions 1.92 and above, some of the libraries and path names have changed. This results in a model build error when the product infra-structures tries to add TCP/IP stack libraries to the generated project as illustrated below: Error building Real-Time Workshop target for block diagram 'c6455_vipldws_tgt'. MATLAB error message: Error using ==> callMakeHook at 49 The call to ccslink_ert_make_rtw_hook, during the exit hook generated the following error:  Error using ==> feval Error using ==> i_exitHookpoint at 93 Error while creating the project: Error using ==> util_filenameParser at 55 The specified file "c:\CCStudio_v3.3\ndk_1_93_eval\packages\ti\ndk\lib\hal\dsk6455\hal_ser_stub.lib" does not exist on the path. The build process will terminate as a result.
Workaround: Install a supported version of the NDK (versions 1.71 to 1.91) from an earlier TI product CD, if available.

Summary: To Workspace block does not generate code when it receives an input signal from a Constant block with a value that is a tunable parameter expression
Description: The To Workspace block does not generate code when it receives an input signal from a Constant block with a value that is a tunable parameter expression. When you try to generate code for a model that includes such a block, a Target Language Compiler (TLC) error occurs and a generic message similar to the following appears: Error: Real-Time Workshop Error in block: "mtunableparam/Constant", block type "Constant": The block is unable to get the address of parameter Value because an operation converted the parameter to a non-trivial tunable expression (int32(val)). Blocks that require a parameter address support direct use of tunable parameters. However, when an operation such as a data type conversion or math operation is applied, a non-trivial expression that cannot be accessed by address can result. For more information, including limitations and workarounds, see sections in the Real-Time Workshop documentation on tunable expressions and data type considerations for tunable workspace parameters.
Workaround: Do one of the following:Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Constant block and To Workspace block and select the Signal Conversion block's "Override optimizations and always copy signal" parameter.Designate the output signal of the Constant block as a test point.

Summary: MATLAB throws a SEGV when trying to call Open function in the Agilent VISA Global Resource Manager COM component.
Description: The following MATLAB code demonstrates the error: rmServer = actxserver('VISA.GlobalRM', 'interface', 'IUnknown'); rm = rmServer.invoke('IResourceManager3'); % The next line throws a SEGVsession = rm.Open('ASRL1::INSTR'); This error is due to the inability of the COM External Interface to call methods with optional arguments exposed by the COM Custom Interface based component implementations. ?

Summary: The active-set algorithm of fmincon sometimes stops prematurely with an exitflag of 5 and the following message:Optimization terminated: magnitude of directional derivative in searchdirection less than 2*options.TolFun and max
Description: One stopping criterion in fmincon 's active-set algorithm is whether the objective function is changing much along a computed search direction at an iteration j. This is supposed to be checked by comparing the gradient of the objective function at the previous point, xj-1 , and the search direction the algorithm took in the iteration, xj  - xj-1 . If the magnitude of the objective function gradient in the search direction is less than 2*TolFun , fmincon  stops with an exitflag of 5. However, the test as implemented uses the gradient of the objective at the current point xj  instead of the previous point xj-1 .
Workaround: Users of Optimization Toolbox 4.0 (R2008a) can try using fmincon's interior-point algorithm.

Summary: Code for a model with a Model block may contain redundant sample-hit checks
Description: Redundant sample-hit checks may enclose calls to a referenced model's functions if: The parent model's Tasking  mode is set to Single-tasking  The referenced model is single-rate  For example, in the following code two identical sample-hit checks guard a referenced model's Update function: if (sldemo_mdlref_basic_M->Timing.TaskCounters.TID[1] == 0) {  if (sldemo_mdlref_basic_M->Timing.TaskCounters.TID[1] == 0) {  /* Update function for ModelReference Block: '<Root>/CounterC' */  mr_sldemo_mdlref_counter_Update(&sldemo_mdlref_basic_B.output,                &(sldemo_mdlref_basic_DWork.CounterC_DWORK1.rtdw));  }}

Summary: Function setm results in an error when applied to a map axes whose parent is not a figure
Description: Function setm  results in an error when applied to a map axes whose parent is not a figure. This happens if the map axes is within a uipanel , for example.
Workaround: To work around these issues, if you are running Release R2008a, replace the setm.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdisp/setm.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: setm.m.oldCopy the version of  setm.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.For older releases (R14 through R2007b), you should back up your copy of setm.m as described above, then replace both instances of get(ax,'Parent') with ancestor(ax,'figure') and replace both instances of get(oldaxes,'Parent') with ancestor(oldaxes,'figure'). (This fix can also be applied in R2008a, in place of the download, except that there are four instances of get(oldaxes,'Parent') to replace instead of just two.)

Summary: Cannot start version-specific MATLAB Automation server.
Description: In R2007b, I started a MATLAB COM Server with "Matlab.Application.Single.7.5 ", and the handle was "COM.matlab_application_7_5 ", but the instance of MATLAB that actually started up was R2008a. The handle to the server should be "COM.matlab_application_7_6 ". MATLAB Class ID was not updated for the last few releases. Due to this bug, certain version-specific PROGIDs are pointing to the latest version of MATLAB instead of the appropriate version.
Workaround: No workaround available.

Summary: PolySpace verification stops during the MISRA-C checking phase with the message: 'Signal 11'
Description: When using the MISRA-C checker when verifying C code containing anonymous structures, the verification stops at the end of the compilation phase with a 'Signal 11' message. A 'Signal 11' message appears in the log file on the following code when using options -allow-unamed-fields  and -misra2 all-rules : volatile union{  unsigned char MYREG;  struct  {  unsigned char MREGB0:1, MREGB0B1:1, MREGB0B2:1, MREGB0B3:1, MREGB0B4:1, MREGB0B5:1, MREGB0B6:1, MREGB0B7:1;  };};

Summary: Printing a waterfall plot to a black-and-white device or format causes errors.
Description: Printing or exporting a waterfall plot to a black-and-white device or format results in an error. For example, the following code will generate an error on most non-Windows platform since the default print device is -dps , a non-color format. [x,y,z] = peaks(50)waterfall(x,y,z)print     The generated error will look like the following: Error:   Error using ==> prepare at 77Error using ==> prepare at 77Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalarvalues.   ?
Workaround: Change the device used when printing to one that supports color, or change the format specified to a color format.For example,[x,y,z] = peaks(50)waterfall(x,y,z)print -dpsc

Summary: PolySpace for C++ crash when using the Remote launcher from a Windows Client to a Linux Server and when the code to verify contains a global variable.?
Description: The conditions to experiment the problem are: PolySpace for C++ is used. The Remote Launcher is used. The Client is a Windows platform and the Server is a Linux platform. The code to verify contains at least one global variable.  In this case, the verification stops with the following error message (message may differ slightly): Generating remote file/usr/local/PolySpace/PolySpaceForCandCPP_R2008a/bin/x86-linux/rte-tar.x86-linux: ALL/PASS1/C\://PolySpace//PolySpaceForCandCPP_R2008a//Examples//Demo_Cpp_Long//sources//tasks.chk: Cannot stat: No such file or directory/usr/local/PolySpace/PolySpaceForCandCPP_R2008a/bin/x86-linux/rte-tar.x86-linux: Error exit delayed from previous errors
Workaround: The problem has been corrected in version 5.1.1 which can be downloaded on the web site.

Summary: Incorrect red check of type dynamic_cast (DCT) on the returned object of a stubbed function.
Description: PolySpace verification can incorrectly report a dynamic_cast (DCT) red check (instead of an orange check) on the returned object of a stubbed function. Here is a small example that reproduces the issue : struct A{ virtual void f() ;} ; struct B : public A { } ; A* getB() ; // stubs int main(){ A*pa = getB() ; B*pb = dynamic_cast<B*>(pa) ; // Red check "incorrect dynamic cast, continue with null pointer"} We have a class (or struct, it is equivalent in this case) A, and another class B that inherits from A. We declare the function getB, but we don't give its definition, so it will be stubbed. getB is supposed to return a pointer to an instance of the class A. getB can return a pointer to an instance of class A, so pa could be well initialized. There should be an orange check on the dynamic_cast, not a red one.
Workaround: Since this problem is caused by stubbing, you can avoid the issue by providing a manual stub of getB.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB repmat issues an array bounds error with N-D matrix input
Description: The Embedded MATLAB REPMAT function issues an array bounds error when the the input array is N-D (ndims >= 3) and the output array has a greater number of dimensions than the input. The bug does not affect 2-D to N-D cases or N-D to N-D cases when the number of dimensions does not change.
Workaround: Since the bug does not affect replicating 2-D matrices to N-D, it is possible to perform the operation in stages. For example, replacey = repmat(a,s);withd = ndims(a);y1 = repmat(a,s(1:d)); % replicate lower dimensionss2 = s(d+1:end);y2 = repmat(y1(:),[1,s2]); % replicate y1 as a 2-D arrayy = reshape(y2,[size(y1),s2]); % reshape to correct size

Summary: MATLAB handle objects that have cell array properties can cause MATLAB to crash in certain circumstances.
Description: MATLAB handle objects that have cell array properties can cause MATLAB to crash in certain circumstances. This crash occurs only if the cell array contains a reference to itself or a cell array in another handle object, i.e. any circular data structure involving cell arrays and handle objects. For example: classdef test_object < handle    properties       prop = {};    end end function isolate    test = test_object;    test.prop = { };    verify(1,test,0);    verify(2,test,{test.prop}); function verify(n,test,elem)    test.prop{n} = 0;
Workaround: Disabling the MATLAB JIT/Accelerator for MATLAB objects fixes this problem. The command to disable acceleration is:feature jitallow mcos off

Summary: UPFIRDN outputs an incorrect result when both the input and filter matrix are complex-valued.
Description: When the input is a vector and the filter is a matrix, UPFIRDN  filters the input with each column of the filter matrix. When both the input and the filter matrix are complex, the output is incorrect.

Summary: Error message appears upon opening second MATLAB session
Description: While running MATLAB, if you open the Deployment Tool and then open another instance of MATLAB, the following error message appears in the Command Window: Unable to restore Deployment Tool, no corresponding class was specified
Workaround: The error message is harmless, so no workaround is required. If you want to avoid seeing the error message, do not open a second MATLAB session while the Deployment Tool is open in another session.

Summary: Trimming in the linem and plotm functions can remove vertices on the edge of the map frame
Description: Trimming in the linem  and plotm  functions can remove vertices on the edge of the map frame. In cases where there are only two vertices defining a line, this can cause the entire line to fail to display.
Workaround: If you are plotting two-dimensional data (latitude and longitude only, not latitude, longitude, and height), in most cases you can use the function geoshow instead of linem or plotm. If you are not familiar with geoshow, please consult its reference page in the Mapping Toolbox documentation; there are many syntax options supporting many different types of two-dimensional geodata. Also, if you are using a linespec string to control line properties like color, linestyle, or marker, you'll need to switch to explicit property name-value pairs instead.

Summary: drss documentation shows incorrect argument order of inputs and outputs
Description: The drss  documentation shows incorrect syntax for generating a random n -th order stable model with m  inputs and p  outputs. The input and output arguments should be in the reverse order of the syntax shown. Incorrect syntax: drss(n,m,p)  Correct syntax: drss(n,p,m)
Workaround: Use the syntax drss(n,p,m) to generate a random n-th order stable model with p outputs and m inputs.

Summary: CAGE projects that contain a large number of complicated features are very slow to load and view.
Description: The Model-Based Calibration Toolbox software can experience significant performance issues when performing certain operations on CAGE projects that include a large number of highly interconnected calibration features. Examples of such operations include loading projects, selecting items for viewing in the CAGE Browser, and viewing features in the CAGE Surface Viewer. For example, for a project that includes 100 features, some of which include several levels of subfeatures, it can take up to 30 seconds to select a feature in the CAGE Browser and up to 30 minutes to view the feature in the Surface Viewer. The performance issues are most noticable when you reuse features to build more complex features. Projects that have fewer and simpler features do not exhibit the same performance issues.
Workaround: A patch is available to improve performance for large, complex CAGE projects, as noted in the bug description, for versions 3.3 and 3.4. You do not need to download this patch if you are not experiencing the performance issues.The patch consists of the four files listed in the following table and the Attachments section.FilenameLocation in installationhorzcat.m$MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbc\@xregpointer\horzcat.mgetAllProjectPtrs.m$MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcdata\@cgprojconnections\getAllProjectPtrs.mgetAllInputs.m$MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcexpr\@cgexpr\getAllInputs.mrecursivefind.m$MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcexpr\@cgexpr\private\recursivefind.m?To replace the files in your current installation with the attached patch files,For each file listed in the table, rename the existing file on your disk from filename.m to filename.m.old.Download each attachment and copy the file to the location listed in the table.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: FFT block errors out for frame-based input with 1-D output
Description: When the input to the FFT block is a frame-based vector and the output is a 1-D vector, the block errors.
Workaround: If you are using R2007a, R2007b, or R2008a, you can work around this issue by either changing the block input to a sample-based signal, or by changing the block output to a 2-D vector.If you are using R2006b, a downloadable fix is available:Type clear mex at the MATLAB command line.Copy the two files sdspfft2.mexXXX and sdspfft2.mexXXX.csf to the following directory:$MATLAB/toolbox/dspblks/dspmexwhere $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. XXX is the file extension for your platform. For example, for 32-bit Windows, the file extension is w32, and for 64-bit Linux, the file extension is a64.Type rehash toolbox at the MATLAB command line.

Summary: Java exception when copying the contents of a uitable via keyboard
Description: Create a uitable and fill it with data: uitable('Data',magic(8)) Copying its contents by selecting cells and typing Ctrl +C generates the following error: Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: 8 >= 8 at java.util.Vector.elementAt(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.table.DefaultTableColumnModel.getColumn(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.JTable.convertColumnIndexToModel(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.JTable.getValueAt(Unknown Source) at com.jidesoft.grid.JideTable$g_.createTransferable(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.TransferHandler.exportToClipboard(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.TransferHandler$TransferAction.actionPerformed(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.SwingUtilities.notifyAction(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.JComponent.processKeyBinding(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.JTable.processKeyBinding(Unknown Source) at com.jidesoft.grid.JideTable.processKeyBinding(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.JComponent.processKeyBindings(Unknown Source) at javax.swing.JComponent.processKeyEvent(Unknown Source)

Summary: The Insert Zero, Puncture and the Convolutional Encoder blocks may give an error for large I/O signal or parameter (Puncture vector, Insert zero vector) sizes.
Description: This affects the Convolutional Encoder block when Puncturing is selected, the Insert Zero and Puncture blocks. The internal computations use signed 32-bit integers (int32) in their computation of the output sizes based on the input signal lengths and the parameter lengths. As a result, the blocks error out if these computations overflow (if greater than 2^31-1) due to large values for the input/output port sizes or the parameter length. ? ?

Summary: After installing a new release of MATLAB Builder NE, legacy applications that previously ran without error may now throw an exception.
Description: One possible thrown exception is: field not found  This error is the result of an improperly versioned MWArray  assembly replacing a previous version of the assembly in the Global Assembly Cache.
Workaround: The workaround for versions R2007a thru R2008a of the MWArray is as follows:Copy the appropriate version of the attached MWArray assembly to \toolbox\dotnetbuilder\bin\win32\v2.0.Rename \toolbox\dotnetbuilder\bin\win32\v2.0\MWArray.dll to MWArray.distrib to back up the file.Rename the copied assembly to MWArray.dllInstall the new assembly in the Global Assembly Cache (GAC) using the following command: \toolbox\dotnetbuilder\bin\win32\v2.0\GACInstaller MWArray.dllReinstall any previous versions of the MWArray assembly in the GAC.

Summary: In Embedded MATLAB, using small double values as conditions in boolean expressions produces incorrect results during simulation.
Description: In Embedded MATLAB, boolean expressions that use double values between -1 and 1 as the condition produce incorrect results during simulation. For example, in the following code, the if  branch never executes: % Assume u = 0.1 as input from constant block in Simulink function y = fcn(u)if u && u? y = 1; % The if branch never executes.else? y = 2; % The else branch executes.end
Workaround: Cast the input to a logical or other integer type, as in this modification of the above example:% Assume u = 0.1 as input from constant block in Simulink function y = fcn(u)if logical(u) && logical(u)? y = 1; % The if branch executes.else? y = 2; % The else branch does not execute.end

Summary: Object property may retain previous value during call to function or method
Description: When using MATLAB classes that have classdef  files, if a property on an object is modified and the object is immediately passed to a function or method, there may be some cases where the object still has its previous value inside the function. This only occurs if all of the following are true: The property has no set  method The class does not have an overloaded subsassign  method The class is not a handle class (subclass of handle ) The code is executed multiple times in a loop  ?
Workaround: Use the following command before running the code:   feature jitallow mcos_methods off

Summary: Model Recovery dialog appears when opening TEST-files containing a Simulink Element
Description: Under certain conditions a "Model Recovery" dialog box will appear when opening a TEST-file with a Simulink element. If the Simulink Autosave feature is enabled in Simulink, and a model under the control of the SystemTest Simulink element causes Simulink or MATLAB to crash, temporary changes made to the model by SystemTest will be present in the Autosaved copy of the model. Because all changes made by SystemTest to a model are temporary, the autosaved model should be discarded by chosing "Ignore All" or "Delete All" in the Model Recovery dialog box.
Workaround: To avoid this scenario, you can disable the Simulink Autosave feature. Open the Simulink Preferences dialog and, under Autosave Options, uncheck "Save before updating or simulating the model." You can re-enable the Autosave feature when exiting SystemTest.If you need to use the Autosave feature, and this scenario occurs, simply select Ignore All or Delete All in the Model Recovery dialog box.

Summary: Possible code generation errors when the Embedded Hardware pane specifies a long with more than 32 bits
Description: This bug can affect code generation and accelerated simulation when a relational operator compares data with dissimilar data types. The bug can occur only when the Embedded Hardware pane specifies long   data to have more than 32 bits. The bug does not affect normal mode simulation. The effects of the bug can include failure of code generation or accelerated simulation, and cryptic error messages. Whether the bug actually occurs, and what happens when it does, depends on the data types compared and the software and hardware used. Relational Operators Before R14sp3:  When the inputs to a relational operator have dissimilar data types, Simulink casts the inputs to a common type, called the comparison type , before performing the comparison. Prior to R14sp3, the comparison type reflected only the input data types. Simulink would use whichever data type had the greatest positive range. The result of this technique is not always the same as the result of a mathematically ideal relational operator. For example, consider the following comparison:  uint32 u_in = 0;  int32 s_in = -1;  boolean out = (u_in == s_in); The uint32  data type has a greater positive range than int32 , so prior to R14sp3, the comparison data type is uint32 . In this example, the selected comparison type is identical to what C/C++ would select based on the language's usual promotion rules (assuming int  or long  was 32 bits). In Simulink, the relational operation involving dissimilar types would employ a saturating cast from int32  to uint32 :  uint32 u_in = 0;  int32 s_in = -1;  uint32 u_temp;  boolean out;  if (s_in < 0) {  u_temp = 0;  } else {  u_temp = (uint32) s_in;  }  out = ( u_in == u_temp ); The result of this code is the value TRUE . This value does not agree with the result of an ideal mathematical operator, which would be the value FALSE . This nonideal result stems from a design decision. This design decision involves a tradeoff between getting the ideal result for all possible values and an efficient implementation. Achieving the mathematically ideal result would require using at least an int33  comparison type. Note that prior to R14sp3, relational operators always give identical results in normal mode simulation, accelerated simulation, and generated code, whether or not those results are also mathematically ideal. Relational Operators from R14sp3 onward:  Beginning in R14sp3, Simulink used information from the Embedded Hardware pane to improve relational operators that compare dissimilar types. The new design allows the comparison type to be as large as the Embedded Hardware's long. This increases the cases for which ideal comparison results are achieved while still providing an efficient implementation. In the previous example, the ideal comparison required at least 33 bits. If the Embedded Hardware pane specified a long larger than 32 bits, then it would be used and the relational operator would give ideal results for all possible values. If the Embedded Hardware pane specified a 40 bit long, then the comparison type would be int40 . The resulting implementation would involve two numerically perfect casts:  uint32 u_in = 0; int32 s_in = -1; int40 s40_temp1 = (int40)u_in; int40 s40_temp2 = (int40)s_in; boolean out = (s40_temp1 == s40_temp2); The result of this code is the value FALSE , which is mathematically ideal. This technique gives ideal results because int40  can perfectly represent all possible values for both int32  and uint32 . The same technique works equally well for any long data type that is big enough to represent all values compared by a relational operator. Relational Operator Bugs in R14sp3 Through R2008a:  The relational operator design change introduced in R14sp3 improved numeric comparisons in normal mode simulation, but introduced bugs when used with code generation or accelerated simulation. In releases R2008a and earlier, code generation is restricted to 32 bits or less for integer and fixed-point data. The use of a relational operator comparison type larger than 32 bits would exceed the code generation limit. In the previous example, the comparison type of int40 exceeds the 32 bit code generation limit. The way that the software responds to the comparison type exceeding the 32 limit depends on the version and platform. R14sp3, R2006a, and R2006b:  Code generation and accelerated simulation will fail when the relational operator comparison type exceeds the 32 bit limit. The bug is that the error message does not provide clear information. The message begins with text like the following:  CGIR assertion in .\dom_core\cg_fixpt.cpp(420): 'nBits <= cg_c_target_nlongbits( (ctx),1)' R2007a and R2007b:  Acceleration and code generation always succeed, but only because the code generator restricts the comparison data type to be at most 32 bits regardless of the size of a long  in the Embedded Hardware pane. The effect is for acceleration and code generation (but not normal mode simulation) to revert to the behavior used before R14sp3. Depending on the values input to relational expressions with dissimilar types, the results of normal mode simulation may not agree with the results of accelerated simulation or generated code. No warning occurs about this possible nonagreement bug. R2008a:  Acceleration and code generation will fail on most MATLAB host computers, as they did before R2007a. Acceleration and code generation will correctly attempt to used the exact same comparison type that normal mode simulation uses. If that comparison type exceeds the 32 bit code generation limit, then an error will occur. The error message contains text like the following:  Problem creating Accelerator MEX file for model 'relop_long_40_accel_R14'. Error returned is:  Error using ==> accelbuild_private at 31  Error using ==> tlc_c at 133  Error during Real-Time Workshop code generation: Fixed-point operation cannot be implemented in 32 bits.
Workaround: One workaround is to change the inputs to the relational operator to have the same data type, perhaps by using a Data Type Conversion block. When comparing identical data types, the relational operator has no need to cast to a larger data type. Since the comparison type is not larger than the inputs, the comparison type can't be the reason a data type larger than 32 bits is used.Another workaround is to change the description of a long in the Embedded Hardware pane to be no more than 32 bits. This technique effectively restores the behavior that existed before R14sp3, and is acceptable only if a larger long was not really needed.

Summary: Incorrect result from complicated multiple assignments involving nested functions.
Description: MATLAB statements that assign their output to multiple indexed variables, and are used in nested functions, have been seen to generate incorrect results. The following statement assigns its output to multiple indexed variables C and D:    [C{1},D{1}] = deal(5,6);
Workaround: To work around this, use simple variables in the multiple assignment, and then do the indexed assignment. For example, suppose the error occurs in the statement   [C{1},D{1}] = deal(5,6); Then the correct results can be obtained using   [tmp1,tmp2] = deal(5,6);   C{1} = tmp1;   D{1} = tmp2;

Summary: Attempting to interactively delete data from a saved figure that has been brushed causes an error.
Description: After loading a figure from a FIG-file, if data is deleted using plot edit mode after it has been brushed, the following error will appear in the command window: Error using ==> getHandleFromProxyValue at 23Unable to find an object with a matching in the figure.

Summary: Macintosh? platforms: Cannot start the MATLAB? software by double-clicking an associated document in the Finder
Description: On Apple? Macintosh? platforms, when you double-click a document in the Finder that is associated with the MATLAB? software, such as a MAT-file, MATLAB does not start.
Workaround: If MATLAB is already running, you can double-click an associated document in the Finder and MATLAB will open the document. The workaround is to start MATLAB first, and then double click the document you want to open.

Summary: Compiled applications or shared libraries created using MATLAB Compiler may fail at runtime if CTF is embedded
Description: Error messages are generated at runtime of the type: Error opening '...'  Followed by: ??? Undefined function or variable 'matlabrc'.  These errors occur when the path to a file within the CTF archive exceeds a certain limit (~110 characters). This may be an issue with the file's original (pre-compilation) path relative to matlabroot , the current working directory (CWD), or the root directory of the file system. This problem only affects Windows platforms (32 and 64 bit). ?
Workaround: There are three possible workarounds:Rename or move the file so that its path within the CTF will be shorter.Set the environment variable MCR_CACHE_ROOT to some relatively short path, such as C:\Temp (the restriction on MCR_CACHE_ROOT is that it must point to an area of the file system that is writeable by the user running the compiled application).Opt-out of embedding the CTF data in the application or library by default. Use the '-C' flag with mcc, or uncheck the box to Embed CTF Data in deploytool's Settings panel.

Summary: Build of model containing S-function with build variable HAVESTDIO undefined may fail with compilation error
Description: Building a model that contains an S-function may fail with a compilation error if all of the following conditions are true: The S-function is non-inlined (that is, does not have a TLC file associated with it). The S-function uses the S-function API function ssPrintf.  The build variable HAVESTDIO is undefined for your target.  Note:  HAVESTDIO is defined by default in the template makefiles (TMFs) provided by Real-Time Workshop. If the listed conditions are met, the model build fails with a compilation error that includes the following text: undefined reference to 'rtPrintfNoOp'
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following files, for example, by adding .old to the end of each file name.matlabroot/rtw/c/src/cg_sfun.hmatlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonhdrlib.tlcDownload the attached files cg_sfun.h, rt_printf.c, and commonhdrlib.tlc and place them in the following directories.matlabroot/rtw/c/src/cg_sfun.hmatlabroot/rtw/c/src/rt_printf.cmatlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonhdrlib.tlcRestart MATLAB.

Summary: NEWNARXSP fails for networks with more than one hidden layer.
Description: NEWNARXSP generates an error if it is called to create a network with more than one hidden layer (i.e. more than two layers total).
Workaround: The work around is to update NEWNARXSP by changing this line of code:net.inputConnect(Nl,1) = 1;to this:net.inputConnect(1,Nl) = 1;

Summary: Calling a builtin that strips the imaginary (or real) parts of a complex scalar variable may incorrectly preserve the imaginary (or real) part of the result when combined with colon indexing.
Description: Calling a builtin that strips the imaginary (or real) parts of a complex variable may incorrectly preserve the imaginary (or real) part of the result when combined with colon indexing. For example,    function isolate     a = 8+ 2 *i;     b = 8+ 2 *i;     c = 8+ 2 *i;     d = 8+ 2 *i;     b(:,1) = real(a(:,1))     c(1,:) = real(a(1,:))     d(1,:,:) = real(a(1,:,:))  This bug only occurs when the variable is scalar and when both the right and left hand sides have the same type of colon indexing applied. ?
Workaround: To work around this bug, either turn the accelerator off using the command    feature eccel offor use a temporary result in the expression. For example,    function workaround     a = 8+ 2 *i;     b = 8+ 2 *i;     c = 8+ 2 *i;     d = 8+ 2 *i;     tmp = real(a(:,1));     b(:,1) = tmp     tmp = real(a(1,:));     c(1,:) = tmp;     tmp = real(a(1,:,:));     d(1,:,:) = tmp;

Summary: Error when using Target Language Compiler (TLC) file to support custom storage class
Description: Under rare circumstances, a Target Language Compiler (TLC) file written correctly to support a custom storage class might generate an error during code generation. The error message is similar to the following: Error: File: path \file-name .tlc Line: ?? Column: ??  Attempt to expand text buffer containing parameter values  ?

Summary: Why does a RESHAPE error occur when loading test results into the Test Results Viewer?
Description: When attempting to load test results into the Test Results Viewer, a RESHAPE error occurs preventing the results from being loaded. This bug only happens if a test is run with 2 test vectors that are grouped. The error will occur when clicking on the "Test Results Viewer" link in the Run Status pane in the SystemTest desktop, or by using the File -> Open menu in the Test Results Viewer. The error does not occur if you load the test results from the command line using STVIEWER , or use the "Visualize and plot saved results by launching the Test Results Viewer" option in the SystemTest desktop.
Workaround: To prevent this error, there are 2 options. The first option is to run the SystemTest test with one additional test vector. You can configure this test vector as grouped to avoid introducing more iterations.The other option is to open the TESTRESULTS file:edit(fullfile(matlabroot, 'toolbox', 'systemtest', 'systemtest', '+systest', 'testresults', 'testresults.m'))Then change line 124 from:value = [1 value(1)];to:value = [1 value(end)];

Summary: Dependency Report on Windows? platforms does not show called functions when run for a root directory
Description: When you run the Dependency Report on Windows? platforms from a root directory, for example, H:/, the report shows unknown  instead of the Children (called functions).
Workaround: To correct the problem, download and install the attached MATLAB file by following these steps:Determine the matlabroot directory by running the matlabroot function in the Command Window.Quit the MATLAB? program.Rename the following MATLAB filematlabroot\toolbox\matlab\codetools\deprpt.mto deprpt.old.Download the attached file, and unzipdeptrpt.m, to the directory specified in step 3.Start MATLAB.In the Command Window, runrehash toolboxcacheThis should resolve the problem.

Summary: mex -setup and mbuild -setup produce syntax error on Macintosh systems
Description: Running mex -setup  or mbuild -setup  on a Macintosh system in R2008a generates the following message: expr: syntax error/mathworks/AH/devel/bat/R2008ad/perfect/matlab/bin/util/mex/mexutil.sh: line 13: [: too many arguments
Workaround: This message does not affect the exectution of the setup. It can saftely be ignored.

Summary: Error message during code generation that refers to error in 'CGIR'
Description: When you try to generate code for a model that contains conditionally executed subsystems or Stateflow charts, you might encounter the following error message: Error building Real-Time Workshop target for block diagram 'mymodel'. MATLAB error message: Error using ==> rtwgen Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: .\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_tag.cpp line 938: getNodeRTWCGTag(startNode) == getNodeRTWCGTag(endNode) && getNodeRTWCGTag(startNode) == rtwTag
Workaround: To avoid this problem, clear the Include Comments check box in the Configuration Parameters > Real-Time Workshop > Comments pane.
Fixed In: R2007b web release, R2008a web release, R2008b

Summary: After a successful execution of a Simulink SaveAs to an earlier release, MATLAB crashes while opening a library using a presavefcn callback in the earlier release .
Description: This is a bug relating to how SaveAs deals with PreSaveFcn callbacks in libraries.
Workaround: To work around this issue, remove the PreSaveFcn callback, then save the model to an earlier version. Then, in the earlier version, add back in the presavefcn callback.

Summary: Error when opening older FIG-file containing a legend in MATLAB 7.4 (R2007a)
Description: In MATLAB 7.4 (R2007a), attempting to open a FIG-file created with a previous release of MATLAB and which contain a legend can generate a dialog box displaying the following error: Error using ==> open at 157. Unable to find function update_contextmenu_cbwithin MATLAB\matlab\scribe\@scribe\@legend\methods.m  In addition, the FIG-file will not open.
Workaround: To work around this issue, add the following line of code to the end of the MATLAB file matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/scribe/@scribe/@legend/methods.m, where matlabroot is the root directory of your MATLAB installation:function update_contextmenu_cb(varargin)

Summary: Check for Updates: the Installed Version reported might be incorrect for some products
Description: When you select Help > Check for Updates , the value for the Installed Version  might be incorrect. Specifically, in R2008a, for some products that use more than 2 positions for the version number, for example, 1.2.3 , the value might be incorrect. Examples of products having this problem include Curve Fitting Toolbox, Database Toolbox, Data Acquisition Toolbox, Embedded IDE Link MU, and others.
Workaround: To verify the correct installed version number, use the ver function.

Summary: Certain matrix multiplication patterns in Embedded MATLAB may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Embedded MATLAB functions may cause MATLAB to crash when they assign the result of a matrix multiplication to a structure field under the following conditions: The structure is an element of an array of structures.  ...and ... The structure field that receives the assignment is a matrix.  For example, the following code may cause this error:     ht = repmat( struct('T', zeros(4)), 1,6 );    for ii=1:6        ht(ii).T = eye(4) * eye(4);    end This bug may occur in: Embedded MATLAB Function blocks and Truth Table blocks in Simulink Embedded MATLAB functions in Stateflow MATLAB code that you compile with emlc  or emlmex
Workaround: Reshape the result of the matrix multiplication. For example, the following highlighted code corrects the error in the sample code shown above:ht = repmat( struct('T', zeros(4)), 1,6 );for ii=1:6    ht(ii).T = reshape(eye(4) * eye(4), 4, 4)end

Summary: Code generation error when Sum block signal types do not match Accumulator type
Description: When the inputs and output of a Sum block are homogeneous floating-point data types (all are single precision or all are double precision) and the Sum block's Accumulator data type does not match the type of the inputs and output, trying to generate code for the model causes an error. The error message names the block and states "Slope was not strictly positive".
Workaround: Set the Accumulator data type to be "Inherit: Inherit via internal rule".

Summary: Build of model containing Lookup Table or Lookup Table (2-D) block may fail with 'Cannot open include file' error
Description: Building a model that contains a Lookup Table or Lookup Table (2-D) block may fail with a compilation error if either of the following conditions are true: Files rt_look.c  and rt_look.h , which otherwise would be generated by the build, already exist and are on the MATLAB path, and double-precision data types are used with the corresponding lookup table blocks. Files rt_look32.c  and rt_look32.h , which otherwise would be generated by the build, already exist and are on the MATLAB path, and single-precision data types are used with the corresponding lookup table blocks.  The compilation error message may include the following text: rt_look1d.h(14) : fatal error C1083: Cannot open include file: 'rt_look.h': No such file or directory
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following file, for example, by adding .old to the end of the file name.matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/genrtlookup.tlcDownload the attached file genrtlookup.tlc and place it in the same directory as the file you renamed.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Calling improfile with an empty selection on an RGB image causes an error
Description: If you call improfile  on an RGB image and then press the Enter key, improfile  returns the following error: >> imshow('peppers.png');>> improfile??? Undefined function or variable "zr". Error in ==> improfile at 215 zg = cat(3,zr(:),zg(:),zb(:)); In this scenario, causing the improfile  function to operate on an empty selection produces the error.

Summary: The Fixed-point column in the Communications Block Data Type Support table is not up to date.
Description: In the documentation for the Communications Blockset, the Fixed-point column in the Communications Block Data Type Support table does not reflect all of the block changes for the 2008A release.
Workaround: As of the R2008A release, the following blocks support Fixed-point data-types:Integrate and Dump Gold Sequence Generator General QAM Demodulator Baseband OQPSK Demodulator Baseband Bipolar to Unipolar Converter Bit to Integer Converter Integer to Bit Converter Unipolar to Bipolar Converter

Summary: Why do I get an error when I specify the boundary conditions matrix as strings?
Description: When the boundary conditions matrix is entered as strings (as is done in the documentation example 2 of assemb) to any PDE toolbox solver it errors.
Workaround: Specify the boundary condition as a function to avoid this error. See the documentation of pdebound and pdegeom functions for examples.

Summary: Simulation results for multirate models may be incorrect for large simulation times
Description: An algorithm performs the 'sample hit calculation' to determine which model sample times have a hit for a given time. Presently, a bug exists in the algorithm for multirate models that are run for thousands or milloins of time steps; Simulink may set the sample hit to 'false' when it should be set to 'true'. As a result, the affected blocks may cease executing after a certain number of time steps, and thereby produce a wrong answer. This bug is most often encountered in models that use blocks from the Signal Processing and Communications Blocksets, because these blocks introduce multiple sample times that cover a range spanning many orders of magnitude.

Summary: Using a signal object with an Alias at the root I/O of a referenced model may cause a spurious error
Description: If a signal object used in a referenced model meets the following requirements, it may cause a spurious error: The signal object has non-Auto storage class. The signal object has a non-empty Alias. The signal object is on a signal that comes from a root inport, or feeds a root outport.  For example, if the referenced model has a signal object named foo  on a signal feeding a root outport, and the object foo  has an Alias of foo_Alias , then simulating or generating code for the parent model may cause an error similar to: "Can not resolve Simulink signal object 'foo_Alias' for output port 1 of 'top/Model'". The error occurs even though the model is actually correct.

Summary: Exception when running on Windows Vista? operating system, Enterprise or Business editions, with MATLAB? program in compatibility mode for Windows? XP software
Description: When running MATLAB on Microsoft? Windows Vista operating system, Enterprise or Business editions, with compatibility mode for Microsoft? Windows XP software, the Command Window might show the following exception java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException 3184 when file types from applications other than MATLAB are in the current directory. The Current Directory browser is unusable.
Workaround: Shutdown MATLAB and clear the option to run in compatibility mode for Windows XP software. Then restart MATLAB.

Summary: An invalid warning relating to inconsistent sample time is reported
Description: A warning similar to the following will be incorrectly reported if five conditions hold in a model: Warning: Inconsistent sample times. Sample time ([0, 1]) of signal driving input port 1 of 'DriverModel_With_Subsystem/Model' differs from the expected sample time ([0, 0]) at this input port. The conditions are: The model has a block that is marked as not permitting a continuous sample time (e.g., a constant block). The block referenced in (1) is placed inside a subsystem and connected to the subsystem output. The subsystem output of (2) is connected to a port-based sample time block (e.g., a model block). The port-based sample time block of (3) has a continuous sample time after sample time propagation; either the block specifies that its sample time is continuous or it inherits the sample time. The block in (1) has the sample time fixed-in-minor-step (i.e., [0,1]) after sample time propagation; either the block specifies that its sample time is fixed-in-minor-step or it inherits a sample time.  ?

Summary: Function findm errors on geolocated data grid input
Description: The function findm  should accept a geolocated data grid as input, but currently this type of input results in an error. A geolocated data grid consists of a numeric 2-D array, Z , along with the latitudes and longitudes of a set of geolocation points. The longitude and latitude data is also provided as 2-D arrays (matrices) having the same size as Z . Note that findm  works correctly on an abbreviated geolocated grid, in which the latitude input is a vector whose length matches the number of rows in Z  and the longitude input is a vector whose length matches the number of columns in Z . This special case is described in the MATLAB-file help, but not in the reference page. Conversely, the MATLAB-file help does not mention the much more typical case, in which the latitude and longitude inputs are 2-D and have the same size as Z  itself.
Workaround: To work around these issues, replace the findm.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/map/findm.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: findm.m.oldCopy the version of findm.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Sporadically when you try to export graphics using Export Setup, you get an error.
Description: Sporadically when trying to export graphics using Export Setup , you get the error: ??? Undefined function or method 'str2dobule'  for input arguments of type 'java.lang.String'. Error in ==> exportsetupdlg>lineminChanged at 1036 ui.style.LineWidthMin = str2dobule(getText(ui.linemin));  This is due to a typo in the code.
Workaround: Follow these steps as a workaround for this problem:1) Quit MATLAB.2) Rename the following file: $MATLAB\toolbox\matlab\graphics\exportsetupdlg.m to exportsetupdlg.old (where $MATLAB = the MATLAB root directory on your machine)3) Copy attached file to: exportsetupdlg.m to $MATLAB\toolbox\matlab\graphics\4) Restart MATLAB.5) After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:  rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Some MATLAB utilities do not work properly on old-style (carriage return only) Macintosh files.
Description: Certain files on Macintosh computer systems ("old-style" files) contain only carriage return characters, with no newline characters. These files are improperly handled in M-Lint and some other tools, including the profiler. However, they work correctly in other cases, including when running the programs and when using M-Lint in the MATLAB editor. The base symptom is that all the M-Lint line numbers are 1. Other programs may experience similar problems.
Workaround: The simplest workaround is to use a Macintosh editor or other utility to read the file and write it back in 'Unix' or 'Windows' mode. This should completely fix any problems.

Summary: Items selected in the Workspace Browser and Variable Editor deselect themselves within several seconds
Description: If you select variables in the Workspace Browser or in the Variable Editor, sometimes they deselect themselves almost immediately. This makes it impossible to copy the selection or plot it. At the same time, the CPU usage of the MATLAB process increases, leading to the instability.
Workaround: Save workspace variables to a MAT-file if necessary. Then, in the command window, type clear all to free memory, then reload your variables.

Summary: Setting subfields on data acquisition object properties does not produce any result
Description: When I set the value for a property for a specific channel it has no effect: >> ai = analoginput('nidaq','Dev1');>> addchannel(ai,0,'Channel0');>> set(ai.InputType.Channel0,'SingleEnded')>> ai.LoggingMode.Channel0ans =     []>> set(ai.LoggingMode.Channel0,'Memory')>> ai.LoggingMode.Channel0ans =     []>> delete(ai)  Only the Channel propery of analog input and analog output objects has subfields, and only the Lines property of digitalio objects has subfields. The code shown above should cause an error similar to the error caused by setting a subfield on a scalar variable: >> x = 1;>> x.a??? Attempt to reference field of non-structure array.
Workaround: Do not get or set subfields on object properties other than Channels and Lines.??

Summary: Columns in a uitable do not resize as expected
Description: I create a uitable and set its Units  property to 'normalized' . When I resize the figure, the table also resizes. However, the widths of its columns do not change as I would expect. For example, after creating a uitable  with the following code, t = uitable('Data',rand(3,4)); set(t,'ColumnName',{'One' 'Two' 'Three' 'A Longer One'}); set(t,'ColumnWidth',{40 60 60 'auto'}); set(t,'Units','normalized');  the table resizes when I make the figure larger or smaller, but the columns do not. Their widths always appear to be expresed as pixels.
Workaround: This is not a bug. No workaround exists because the Units property does not apply to the ColumnWidth property. The widths of uitable columns are always in pixels, and therefore are absolute.

Summary: Incorrect initialization of a Discrete-Time Integrator block without an external reset port when its IC port is driven by a Merge block.
Description: Consider a Discrete-Time Integrator  block at the top level of a model. At each time step during simulation, a Discrete-Time Integrator block with an external IC port reads the input signal value from the IC port and checks if it needs to initialize its state. However, if the Discrete-Time Integrator block does not have an external reset port, the integrator cannot be reset after initialization. In this case, the signal value from the IC port is useful only at the first time step, and reading it at every time step is wasteful. Therefore, when the Discrete-Time Integrator block detects that all blocks driving its IC port can compute their outputs before simulation, it reads the signal value from the IC port and initializes its state before simulation begins. This optimizes performance by not checking the value of the IC port at every time step. This optimization assumes that the signal value of the IC port before simulation is the same as the value at the first time step. However, this assumption is incorrect when a Merge  block is driving the IC port of the Discrete-Time Integrator block. The output value of the Merge block at the first time step may differ from the value initialized before the start of simulation. Assuming that the Merge block is used properly (its inputs are always driven by a conditionally executed subsystem) the output value of the Merge block is initialized as follows: If the Merge block has an IC value, the output of the Merge block is the IC value. If the Merge block does not have an IC value, the output of the Merge block is the IC value of the output of the conditionally executed subsystem that executes last.  In either case, the output value of the Merge block at the first time step may not be the same as the initial value. This occurs because a conditionally executed subsystem driving the Merge block may execute at the first time step and override the initial value.
Workaround: To prevent this Discrete-Time Integrator block optimization from occuring:Insert a Signal Conversion block between the output port of the Merge block and the IC port of the Discrete-Time Integrator block.Select Override optimizations and always copy signal in the Signal Conversion block.

Summary: In Simulink versions 6.6 (R2007a) through 7.1 (R2008a), using Save As to save a model that contains a Concatenate block as an R2006b or earlier model corrupts the model
Description: In Simulink versions 6.6 (R2007a) through 7.1 (R2008a), saving a model as an R2006b or earlier release might produce a corrupted model when the original model contains a Concatenate block with the following parameter values: Number of inputs:  not equal to 2 Mode:  Multidimensional array Concatenate dimension:  1 or 2  When the Concatenate block settings meet these conditions, using Save As to save the model disconnects some of the input signals to the Concatenate block.
Workaround: As a workaround, download and use the attached script save_system_with_concatenate_block_as.p.The help for the script is supplied.SAVE_SYSTEM_WITH_CONCATENATE_BLOCK_AS('SYS', 'NEWNAME', 'VERSION')  Save original model with the name SYS as an R2006b compatible model or an  earlier version with the name NEWNAME. This script fixes the problem with  the Concatenate block connections when saving an R2007a or later model as an  R2006b or earlier model. Please note that VERSION must be 'R2006b' or  earlier and that script does not perform such check. See help for  SAVE_SYSTEM on how to specify VERSION.

Summary: Pro/ENGINEER-to-SimMechancs translator fails to translate welds properly when components are constrained with the Allow assumptions option
Description: With the Pro/ENGINEER-to-SimMechanics translator, components fully constrained using the Allow assumptions  option are not translated with the correct Weld joints upon SimMechanics model import. In Pro/ENGINEER, there is quick way to define a fully constrained component by selecting the Allow assumptions  option during component definition. When Allow Assumptions  is selected during component assembly (which is the default), Pro/ENGINEER automatically makes the needed constraint orientation assumptions. For example, only one Align and one Mate constraint are required to fully constrain a bolt to a hole in a plate. After an Align constraint has been defined between the axes of the hole and the bolt, and a Mate Constraint has been defined between the bottom face of the bolt and the top face of the plate, Pro/ENGINEER assumes a third, invisible constraint. This constraint controls rotation about the axes, thereby fully constraining the component. This is accomplished with the Allow assumptions  option on the component. The Pro/Engineer-to-SimMechanics translator should weld those components which are fully constrained with the Allow assumptions  option, but it does not.
