Summary: Cell mode: Whitespace or comment after a line continuation, "...", causes cell to break in the wrong location
Description: When using the MATLAB? Editor in cell mode, if white space or a comment follows a line continuation, "...", then the cell containing that line ends immediately after that line, instead of at the expected location. In the following example, you expect the Editor to create a cell containing all the code within the for  loop. Instead, the Editor inserts cell breaks after the for  statement and after the line containing the imagesc  function: %% Scale Data and Display as Image   for i=1:3 %% imagesc ... % scale data  (magic(i))end   If you attempt to evaluate the cell with the misplaced cell break, a MATLAB Editor dialog box opens stating: "The selected cell cannot be evaluated because it contains an incomplete statement." However, assuming there are no other problems, if you attempt to run the entire file, it runs successfully.
Workaround: Remove the whitespace or comment after the line continuation, "...".

Summary: Changing a data type with the Lookup Table Editor may fail and can corrupt data
Description: The Lookup Table Editor contains pulldowns for setting or changing a lookup table's Row, Column, or Table data type. Using any of these pulldowns to change a data type in an existing lookup table may fail to make the desired change; generate a spurious error; or corrupt the data in the table. Any data corruption that occurs affects only the editor's copy of the data. Choose File > Reload Block Data  to discard the corrupted data and redisplay the original lookup table data in the editor. ?
Workaround: Do not use the Lookup Table Editor to change a lookup table's Row, Column, or Table data type. If the source data appears literally in the lookup table block dialog, edit the data to have a different type. If a workspace variable provides the source data, edit the variable to change the data type.?

Summary: GUIDE-generated callbacks cannot be altered or removed
Description: If you use the Property Inspector to type in a Callback for an object or click the Pencil icon to cause GUIDE to generate a callback automatically, you cannot edit or remove this callback. This has several consequences: If you inadvertently create a callback in the Property Inspector, you cannot remove it. You cannot edit the arguments of the callback GUIDE generated via the Property Inspector. If you remove such a callback's MATLAB code from the GUI's MATLAB file, the GUI will raise an error if the user triggers it.  ?
Workaround: To remove a GUIDE auto-generated callback safely for a component (for example, the CreateFcn of object1), do the following:1. In the GUI's MATLAB file, add the following code to the OpeningFcn.set(handles.object1,'CreateFcn','')2. Open the GUI in GUIDE and run it.3. Save the GUI in GUIDE (you may need to make a small change, then undo it, to alllow it to be saved).4. Remove from the OpeningFcn the set command you placed there.5. Remove code for the callback that GUIDE placed in the GUI's MATLAB file6. Save the MATLAB file.The callback for object1 is now deleted.

Summary: The Simulink Configuration Parameters dialog box and Simulink Library Browser sometimes use the CPU excessively or cause the system to hang
Description: When the Configuration Parameter dialog box's tree view or the Library Browser's tree/list view is not wide enough to display all of the tree, MATLAB may use your system's CPU excessively or hang your system.
Workaround: Make sure that the tree view in the Configuration Parameters dialog box or the tree/list view in the Library Browser is completely visible by either moving the horizontal scroll bar on the dialog box all the way to the left or resizing the dialog box so that the horizontal scroll bar disappears.

Summary: A Simulink model may fail to simulate if it contains a Model block with a testpointed bus signal
Description: A Simulink model that contains a Model block may fail to simulate under the following conditions: The model that contains the Model block was created using Simulink R2006b or later. The model executes in Simulink R2007a or earlier, or the Model block is configured for Accelerator mode. The referenced model contains a bus signal that contains a Mux signal as one of its inputs. The Mux signal is driven by one or more root Inport blocks. The bus signal is testpointed.  Under these conditions, Simulink may issue an error similar to the following during simulation: ### Updating model reference SIM target for model: referenced_modelError: File: C:\matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\capi.tlc Line: 348 Column: 73Undefined identifier RegionError: File: C:\matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\capi.tlc Line: 348 Column: 80The argument for an [] operation must be a repeated scope symbol, a vector, or a matrixError: File: C:\matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\capi.tlc Line: 348 Column: 84Arguments to ISFIELD must be recordsError: File: C:\matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\capi.tlc Line: 359 Column: 47Undefined identifier Region### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: 'referenced_model' aborted due to an error.
Workaround: Remove the testpoint from the bus signal. The model can then simulate without error.

Summary: parallel function's name-based syntax incorrectly resorts I/O names
Description: The parallel  function's name-based syntax, parallel(sys1,sys2,'name') , sorts the names of the connected channels in alphabetical order. This sorting produces unexpected results when sys1  and sys2  are MIMO systems with the same I/O names, and the I/O names are not in alphabetical order, as shown in the following example. sys1 = ss(diag([1 2]),'inputname',{'v','u'},'outputname',{'y','z'});sys2 = ss(diag([3 4]),'inputname',{'v','u'},'outputname',{'y','z'});parallel(sys1,sys2,'name')  d =   u v  y 0 4  z 6 0Static gain.  Note that the resulting input channels are u,v  instead of v,u . The expected result is d =   v u  y 4 0  z 0 6Static gain.
Workaround: Use the index-based syntax of parallel.

Summary: When opening GUIDE, the Browse button does not appear on the bottom right of the Open Existing GUI tab
Description: The GUIDE Quick Start dialog Open Existing GUI  tab contains a list of recently opened figure files and a Browse  button that allows the user to navigate to and open figure files not in the recent list. However, if there are no recently opened files, the Browse button does not appear on the bottom right of the tab.
Workaround: Open GUIDE withguide(0)This starts GUIDE without using the Quick Start dialog. Save the figure. (Doing this populates the "Recently Opened" list.)The next time you open GUIDE, the Recently Opened GUI tab will have an entry and the Browse button will be visible.

Summary: Certain Simulink blocks might throw segmentation violation when fed by discontiguous signals
Description: Simulink will throw a segmentation violation if the following conditions are met. 1. Discontiguous signals are fed into the Math Function, Gain, Prelookup, Interpolation using Prelookup, or Lookup Table n-D blocks. 2. Start simulation without first doing an Update Diagram.
Workaround: Update Diagram before starting a simulation.

Summary: Variable Editor displays arrays of objects incorrectly
Description: The Variable Editor displays an array of objects as if it was a single object. In addition, the property values it displays are incorrect; the values of the first listed property might be repeated for all properties of the object.
Workaround: Type the name of the variable in the Command Window and press Enter.

Summary: The Simulink.ModelDataLogs class whos() method displays incorrect Simulink class information for an object reloaded from a MAT file
Description: Prior to R2008b, a ModelDataLogs  object stored the class information for each of its fields in a manner that was not written out to the MAT file appropriately. As a result, after reloading the object from the MAT file, the whos()  method did not work correctly when passed the 'systems'  or 'all'  options. Specifically, it did not recurse into SubsysDataLogs  or ModelDataLogs  objects, and printed (null)  in the Simulink Class column. In the following example, logsout  is a ModelDataLogs  object. First, the correct whos()  behavior is shown. Next, logsout  is saved to and reloaded from a MAT file. Finally, the incorrect whos()  behavior is shown. Note that the Subsystem.d  entry is missing, and the classes are shown as (null) . >> logsout.whos('systems')Simulink.ModelDataLogs (m431472):  Name                   Elements  Simulink Class  c                         1      TsArray  Subsystem                 1      SubsysDataLogs  Subsystem.d               1      TsArray>> save tmp.mat logsout>> clear>> load tmp.mat>> logsout.whos('systems')Simulink.ModelDataLogs (m431472):  Name                   Elements  Simulink Class  c                         1      (null)  Subsystem                 1      (null)

Summary: Simulating a model that displays port values and uses the block reduction optimization can crash MATLAB
Description: Simulating a model that displays block port values and uses the block reduction optimization can crash MATLAB

Summary: Enabling the chart block options, "Treat as atomic unit" and "Minimize algebraic loop occurrences," causes a segv if the chart has an input data port and the input data port is not connected to other block.
Description: Enabling the chart block options, "Treat as atomic unit" and "Minimize algebraic loop occurrences," when the chart has an input port, causes MATLAB to terminate execution.
Workaround: Do not treat the chart block as an atomic unit.

Summary: Changes to a MATLAB file in the Embedded MATLAB Editor may be ignored during Simulink model compilation if the file does not contain the %#eml pragma
Description: If you edit an Embedded MATLAB Function block without saving your changes, the updates will be ignored when you build the model if the function code does not contain the #%eml  pragma. When you build the model for simulation or Real-Time Workshop code generation, the Simuink engine reads and compiles the old version of the function code from disk, ignoring the changes you made in the Embedded MATLAB Editor. ?
Workaround: Add the %#eml pragma to the code in the Embedded MATLAB Function block or save your changes before building the model.

Summary: Unexpected simulation results using Real-Time Workshop generated S-function with rising or falling edge trigger signal as input
Description: Simulation using a Real-Time Workshop generated S-function might not produce the same initial output as the original model or subsystem from which the S-function was generated if both of the following conditions are true: An input into the model or subsystem from which the S-function is generated is a rising or falling edge trigger signal driving a triggered subsystem. The S-function is generated using the Real-Time Workshop S-function target (rtwsfcn.tlc ) and then replaces its source model or subsystem for simulation.  The generated mdlStart()  code for the S-function fails to initialize the trigger signal value, potentially altering the initial simulation output.
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2008a, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the matlabroot command.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following file, for example, by adding .old to the end of the file name:matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonbodlib.tlcDownload the attached file and place it at the same location as the file you renamed.Regenerate the Real-Time Workshop S-function for your model.

Summary: Running a model that contains an S-Function Builder block with continuous states, in Accelerator mode, causes errors
Description: Running a model that contains an S-Function Builder block causes errors if the following is true: Model contains an S-Function Builder block with continuous states Model is run in Accelerator mode  Running a model with these conditions generates a number of error messages in the MATLAB Command Window, including the following string: Disagreement in number of macro arguments

Summary: Out of Memory Errors when Using Large Model Test Generation Optimization
Description: Using the large model strategy for test case generation creates out of memory errors of the form:  ??? Detected 1 error(s) in AvtUI.Progress.getDialogSchema: Out of memory. Type HELP MEMORY for your options.   In some situations, the problem may crash the MATLAB application or cause it to disappear.
Workaround: Replace this file in your R2008a installation toolbox\sldv\sldv\slavteng.mexw32with the attachment from below.Note: Be careful to apply this patch onlyto an R2008a installation.

Summary: Variable step solvers slow down or hanging for user specified logging time and ToWorkSpace block with discrete sample time
Description: For variable step solvers, if the user specifies logging time or if there is a ToWorkSpace block with a discrete sample time, then Simulation may take several very small time steps around the data logging time specified by the user, or by the ToWorkSpace block. This will cause a simulation to slow down. Furthermore, if the solver is ODE15s, then the simulation may hang.

Summary: PolySpace "PolySpace_Macros.xls" does not support results with more than 65,000 lines of code
Description: PolySpace software reports the following error in the log file: Generating GUI filesCAUTION!!! Exported file/var/PolySpace/analysisXX/PolySpace-Doc/MyVerification_Variable_View.txt exceeds 65535 lines.Statistics in PolySpace_Macros.xls will be erroneous.Done  ?
Workaround: To workaround this issue, you can download the attached file (PolySpace_Macro.zip), and unzip its contents into the folder <PolySpaceInstallProduct>\lib.On a Standard Windows installation <PolySpaceInstallProduct> is referenced by %POLYSPACE_C% environment variable.On a Standard Linuw/UNIX installation <PolySpaceInstallProduct> is referenced by $POLYSPACE_C environment variable.To complete the workaround:Create a backup copy of the existing file <PolySpaceInstallProduct>\lib\PolySpace_Macros.xls.Unzip and Install the new file PolySpace_Macro.xls.Relaunch the verification.Note: You must perform step 2 on both Client and Server systems.

Summary: Some symbolic definite integration commands involving trigonometric functions with poles and singularities crash MATLAB.
Description: Some definite integrals with complex integrands and with endpoints of integration that span singularities crash MATLAB.
Workaround: Two recommended workarounds may help avoid the crash:Split up the interval of integration to put the singularities at the endpoints of the interval or outside the integration region entirely.Replace the integration endpoints or parts of the integrand with numeric approximations. If possible, set the number of significant digits to 10.

Summary: Editing an imported filter in SPTool causes command-line errors.
Description: After editing an imported filter in SPTool (e.g., PZlp in the default SPTool setup), using FDATool to create multiple new filters generates MATLAB command-line error messages. The following error messages appear when you create the second filter in FDATool:  ??? Reference to non-existent field 'SPTIdentifier'. Error in ==> C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2008adevbash\toolbox\signal\sptoolgui\sptool.p>sptool at 762 componentNum = findcstr({ud.components.structName},... Error in ==> C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2008adevbash\toolbox\signal\sptoolgui\filtdes.p>filtdes at 563 sptool('import',struc,1); % This will make the new filter onto list Error in ==> C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2008adevbash\toolbox\signal\sptoolgui\sptool.p>sptool at 1300 feval(ud.components(i).verbs(j).owningClient,'action',... ??? Error while evaluating uicontrol Callback

Summary: The web function fails to open a document in the MATLAB Web browser when the path to the document contains a # character?
Description: Running web path #name  fails because path #name  contains a # character (an octothorp, also known as a pound sign). ?
Workaround: Do one of the following:Rename the folder to remove the # character. For example, rename path#name to pathname.Run web with the -browser option, which opens the file in your system browser. For example, run web path#name -browser.

Summary: Stateflow Search & Replace tool corrupts truth tables in a chart
Description: Using the Stateflow Search & Replace  tool to replace a string inside a truth table can corrupt text entries in the table.

Summary: Incorrect dimension propagation for Pad block
Description: The Pad block does not correctly set input port dimension via backward propagation if the "Output row/column/size mode" parameter is "User-specified" or "Next power of two".

Summary: Passing the wrong interface name to MAKEMID causes driver errors
Description: If you pass the wrong interfacename to MAKEMID, like IIVIPwrMeter instead of IIviPwrMeter it will not error immediately. However, when you later try to access properties, you will notice driver errors. Some IVI-COM objects boolean property values are returned as doubles, but other are returned as strings. This causes a Java error when you try to set the value of the boolean property for the object as a string, and the propinfo states the constraint values are 'on' or 'off'. >> set(r,'Enabled','on')Invalid property name or number of arguments.??? Java exception occurred:java.lang.ClassCastException: java.lang.Double cannot be cast to java.lang.String  at ICGroup7.getEnabled(ICGroup7.java)  ? ?
Workaround: ?Make sure that you pass the correct interface name including case sensitivity (IIVIPwrMeter vs IIviPwrMeter).

Summary: The TestBenchReferencePostFix property always retains its default value ( '_ref') regardless of the property setting.
Description: If you set the Test Bench property TestBenchReferencePostFix  to a non-default value, your setting is ignored. The TestBenchReferencePostFix  property always retains its default value ( '_ref') regardless of the property setting.

Summary: subimage displays scaled indexed images with non-gray colormaps incorrectly.
Description: When you call subimage  to display a scaled indexed image with a colormap that is not gray, the resulting image is displayed gray. When you execute the following example, note how the image displays with a jet colormap prior to the call to subimage , then appears gray after the call to subimage : a = imread('rice.png');subplot(1, 2, 1);out = imshow(a);colormap(jet);disp('Press any key to continue')pausesubplot(1, 2, 2);load clownsubimage(X, map)
Workaround: Replace existing file $MATLAB/toolbox/images/imuitools/subimage.m with attached version. ADDITIONALLY, add attached file scaledind2ind.m to $MATLAB/toolbox/images/imuitools/private/scaledind2ind.mTo work around this problem:At the MATLAB prompt, type the matlabroot command, and take note of the directory name returned. This directory will be referred to as ${MATLABROOT} in following steps.Quit MATLAB.Change directories into ${MATLABROOT}/toolbox/images/imuitools.Make a backup copy of the file subimage.m by copying it to subimage.m.old.Download the attached file, extract subimage.m, and store it in the current directory.Change directories into ${MATLABROOT}/toolbox/images/imuitools/private.Download the attached file, extract, scaledind2ind.m, and store it in the current directory.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Using == with 64-bit integers may return incorrect results.
Description: Using the == operator with 64-bit integers to compare a temporary scalar with an 8-element array may return incorrect results. The following code illustrates the bug. X=zeros(1,8,'int64');X==int64(1)   ans =  0 1 0 0 0 0 0 0
Workaround: Avoid using temporary scalars with 64-bit integers; use a variable to store the intermediate values. The following code will behave correctly.X=zeros(1,8,'int64');Y=int64(1);X==Yans =0 0 0 0 0 0 0 0

Summary: Cannot modify certain parameters (such as number of pole pairs) of machine blocks when a preset model is selected
Description: One cannot modify and apply changes to predetermined parameters of machine blocks (Synchronous Machine, Asynchronous machine, Permanent Magnet Synchronous Machine, DC Machine) when a preset model is selected.
Workaround: To start from a given preset model and then modify machine parameters, you have to do the following:1. Select the desired preset model to initialize the parameters.2. Change the Preset model parameter value to No. This will not change the machine parameters. By doing so, you just break the connection with the particular preset model.3. Modify the machine parameters as you wish, then click Apply.

Summary: Simulink Variable and Function table components do not report on fields of a Simulink.Parameter object.
Description: When a block has a Simulink.Parameter  object, the generated report does not include the fields 'Description', 'Min','Max' and 'DataType'.
Workaround: 1. Backup the following files:matlab/toolbox/simulink/components/@rptgen_sl/@csl_obj_fun_var/makeWordList.p matlab/toolbox/simulink/components/@rptgen_sl/@csl_obj_fun_var/makeVariableList.p2. Copy attached files to the above directory.3. Run >> rehash toolboxcache.4. Restart MATLAB.?

Summary: Multiple calls to impixelinfo take longer to execute with each call.
Description: Starting in R2007b, if you call the impixelinfo  function multiple times, it will take longer and longer to execute each time.

Summary: lyap function crashes MATLAB? when applied to sparse matrices
Description: The following syntax crashes MATLAB? when the arguments are sparse matrices:   X = lyap(A,B)
Workaround: Coerce the sparse matrices to full matrices before using the lyap function. For example, replaceX = lyap(A,B)withX = lyap(full(A),full(B))

Summary: Memory leak in MATLAB COM Automation client interface.
Description: We detected a memory leak in the MATLAB COM Automation client API. The memory leak occurs in the calls to COM Automation object functions which return a reference to a VARIANT  with the SAFEARRAY  data type as an [out]  parameter. For example, COM Automation server functions with the following IDL signature: HRESULT GetData([out] VARIANT* pData); This can affect the system performance when large amounts of data are passed to MATLAB from COM Automation server applications.

Summary: Model fails to update when it includes a referenced model with a single-element bus input or with an input from a Bus Selector with a virtual bus input
Description: A model fails to update if it has a Model Reference block with an input signal that either : Is a virtual bus with only one element, or Comes from a Bus Selector block that is fed by a virtual bus  The error message Simulink issues is similar to: Data type mismatch. Output port 1 of 'model/Bus Creator' is a signal of data type 'double'. However, it is driving a signal of data type 'bus'. This error message is indirectly related to a hidden SignalConversion block. This block is added for block 'model/Bus Creator' at input port 1 as result of block insertion or expansion. The hidden block's parameter 'Output' is set to 'Virtual bus'. Consider manually inserting such a block to debug the problem.

Summary: PolySpace stops during 'Level 2 Software Safety Analysis' with the message: 'FATAL ERROR in PLUS_CLOSURE'
Description: When launching PolySpace verification on C code, the analysis stops with the following message in the log file: - analyzing Axxx : #<other>  - 2.4s elapsed (wall clock).  - analyzing file.FunctionName : #<procedure>* error in structure_name, v=$11* compose  x1=#<twp4>  x2=#<twp4>FATAL ERROR in PLUS_CLOSURE, t1=#<twp4>exception Match(Match) raised.  raised at: /home/sylvestre/be.sml:40134.56  /home/sylvestre/be.sml:40170.55  /home/sylvestre/be.sml:40761.40Uncaught fatal error ...
Workaround: Relaunching the verification with the option -O0 will resolve this issue.

Summary: Calling the jordan function with a 4-by-4 or larger symbolic matrix and two outputs may error.
Description: Calling the jordan  function with a 4-by-4 or larger symbolic matrix and two outputs may error with the following error message: ??? Error using ==> sym.double at 27 DOUBLE cannot convert the input expression into a double array. If the input expression contains a symbolic variable, use the VPA function instead. Error in ==> jordan at 24 V = double(V);

Summary: Signal logging inconsistently eliminates duplicated regions
Description: A virtual signal  is a signal that graphically represents other signals or parts of other signals. Virtual signals are purely graphical entities; they have no functional or mathematical significance. The nonvirtual components of a virtual signal are called regions . For example, if a Mux block (which is a virtual block) inputs two nonvirtual signals, the block outputs a virtual signal that has two regions. When a virtual signal contains duplicate regions, signal logging excludes all but one of the duplicates in some contexts, but includes all of the duplicates in other contexts, giving inconsistent results. For example, if the same nonvirtual signal is connected to two input ports of a Mux block, that one signal is the source of two regions in the Mux block output. If that output is being logged in Normal mode simulation, the log object will contain data for only one of the regions, because the other was eliminated as a duplicate.
Workaround: When logging a signal that has duplicate regions, insert a Signal Conversion block. Since the Signal Conversion block copies the regions, each region will be unique.

Summary: MDD file for AgilentSAnSA driver cannot be parsed
Description: The MATLAB instrument driver file created by MAKEMID for the AgilentSAnSA driver contains certain special characters. Therefore, neither MIDEDIT nor ICDEVICE can parse this file.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, locate the special characters in the file and open the file in a text editor to manually remove these characters.Type the following at the MATLAB command prompt to find the location where parsing fails (AgilentSAnSA_mod.mdd is the file created using the MAKEMID command):>> xmlread('AgilentSAnSA_mod.mdd')The output begins with the following line:[Fatal Error] AgilentSAnSA_mod.mdd:7731:292: An invalid XML character (Unicode: 0x8) was found in the element content of the document.This indicates that the offending characters are located at line 7731 of the file. Now, open the .mdd file in a text editor and manually remove these characters.

Summary: Reaction forces measured from a Joint in a 2D machine differ depending on the plane in which the machine is placed
Description: For example, if you model a point mass sliding down an inclined plane using a Body attached to a Prismatic Joint, the reaction forces measured from the prismatic primitive differ depending on the whether the prismatic primitive axis lies in the XZ or YZ plane. The inclination of the plane from the Z axis is the same in both cases, and the gravity acts in the Z direction.
Workaround: To work around this bug, force the simulated machine dimensionality to be three, even though the mechanism is 2D.You can do this by setting the Machine dimensionality parameter in your machine's Machine Environment block to 3D Only. The simulation might run more slowly after you change this setting.

Summary: Check Input Resolution blocks that specify scalar resolution might produce incorrect results
Description: Suppose your model contains a Check Input Resolution block whose Resolution  parameter specifies a scalar value. In this case, the block should detect whether the input signal is a multiple of Resolution  within a 10e-3 tolerance. Instead, the block erroneously detects whether the input signal is equal to Resolution , unless the scalar value is an integer from 0 to 9. Consequently, the block might produce incorrect results.

Summary: The Load Flow tool does not take into account the friction factor parameter of the Synchronous Machine and Asynchronous Machine blocks
Description: The Powergui Load Flow tool in its calculations does not take into account the friction factor value specified for the Synchronous Machine and Asynchronous Machine blocks as part of its Inertia, friction factor, pole pairs  parameter. Because of this, the simulation set to start in steady state does not actually start in steady state.
Workaround: To work around this issue for the Synchronous Machine block, calculate and add the losses caused by the friction factor (for the set active power) to the calculated 'Pmec' value in the Constant block that feeds into the Pm input of the Synchronous Machine block. Once this is done, the simulation will start in steady state.The expression for calculating the losses is:Pfriction = Nominal power * friction factor

Summary: Linearizing at snapshot instants with discrete time signals may return incorrect result
Description: Linearizing a Simulink? model at specific snapshot times may return an incorrect result when the model contains discrete-time signals and the linearization occurs at an off-sample time instant. For example, consider a model that contains the following sample times: 0, 0.01 and 0.1. The linearization behavior for this model varies by release. In releases prior to R2007b, you could linearize at the offsample simulations times, t = 0.015. In R2007b, the linearization snapshots must occur at a point in time of the simulation when all of the discrete signals are updated. In the example, a linearization snapshot could occur at t = 0.1*T where T is an integer multiplier. In R2008a, there is no requirement that the linearization snapshots occur at integer multiples of the model sample times. However, each linearization snapshot may impact evolving the simulation.
Workaround: In R2008a replace the file [matlabroot]\toolbox\slcontrol\slctrlutil\opcondsnapshot.m as follows:Rename the file [matlabroot]\toolbox\slcontrol\slctrlutil\opcondsnapshot.m to opcondsnapshot.tmp.Download the attached file named opcondsnapshot.m.Save opcondsnapshot.m to [matlabroot]\toolbox\slcontrol\slctrlutil\.Applying this patch imposes the behavior described in R2007b and addresses the issue regarding the impact on the simulation.In both R2007b and R2008a, to ensure that a linearization is performed at a integer multiple of the model sample times:Insert the Trigger-Based Operating Point Snapshot block from the Simulink? Control Design? block library.For the block's Trigger type parameter, select the function-call option.Insert the Function-Call Generator block from the Simulink/Ports & Subsystems library and specify the interval for linearization.Linearize the model using the syntax SYS = linearize(MODELNAME,TFINAL,IO) where MODELNAME is the name of the Simulink model, TFINAL is the final time of the simulation, and IO are linearization I/O objects created using the commands LINIO or GETLINIO. The linearized models are returned in an LTI variable SYS, where SYS(:,:,1:end-1) contains the linearizations at each of the discrete times and SYS(:,:,end) contains the linearization near TFINAL.The final linearization is only near TFINAL since the snapshot at TFINAL is determined using a zero crossing during simulation.

Summary: Link for CCS software stops working after you install service release 7 (SR7) for Texas Instruments' CCS 3.3
Description: Installing the CCS 3.3 software update SR7 (upgrading CCS to version 3.3.67) causes some product features not to work properly. You get incorrect results when you try the following operations: Reading the address of a symbol in your project Reading from memory locations or registers on the processor  These incorrect results affect the operation of a number of features: PIL Execution profiling Stack profiling Function Call  The incorrect results also affect MathWorks products that require Link for CCS: Target for TI C2000 software Target for TI C6000 software
Workaround: Install service release 8 (SR8) for CCS software to upgrade your CCS 3.3 installation to version 3.3.77. SR8 is available from Texas Instruments.

Summary: Incorrect DRS range for floats when the keywords min or max are used
Description: PolySpace does not correctly interpret the 'min' and 'max' keywords from a DRS file when used on a float. For example, when a model input is of type float, and it is declared in the workspace with a ImportedExtern storage class (with a inf..inf range), the generated model consists of an "extern float" definition, and the DRS entry contains 'min/max' keywords. In this case, PolySpace incorrectly interprets the 'min/max' keywords (they are both replaced by 0). The variable is then interpreted as 0 (zero) during verification, not full range float as it should be.
Workaround: Replace min and max by their actual value in the DRS file

Summary: clear global from a function also clears persistent variables
Description: Calling "clear global" from within a function has the side effect of clearing persistent variables in all currently running functions. For example, running the following function will generate an error on line 5 that the reference to "x" is a reference to a cleared variable. function demopersistent xx=5;clear globalx

Summary: Function evaluation count is incorrect in fmincon interior-point when the user does not provide gradients.
Description: The function evaluation count, as indicated by the iterative display and by the output field output.funcCount, is incorrect (larger than it actually is) in the interior-point algorithm in fmincon, when the user does not provide gradients (the default).

Summary: From block dialog lists scoped tags that are that are not visible to the block
Description: The block dialog of the From  block lists scoped tags that are not visible to the block because of its position in the hierarchy (scoped tags should only be visible in block dialog within scope). If you select one of these tags, Simulink issues a warning during model update.

Summary: An error occurs when using xlsread to read an Excel file that contains an empty string in one cell that is followed by cells with text.
Description: The following error occurs: ??? Index exceeds matrix dimensions.

Summary: Simulink color picker does not save custom colors
Description: Use the Simulink color picker to define a custom color, for example, for the background of a block. If you then try to pick the custom color for another block, you find that the color has disappeared from the color picker (i.e., it was not saved). In addition, color picker selections do not persist across MATLAB sessions. For details, see Bug Report 801807 https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/search_results?search_executed=1&keyword=801807&release_filter=Exists+in&release=0&selected_products=simulink
Workaround: Download the file sl_customization.m (see attachment) to a directory on the MATLAB path.Execute the following command at the MATLAB prompt: sl_refresh_customizations. This adds commands to the Simulink model editor's Tools menu that allow you to copy one block's background color to another block.To use these commands to copy a custom background color from block A to block B:Select Background Color -> Custom from block A's context menu.Select block A.Select Tools-> Copy Block Background Color from the model editor's menu bar (or press Ctrl+J). This copies block A's background color into the variable copied_color in the model workspace.Select block B.Select Tools->-Paste Block Background Color from the model editor's menu bar (or press Ctrl+L). This copies the color stored in copied_color (if it exists) to the selected block.Note that the keyboard shortcuts may not work on all systems.

Summary: PolySpace scripts, such as polyspace-c, located in the bin directory are not working on Windows
Description: When trying to execute script commands like polyspace- c  under a Cygwin shell, you get an error message like the following: PolySpace: Product installation seems corrupted: internal files are missing. Please re-install the product In release R2008a and later, PolySpace no longer uses the Cygwin API. The PolySpace scripts inside %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\bin  directory are obsolete.
Workaround: An equivalent list of executables with the suffix .exe is available in the following directory:%POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\wbin.

Summary: Code generation errors out incorrectly when using non-addressable CSCs in Stateflow charts
Description: When non-addressable CSCs (Custom Storage Classes) are specified with Stateflow chart inputs or outputs, code generation may error out incorrectly even though there is no access to addresses of inputs or outputs in the chart action code. The following is an example of this error message: "... block attempts to take the address of its input signal 'speed' but that signal is not addressable because of its custom storage class. ..."

Summary: MATLAB may stop responding when you use uigetfile with filters containing dots in them
Description: If you execute commands in MATLAB such as: sCPTSfx = '.cpt.mat'; [sFn, sPth] = uigetfile( ... {['*',sCPTSfx];'*.*'}, 'Pick suite of file cpt data files', ... 'MultiSelect','on');  The dialog box opens and if you select any file causes MATLAB to stop responding.
Workaround: To work around this issue, add a trailing wild card to the filter:sCPTSfx = '.cpt.mat'; [sFn, sPth] = uigetfile( ... {['*',sCPTSfx,'*'];'*.*'}, 'Pick suite of file cpt data files', ... 'MultiSelect','on');

Summary: When you use Measurement Computing devices certain calls to stop are ignored
Description: When you use a TimerFcn callback with Measurement Computing devices, if the function takes the same amount of time, or more time than the length of the TimerPeriod, calls to stop will be ignored. ai = analoginput('mcc', '1');addchannel(ai, 0);  % Repeat until stop is called.ai.TriggerRepeat = Inf;  % Set the timer period to 1 second.timerPeriodValue = 1;set(ai,'TimerPeriod', timerPeriodValue);  % Set the TimerFcn to pause for the same amount of time as the TimerPeriod.set(ai, ' TimerFcn', 'pause(timerPeriodValue);disp(''TimerFcn executed.'');');  % Start the device. We will begin to see that the TimerFcn has been called.start(ai);  % Pause to let the function run twice, then stop the device.pause(2);disp('Will now attempt to stop the device.');stop(ai)% The following line does not execute.disp('Device has been stopped.');delete(ai)  Running the above code produces the following output. Notice that we never return from the call to "stop(ai)". TimerFcn executed.TimerFcn executed.Will now attempt to stop the device.TimerFcn executed.TimerFcn executed.TimerFcn executed.TimerFcn executed.TimerFcn executed.
Workaround: Make sure that the time to execute the TimerFcn is less than the timer period.Either increase the value of TimerPeriod or do less work in the TimerFcn.

Summary: Accessing bus objects through mask parameters can cause missing and spurious error messages
Description: This problem can occur when a Bus Creator, Inport, or Outport block specifies a Bus Object  using a symbol that resolves to a mask parameter, and that mask parameter resolves to a base workspace parameter whose value is a bus object. If you update the model successfully, change the base workspace parameter to a value that is not  a valid bus object, and update the model again, the update occurs exactly as it would if you had not changed the base workspace parameter. This behavior is incorrect: Simulink should generate an error. If you again update the model, Simulink reports the error correctly. However, you cannot thereafter correct the error.  All subsequent updates fail, even if you change the base workspace parameter value to again be a valid bus object.
Workaround: Do not specify the Bus Object parameter for a Bus Creator, Inport, or Outport block using a mask parameter.

Summary: Certain custom date formats for timeseries objects are not honored by the overloaded timeseries plot method
Description: When you specify certain time/date string formats for timeseries objects, such as 'mmmyy' , plotting the object does not display the specified date format. For example, the code ts = timeseries(randn(3,1),{'1-Jan-2003' ;'2-Jan-2003' ;'3-Jan-2003'});ts.TimeInfo.Format = 'mmmyy';plot(ts) generates an overloaded timeseries plot using the time/data format 'yyyy-mm-dd HH:MM:SS'  instead.
Workaround: To work around this issue, in MATLAB 7.5 replace the contents of the overloaded timeseries plot method in matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/timeseries/@timeseries/plot.m with the file, plot.m, archived in the attached zip file as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/timeseries/@timeseries/plot.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, plot.m.OLD.Download the attached file, plot.m, storing it the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolboxreset command at the MATLAB prompt.?

Summary: Calling bitreplicate function in Embedded MATLAB Function blocks may cause compilation error.
Description: Calling the bitreplicate  function in Embedded MATLAB Function blocks may cause a compilation error when the following conditions are true: The block has inputs or outputs of inherited type The block calls bitreplicate  with scalar input  This error produces the following message: Undefined function or variable 'v'.Internal Error: This error occurred inside a MathWorks function.Function 'bitreplicate.m' (#28.303.304), line 13, column 46:"v"
Workaround: Specify type explicitly for each input of the Embedded MATLAB Function block. orSpecify type, size, and complexity explicitly for each output of the Embedded MATLAB Function block.To change the properites of Embedded MATLAB Function block inputs or outputs:Double-click the block to open the Embedded MATLAB editor.Select Tools > Edit Data/Ports.Click on the input or output you want to change and edit the type, size, or complexity properties in the right pane.?

Summary: Simulink Extras library blocks with unspecified initial output values might generate incorrect results
Description: The following Simulink Extras library blocks initialize their masks incorrectly when the Initial output  parameter is empty: Discrete Transfer Fcn (with initial outputs) Discrete Zero-Pole (with initial outputs) State-Space (with initial outputs) Zero-Pole (with initial outputs)  In this case, the block produces incorrect results.
Workaround: To work around this issue, ensure that you specify a value for the Initial output parameter on the affected block's dialog box.

Summary: uitoggletools on a menu do not indicate a depressed state on Macintosh OS 10.5 machines
Description: When you create a uitoggletool on a menu and click on it, the button does not show a visible depressed state on Macintosh OS 10.5 machines. On other Macintosh machines, a border appears around the button to indicate that it has been pressed.
Workaround: No workarounds exist.

Summary: An error occurs after you change sample rate in Data Acquisition Toolbox softscope tool
Description: If you acquire data using softscope, and then change the sample rate, and then point your mouse over the area where the trace appeared, a java exception message will appear in the MATLAB Command Window similar to the following: Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: -1at java.util.Vector.elementAt(Unknown Source)at com.mathworks.toolbox.daq.scope.DataStore.getTimeAtSample(DataStore.java:202)at com.mathworks.toolbox.daq.scope.DataStore.getTimeAtSample(DataStore.java:239)at com.mathworks.toolbox.daq.scope.Trace.getHitLabel(Trace.java:601)at com.mathworks.toolbox.daq.scope.ScopeDisplay.setDataTip(ScopeDisplay.java:685)at com.mathworks.toolbox.daq.scope.ScopeDisplay.mouseMoved(ScopeDisplay.java:621)--- Additional messages truncated --- ?
Workaround: Since this error does not cause any serious issues, you can ignore the error message. If you acquire more data by clicking the trigger button again, the error will not occur.

Summary: A representation clause on an enumerated type is given after the type is used and frozen.?
Description: If a value of an enumerated type is used as a default value of a discriminant, and then a representation clause is given on the enumerated type, then the compilation phase stops with an internal error Assert_Failure . The following piece of code illustrates the problem: package Test is  type Et is (E1, E2, E3);  type Rec is record  Ce : Et;  end record;  -- the default value freezes the type Et and then  -- no representation clauses can be given anymore on type Et ...  type Recwdisc (D : Et := E1) is record  Cb : Boolean;  end record;  for Et use (E1 => 1,  E2 => 2,  E3 => 3);  procedure Main;  end Test;  The error given during the compilation phase looks like: ================================================= rte-gnat1 fatal error (Assert_Failure)== Detected at file.ada:15:4===============================================  N_Enumeration_Representation_Clause (Node_Id=1468) (source)  Sloc = 484 file.ada:15:4  Identifier = N_Identifier "et" (Node_Id=1467)  Array_Aggregate = N_Aggregate (Node_Id=1470)  compilation abandoned  ---------------------------------------------------------------------- ------ Verifier has detected compilation error(s) in the code. ------ Please correct them and launch the analysis again. ------ ----------------------------------------------------------------------  ?
Workaround: Move the representation clause to a location immediately after the declaration of the enumerated type on which the representation clause applies.?

Summary: Default SOS scaling options of SOS filter designs in FDESIGN are not applied.
Description: The default SOS scaling options of Second-Order Sections (SOS) IIR algorithm (e.g., Butterworth, Chebyshev, elliptic, etc...) designs in FDESIGN are ignored. For example: f = fdesign.lowpass;h1=design(f,'cheby1'); % Default design opts = fdopts.sosscaling; % Default SOS scaling options  h2=design(f,'cheby1','SOSScaleOpts',opts);norm(h1.sosMatrix-h2.sosMatrix) % Default SOS scaling options not applied to default design   ans =   4.2200   The filters h1 and h2 should be identical.

Summary: Different subplot heights for plot and plotyy graphs in figures with wide aspect ratios
Description: In figures having high width-to-height aspect ratios, subplots created with plot  and plotyy  can have different heights, as the following code illustrates: fig = figure;set(fig,'Units','Pixels','Position',[231 828 617 94]);ax1 = subplot(2,1,1);plot([1:10],[1:10],[1:10],[1:10:100]);ax2 = subplot(2,1,2);plotyy([1:10],[1:10],[1:10],[1:10:100]); ?
Workaround: Use either of two workarounds:Set the Units property of each subplot to 'Pixels'.Set the Position property of each subplot to the same value.

Summary: PolySpace software incorrectly reports a Red correctness check when using the operator not with constants on modular types.
Description: When using the operator not  with constants on a modular type, PolySpace Client/Server for Ada software incorrectly reports a red check. This problem is due to incorrect "constant folding" by PolySpace software on the operator for modular types. Example: type M is mod 2**5;X1 : constant M := 2#00001#;X3 : constant M := 2#01000#;A : constant M := not (X1 or X3); -- red COR: [value not in range of type "M"]
Workaround: The only workaround is to intrusively turn constants into variables in the code. You could achieve this workaround in the previous example as follows:type M is mod 2**5;X1 : M := 2#00001#;-- remove constant keyword to avoid foldingX3 : M := 2#01000#;-- remove constant keyword to avoid foldingA : constant M := not (X1 or X3);

Summary: Wavelet 1-D GUI Tool cannot analyze column vector loaded from the workspace
Description: In the Wavelet 1-D GUI tool, an error occurs if you load a column vector from the workspace and then change any analysis parameters in the GUI.
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the current dw1dmngr.m file in your $ROOT\toolbox\wavelet\wavelet directory with the file in the Attachments section below.

Summary: Year of graduation field is not visible in Student Version activation application when running on Mac OS X 10.5
Description: On Mac OS X 10.5 (Leopard) you may get an error about not entering your year of graduation. There is a drop down menu for selecting year of graduation, but it may not be visible on the dialog box.
Workaround: Resize the dialog box so that the year of graduation field becomes visible.

Summary: S-Function Builder generates an incorrect TLC file, if an S-Function Builder parameter is used to initialize a discrete or continuous state.?
Description: S-Function Builder generates an incorrect TLC file when an S-Function Builder parameter is used to initialize a discrete or continuous state. When a parameter is used to initialize the states, the S-Function Builder generates the correct code for simulation, but the TLC code is incorrect. Therefore, the following error message is returned when generating code with Real Time Workshop for a model that contains this S-function Builder block: The left-hand side of a . operator must be a valid scope identifier
Workaround: Please try the following workaround if you are running R2008a:cd to: $MATLAB/matlab/toolbox/simulink/simulink/private (Where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory)Rename the file sfunctionwizard.pl to sfunctionwizard.pl.bak $MATLAB/matlab/toolbox/simulink/simulink/privateDownload the attached file sfunctionwizard.zip into $MATLAB/matlab/toolbox/simulink/simulink/private and unzip sfunctionwizard.plRestart MATLAB and rebuild the S-Function with the S-Function Builder block

Extract the files from the .zip file. ????????????? ???????????????????? Windows Systems:?????????????????????????? ????????????????????????????????? Summary: PolySpace verification stops at the end of the function call generation phase with the message: 'Received signal 11'
Description: When launching PolySpace verification on C code, the verification stops with the following message in the log file: ...Generating init for variable: myvarGenerating call to function: fooReceived signal 11  ---------------------------------------------------------------------- ------ Verifier has encountered an internal error. ------ Please contact your technical support. ------ ----------------------------------------------------------------------...  This happens on the following code when using the main generator on anonymous global variables: struct { struct { int x; } a; }; int foo(void) { return a.x; }

Summary: Elementwise math operations on integer inputs may cause MATLAB to crash on multicore machines.
Description: Elementwise math operations on integer inputs, such as in the code shown below, may cause MATLAB to crash when run on multicore machines:    warning on MATLAB:intConvertNonIntVal;    nn= rand(1000);    for k = 1:1000        disp(k)        uint8(nn);   end On some architectures (e.g., 32-bit x86), this only happens if integer warnings are enabled. On all other platforms, it may happen regardless of the integer warning setting.
Workaround: You can either disable the JIT in the function the bug occurs in, or or set the number of computational threads to 1.To disable the JIT, use the following command in the function in which the bug occurs:    feature('scopedAccelEnablement','off') To set the number of computational threads, use either the MATLAB Preferences dialog box or the maxNumCompThreads function.

Summary: The end of the log file is missing for a remote verification
Description: When a verification is performed remotely on a dedicated Server, the end phase message is missing. The missing part follows: **************************************************************** Level <level number> Software Safety Analysis done*************************************************************Ending at: <date and time>User time for pass<level number>: <CPU time>User time for polyspace-c: <CPU time> ****** End of PolySpace Verifier analysis*** The log file is stored in the results folder, see <results_dir>/ALL/.log

Summary: Incorrect results are generated when the reset dates span more than one level of the tree.
Description: Incorrect results are generated when pricing a cap and the reset dates span more than one level of the tree.

Summary: Macintosh? OS X 10.5: slow scrolling in MATLAB? Editor and some other GUIs
Description: On Macintosh? OS X 10.5 platforms, scrolling in the MATLAB? Editor and some other GUIs is slower than it was on OS X 10.4. This is due to a different default Java? JVM? Graphics2D rendering engine used by OS X 10.5 (Sun2D), where OS X 10.4 used Quartz2D. The new engine results in better performance for some graphics operations such as figure drawing, but slower performance for some other operations such as scrolling.
Workaround: Follow these steps:1. Apply the latest available Java update. Scrolling performance is improved in Java for Mac OS X 10.5 Update 4.2. If scrolling performance in the Editor and other tools is still unacceptable, then check to see if you have a java.opts file in one of the following locations:For MATLAB R2007b and earlier, the Applications > MATLAB root folderFor MATLAB R2008a or later, the Applications > MATLAB root > bin > maci folderIn the directory from which you launch MATLAB, from an xterm or Terminal command line3. Depending on whether the java.opts file exists in one of the listed locations, do one of the following:If the file does not exist, download and unzip the attached java.opts file to one of the locations listed in step 2.If the file already exists, edit it using MATLAB, Xcode or Emacs, to add the following line, which instructs Java to use the Quartz version of Graphics2D when MATLAB starts:-Dapple.awt.graphics.UseQuartz=trueNote: you do not need to edit the attached file, as it already contains this line.4. Restart MATLAB.5. Confirm that the java.opts file is working, by typing the following in the MATLAB Command Window and ensuring it returns true:java.lang.System.getProperty('apple.awt.graphics.UseQuartz')If the command returns false, check that you downloaded the java.opts file to a location specified in step 2, or edited the existing file correctly, and then restart MATLAB.Note: Figure drawing might become slower as a result of this workaround. You might want to stop using the workaround while doing extensive figure drawing, as follows:Quit MATLAB.Temporarily rename the java.opts file to a new name, for example java.opts.off.Restart MATLAB.When you finish figure drawing, resume using the workaround by quitting MATLAB, renaming the workaround file back to java.opts, and then restart MATLAB.Note: Some users find that Quartz rendering causes odd shifting behavior with variable-width fonts. A workaround for this is to use a monospaced font, such as Monospaced, Andale Mono, or Courier New.

Summary: Workspace browser: variables with large UINT32 values appear incorrectly
Description: Variable with large UINT32 values, that is, larger than intmax('uint32')/2 , or 2147483648 , appear incorrectly as 2147483648  in the Workspace browser's Value  column.
Workaround: To see the correct value, type the name of the variable in the Command Window and press Enter.

Summary: When using the PolySpace Model Link SL product with Simulink, the message "Error evaluating 'OpenFcn' callback of SubSystem block" may appear when you attempt to run a PolySpace verification.
Description: When you attempt to run a PolySpace verification from within a Simulink model, the following error message is sometimes displayed: "Error evaluating 'Function' callback of SubSystem block (mask)'<model name>/PolySpace for RTW Embedded Coder'. Error using ==> get_param Invalid Simulink object specifier".
Workaround: This issue can be avoided by modifying the file C:\PolySpace\PolySpace_Common\PolySpaceModelLink\PolySpaceForEmbeddedCoder.mIn line 132, replace:currentCS = getActiveConfigSet(gcs);with:currentCS = getActiveConfigSet(bdroot);?

Summary: Macintosh? OS X 10.5: Java NullPointerException on MATLAB startup
Description: On Macintosh? OS X 10.5, the following Java errors may appear when starting MATLAB: Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.lang.NullPointerException at apple.awt.CGraphicsEnvironment.displayChanged(CGraphicsEnvironment.java:65) at apple.awt.CToolkit$4.run(CToolkit.java:1259) at java.awt.event.InvocationEvent.dispatch(InvocationEvent.java:209) at java.awt.EventQueue.dispatchEvent(EventQueue.java:461)  at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpOneEventForHierarchy(EventDispatchThread.java:269) at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEventsForHierarchy(EventDispatchThread.java:190) at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEvents(EventDispatchThread.java:184) at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEvents(EventDispatchThread.java:176) at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.run(EventDispatchThread.java:110) The error messages do not affect MATLAB's operation.

Summary: dicomread errors when reading file containing multiple overlays
Description: dicomread , when called with the 'Frames'  parameter, errors when reading a file containing more than one overlay in an OverlayData  attribute. When called without the 'Frames'  option, dicomread  returns incomplete data, returning just the first overlay in an OverlayData  attribute.

Summary: Mac OS X 10.5: text in some tools is misaligned and difficult to read
Description: On Mac OS X 10.5, the text in the text fields for some tools is misaligned and difficult to read. For example, this occurs in the Filter Design and Analysis Tool in the Filter Design and Analysis Toolbox.

Summary: Exporting a datatip to a variable hangs MATLAB when its name matches that of a workspace variable
Description: When attempting to export the value(s) of a datatip using Export Cursor Data to Workspace  from the context menu, MATLAB hangs if the name specified for the exported variable is the same as a variable in the base workspace.
Workaround: To work around this issue, make sure that any variable names chosen for data cursor export are unique. Do not export to the same variable name more than once.

Summary: dicomwrite errors when writing to directories with embedded periods
Description: When trying to write DICOM files in a directory whose name contains one or more periods, dicomwrite  returns the following error: >> filename = '/tmp/name.with.period/file.dcm';>> dicomwrite(X, filename)??? Error using ==> dicom_open_msg at 39Could not open "/tmp/name_01.with.period/file.dcm" for writingError in ==> dicom_open_msg at 39 error('Images:dicom_open_msg:fileOpen', ...Error in ==> dicomwrite>write_stream at 589file = dicom_open_msg(file, 'w');Error in ==> dicomwrite>write_message at 264 msg = write_stream(destination, data_stream);Error in ==> dicomwrite at 195 status = write_message(X, filename, map, metadata, options); ?
Workaround: To workaround this error, edit the dicomwrite.m file in your MATLAB installation. Before beginning, make a backup copy of the file.Open toolbox/images/medformats/dicomwrite.m and look for the following code near lines 610-611:% Look for an extension.idx = strfind(file_base, '.');Change the second line to find the last period (if there is one):idx = max(strfind(file_base, '.'));

Summary: Structure arrays cannot be passed to MATLAB from MEX-functions created using emlmex or emlc.
Description: Arrays of structures cannot be passed to MATLAB from MEX-functions created using emlmex  or emlc . This restriction applies to: Passing structure arrays as arguments to the MEX-function Returning structure arrays from the MEX-function Passing structure arrays to or from MATLAB using the eml.extrinsic  construct

Summary: Bitwise operator block supports input types with different base containers.
Description: The bitwise operator block was designed to support input types with same base container. Release R2007a mistakenly supports input types with different base containers.

Summary: When defining a custom target (mcpu) in the PolySpace launcher, the -logical-signed-right-shift option is selected even if you select arithmetical right shift.
Description: In the PolySpace Launcher, if you choose logical signed right shift for your custom target, the generated launching command will specify an arithmetical right shift. ? ?
Workaround: Since this is a problem with the Launcher, you can bypass the issue by changing the generated launching command, and setting the correct option.Under Windows, the launching command is: launchingCommand.bat.Under Unix, the launching command is: launchingCommand.shPlease note that in both cases, the script uses a redirection to a log file, so nothing appears in the console window by default.The redirection looks like the following:> C:\PolySpace_results\rte1.log 2>&1It can be removed to follow the verification progress.?

Summary: web function does not open page in Mozilla? browser, even though it is the default on Windows Vista? operating system
Description: When the Mozilla Foundation Mozilla? browser is set as the default browser on the Microsoft? Windows Vista? platform, the statement web www.mathworks.com -browser opens the Web page in Microsoft? Internet Explorer?. The page is expected to display in the Mozilla browser.

Summary: Documentation for VER and VERSION needs clarification
Description: The documentation and command line help for the VER and VERSION functions has not been clear on distinguishing between the "MathWorks product family" version number and the "MATLAB product" version number that are displayed by these functions.
Workaround: Users of MathWorks products need to be aware that the VER and VER MATLAB commands display two different numbers labelled as "MATLAB Version." The first of these, immediately following the first dashed line, is actually the MathWorks product family version number. The second of these, immediately following the second dashed line, is the MATLAB product version.   >> ver matlab   -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------   MATLAB Version 7.6.0.38073 (R2008a)   MATLAB License Number: unknown   Operating System: Microsoft Windows XP Version 5.1 (Build 2600: Service Pack 2)   Java VM Version: Java 1.6.0 with Sun Microsystems Inc. Java HotSpot(TM) Client VM mixed mode   -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------   MATLAB Version 7.6 (R2008a Prerelease)The VERSION function displays only the MathWorks product family version number   >> version   ans =   7.6.0.38073 (R2008a)

Summary: Error message "Unable to copy.../results-dir/C-ALL/dummy.c to /ALL/SRC" when a c file is included by another c file
Description: Reproduction: 1- Create two C files, File1.c and File2.c File1.c is:#include "file2.C" File2.c is:int foo(void){ return 1;} 2- Add these 2 files to the project. 3- You get the message because File2.c is included twice. The first time because it is included by File1.c and then because it is in the list of the source files in the project. ?
Workaround: Remove the included file from the list of source files in the project. It is useless.?

Summary: Error mentioning "singularity / Inf / NaN in the solution" when running model in Accelerator mode
Description: An error might occur when running a model in Accelerator mode. The error mentions "singularity / Inf / NaN in the solution".

Summary: Brackets appear incorrectly in the title of an ezplot
Description: Execute the following expression in ezplot : ezplot('1/(x^2/2+2^(1/(x-2)))') The plot is produced properly but notice that in the title, the parentheses in the expression (x-2)  are misaligned.
Workaround: A?matching parentheses bug exists within ezplot that?affects the displayed title.? The workaround is to modify the title string after the figure is constructed.? For example,ezplot('1/(x^2/2+2^(1/(x-2)))') h = get(gca,'title'); str = get(h,'String') str = '{1}/({x}^{2}/{2}+{2}^{{1}/({x}-{2})})'; set(h,'String',str)

Summary: N-Server Block does not maintain sequence for pending entities
Description: When the N-Server block stores multiple entities beyond their service completion, they might depart in a sequence that does not account for the sequence in which they completed their service.

Summary: Simulink MinMax block may fail to ignore NaN input values when calculating output values
Description: The MinMax block should ignore any input value that is NaN, except where every input value is NaN. Where all input values are NaN, the output should be NaN also, either as a scalar or as the value of each output vector element for which all corresponding input elements were NaN. However, when the MinMax block inputs a NaN value and compares it with one or more numeric values, the block may output NaN rather than the correct numeric value. The error depends on the positions of the NaN value(s) in the input data. Such an error can occur in simulation, generated code, or both, and the effect of the error can be different in simulation than in generated code.
Workaround: Eliminate any NaN values from data input to the MinMax block. To flag NaN values output by any block during simulation, including the MinMax block and any block that inputs to it, enable:Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Data Validity > Inf or NaN block output

Summary: Simscape and its vertical products (SimDriveline, SimMechanics, SimHydraulics, and SimElectronics) do not comply with the Simulink default font preferences
Description: When you change the Simulink Font Defaults in the Simulink Preferences pane and then add a block from Simscape or its vertical products to a model, the block ignores these preferences.
Workaround: Follow these steps to change Simscape library font to default Simulink block font:1. Download the attached file change_Simscape_lib_font.p to a local directory, for example, C:\work.2. Start MATLAB and go to the directory where you downloaded the file.3. At the MATLAB Command prompt, type:  change_Simscape_lib_fontAfter this change, new models created with blocks from Simscape and its vertical products will comply with the Simulink default font preferences.

Summary: Using the FIFO Write block in a model might overwrite data
Description: Using the FIFO Write block in a model might overwrite data. The FIFO Write block data present  output is a boolean signal that is a single byte wide. However, this block writes this data as a 4 byte integer. Whether or not this is a fatal error depends on how the Real-Time Workshop software has placed data in memory.
Workaround: If you have xPC Target version 2.9 (R2006a) or later, you can use the following procedure to work around this issue:Stop MATLAB.Download the attached file fifowrite.c and save it to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks.Restart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.

Summary: MATLAB can terminate unexpectedly when a windowButtonDownFcn callback deletes the figure.
Description: In MATLAB, if you execute the following functions, a segmentation violation occurs. function surftooln_Startup();end function n_Startup disp(['Closing']);delete(gcbf);f = figure('menubar', 'none', ...    'Position', [20 20 500 300], ...    'ButtonDownFcn', @(s, e)n_Startup());end Stack Trace: [0] hg.dll:_figure_WindowButtonDownFcn(0x0d4e7400, 0x0307caa8, 0x0d4e7400, 0x498d3309) + 445 bytes [1]
Workaround: There are no workarounds.

Summary: Simulation using the Accelerator mode is slower in SimPowerSystems 4.6 (R2008a) than in previous releases
Description: You can notice a slowdown in performance if you compare simulation speed of SimPowerSystems models running in R2008a and using the Accelerator mode, compared to the previous releases. This issue is oberved if you have the lcc  compiler installed as the default compiler for building external interface (mex ).

Summary: Exporting Data Cursor data to workspace freezes MATLAB if variable names clash
Description: When exporting Data Cursor data to the base workspace, if you assign it a variable name which is the same as that of a current variable, MATLAB becomes unresponsive. ?

Summary: ASAP2 file generation can error out or create duplicate entries in ASAP2 file when sharing input values or table data between multiple 1-D and/or 2-D Lookup Table blocks
Description: ASAP2 file generation can error out when 1-D and 2-D Lookup Table blocks are configured to share the same input values -- that is, breakpoints, rows, or axis points. The error message resembles the following: Conflicting ParameterGroup references to parameterValue . It is used in both Lookup2D.Member[0] and Lookup1D.Member[0]. Additionally, when lookup tables share the same input values or table data, such that the same Simulink.Parameter  data object is used as input values or table data for multiple Lookup Table blocks, duplicate CHARACTERISTIC  or AXIS_PTS  records are created in the ASAP2 file.
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2006a, R2006b, R2007a, R2007b or R2008a, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the matlabroot command.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following files, for example, by adding .old to the end of each file name.matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/asap2lib.tlcmatlabroot/toolbox/rtw/targets/asap2/asap2/user/templates/asap2lookup1d.tlcmatlabroot/toolbox/rtw/targets/asap2/asap2/user/templates/asap2lookup2d.tlcDownload the attached files and place them at the same locations as the corresponding files that you renamed. Refer to the paths listed above.Regenerate code for your model.

Summary: The documentation contains contains incorrect and incomplete descriptions of the rounding methods for the RoundMode parameter
Description: The documentation contains contains incorrect and incomplete descriptions of the rounding methods associated with the RoundMode property. The methods are described in many places and inconsistently. In some  cases: the definitions for ceil  and convergent  were swapped. the definitions of round  and nearest  were omitted or swapped. the definition of convergent  was incorrect. two names, fix  and zero , are used for the same method  A customer reading these errors might select the wrong rounding method, introducing unexpected rounding behavior and bias. ?
Workaround: Review the following method descriptions and apply the correct one:ceil - Round toward positive infinity.convergent - Round to the closest representable integer. Ties round to the nearest even stored integer. This is the least biased of the methods available in this software.fix/zero - Round toward zero.floor - Round toward negative infinity.nearest - Round toward nearest. Ties round toward positive infinity.round - Round toward nearest. Ties round toward negative infinity for negative numbers, and toward positive infinity for positive numbers.

Summary: Conditionally executed Subsystem block initialization may differ depending on?if its?output is connected to an S-Function block or the IC port of an Integrator block.
Description: A conditionally executed Subsystem block may be initialized differently if its output is connected to an S-Function block or to the IC port of an Integrator block. Specifically, consider a conditionally executed Subsystem block with the following conditions: The subsystem is configured such that it does not execute at the first time step. A Constant or Initial Condition block with parameter value IC  directly drives the subsystem Outport block. The Outport block has unspecified Initial output  value ([] ).  With the above conditions, the output of the subsystem at the first time step will be one of the following: IC , if the subsystem output is connected to an S-Function or the IC port of an Integrator block. Zero, otherwise.
Workaround: You can workaround this issue by doing one of the following:For the Initial output parameter of the Outport block (in the subsystem) driven directly by the Constant or Initial Condition block, specify an explicit value. Doing so will make both subsystems have the same specified initial value at the first time step.At the output port of the conditionally executed Subsystem or at the input or IC port of the S-Function or Integrator block, insert a Signal Conversion block. ?Be sure to select the Override optimizations and always copy signal check box for the Signal Conversion block. This will prevent the parameter value IC from being seen as the initial value of the subsystem's output.

Summary: Uninstalling CCS V3.3 renders existing CCS V3.1 unusable with Link for CCS
Description: On a machine where CCS V3.3 and CCS V3.1 are installed, uninstalling V3.3 makes V3.1 unusable from MATLAB.This is because the CCS V3.3 uninstaller leaves stale registry entries in the Windows Registry that prevent MATLAB from connecting to CCS V3.1.Texas Instruments has documented this uninstall problem and the solution on their Web site. Search the TI Digital Signal Processing KnowledgeBase with the keyword Typelib .In the search results, look for the article?"How come the RTA plug-ins in CCStudio  no longer update when the target is running and only when I halt the target?".
Workaround: To resolve this problem, apply the workaround described in the following document that you can find in the DSP KnowledgeBase on the Texas Instruments Web site:"How come the RTA plug-ins in CCStudio no longer update when the target is running and only when I halt the target?"?

Summary: Saving the 'sfroot' object to a MAT file crashes MATLAB
Description: The MATLAB save  command causes MATLAB to crash if the MATLAB workspace contains a variable that references the the Stateflow root object. For example, executing the following commands causes MATLAB to crash: >> x = sfroot; >> save myfile.mat   % Crashes MATLAB
Workaround: Before saving to a MAT file, use the 'clear' command to get rid of any variables representing the Stateflow root object. >> x = 5; >> y = sfroot; >> z = ones(4); >> clear y; >> save myfile.mat; % Ok because 'y' was cleared from the workspace

Summary: Filter wizard/realizemdl fixed-point models may not produce bit-true results when CastBeforeSum is true.
Description: Fixed-point filter models generated by the realizemdl  function may not always produce results that are bit-true with the MATLAB simulation when the CastBeforeSum  property is true, especially when the accumulator format of the filter is more stringent than the product format. This is due to a new implementation of Simulink's sum block starting in R2007b that no longer casts its operands before the summation. The following filter structures are potentially affected: dfilt.df1dfilt.df1tdfilt.df2dfilt.df2tdfilt.df1sosdfilt.df1tsosdfilt.df2sosdfilt.df2tsosdfilt.latticemamaxdfilt.latticemamindfilt.latticeardfilt.latticeallpassdfilt.latticearmamfilt.cicdecimmfilt.cicinterp  You can also generate these models by going to FDATool's RealizeModel panel and checking "Build model using basic elements".

Summary: Block property dialog boxes can open either on the wrong display or entirely offscreen on Mac OS X machines with multiple displays
Description: Opening a block property or mask dialog box on a Macintosh OS X system with multiple displays can result in the dialog box appearing either on the wrong display or offscreen. This happens only when the Macintosh menu bar is not positioned on the leftmost display.
Workaround: Open the Macintosh's System Preferences application.Click on the "Displays" icon.Click on the "Arrangement" tab.In the arrangement view, drag the menu bar so that it is on the leftmost display. If your displays are oriented vertically (i.e. on top of one another), drag the menu bar to the topmost display.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Ascii Decode block does not properly handle comma after %c descriptor
Description: The Ascii Decode block does not properly handle a comma after the %c  descriptor. For example, the block might not be able to handle a format string such as '%f,%c,%f\r' The %c  descriptor causes the block to incorrectly handle the comma that follows it.
Workaround: As a preferred workaroundUpgrade your xPC Target release  to xPC Target 3.4 (R2008a) and replace the Ascii Decode block with the V2 Ascii Decode block, which is new in this release.If you cannot upgrade your xPC Target version, use one of the following workarounds.Remove the comma following the %c descriptor.Replace the comma after %c with a space.Note, the older Ascii Decode block has been retained to preserve the behavior of old models. To prevent the error or if you do not want to perform the workaround, you must upgrade to xPC Target version 3.4 to use the new V2 Ascii Decode block.
Products: xPC Target Embedded Option, Simulink Real-Time

Summary: Using a finish.m file with a quit cancel statement does not work correctly on Microsoft? Windows? platforms.
Description: On Windows platforms, if you use a finish.m  file that contains a quit cancel  statement (to confirm quitting), the MATLAB? application always terminates, even if quit cancel  is called. For example, if you instruct MATLAB to display this message upon exiting Do you really want to quit (Y/N)? and you enter Y , MATLAB terminates, but if you enter N , MATLAB should continue running. The following code accomplishes this: reallyQuit = input('Do you really want to quit (Y/N)?', 's');switch lower(reallyQuit)  case 'n'  disp('Quit cancelled')  quit cancel  case 'y'  disp('Exiting MATLAB')end The problem is that MATLAB always terminates, even when you enter N , meaning even when quit cancel  is called.
Workaround: As a workaround to confirming quitting with a finish.m file, instead use a preference to confirm quitting. Select File > Preferences > General > Confirmation Dialogs. Select the check box Confirm before exiting MATLAB. Click the OK button.When you exit MATLAB, a dialog box appears, asking if you really want to quit. If you click Yes, MATLAB terminates, but if you click No, MATLAB continues running.

Summary: Model compilation may?be slow?in a model that has numerous Goto/From blocks that carry bus signals
Description: If a model contains a large number of Goto/From blocks that have bus signals passing through them, the model may compile slowly. In addition, the execution of Bus Creator and Bus Selector blocks?that operate on those bus signals will be slow. The slowdown is exacerbated when Goto blocks have global scope.
Workaround: Replace Goto/From block pairs with wired connections between the source and destination.

Extract the files from the .zip file. ????????????? ???????????????????? Windows Systems:?????????????????????????? ????????????????????????????????? Open the .zip file.????????????????????????????????? Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.?????????????????????????? ???????????????????? ???????????????????? Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): ?????????????????????????? cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>???????????????????? ????????????? ?????? ?????? Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: hdf5write does not release file resources in case of an error.
